spec.title: Engaging Motion Graphics for Schugle
spec.grounding_note:
REAL (verbatim from the Freelancer.com brief in db_all.json): Schugle is a German AI web product for teachers that helps create/formulate/structure/revise Foerderplaene (individual support plans) while protecting sensitive pupil data via anonymised placeholders (real pupil names never go to the AI). The client wants THREE related promotional/explanatory videos with one coherent look: (1) a 10-second clip also usable as a SILENT website hero/background loop that works without voiceover, (2) a 30-second promotional/social/reel version, (3) a 2-minute explainer that may reuse material from the 10s and 30s. German on-screen text is REQUIRED; the client may supply/change German voiceover later, so German text must stay EDITABLE (text layers, not flattened). The narrative direction is given verbatim: "Messy notes -> structured thoughts -> protected placeholders -> AI-assisted formulation -> clear Foerderplan," plus the Schugle tilted "g" icon as a recurring motif and tone that is calm/credible/professional/modern — explicitly NOT generic education stock (no happy teachers, smiling children, chalkboards, graduation caps, cartoon kids). The client also asks for: clean no-text versions, separate audio tracks for music/SFX/VO placeholders, subtitle SRT/VTT if subtitles are used, and a full list of fonts/stock/icons/templates/music WITH license terms. Budget up to $1,500 USD; tools the client expects include After Effects/Premiere Pro/Canva/Figma. The reference tone sites foerder-fokus.de and schugle.de/einstieg are named in the brief.
ENRICHED (added only where the brief implies it, to make it a runnable connector benchmark, NOT to change the deliverable): (a) The exact narrative-beat extraction is operationalised by running media_summarize over a supplied reference tutorial clip (the brief explicitly points to existing schugle.de/einstieg tutorial videos as reference) so the storyboard is GROUNDED in the real product flow rather than invented. (b) The Express template-track (search_design -> fill_text -> animate_design) is chosen as the in-connector motion path because the capability sheet routes motion-graphics output through it; the brief's "templates, AI tools, stock assets, custom animation, or a mix" line explicitly permits templates. (c) Specific aspect ratios 16:9 / 9:16 / 1:1 are enriched from the brief's stated channels (website hero = 16:9, LinkedIn/social/reel = 9:16 and 1:1) — the brief names the channels, the exact ratios are the standard mapping. (d) A licensed Stock music bed is enriched from the brief's explicit requirement to deliver "separate audio files/tracks for music" WITH license terms. (e) The exact German on-screen copy strings and the Foerderplan reference data row are enriched to concrete sample text, faithful to the brief's described content (Foerderplaene, anonymisation, less paperwork). No deliverable is invented beyond the three videos + editable text + clean versions + audio + license list the client asked for.
spec.one_line_ask: Deliver a coherent German motion-graphics campaign for the ed-tech product Schugle — a 10s silent website-hero loop, a 30s promo/reel, and a 2-minute explainer — built from a branded Express motion template with editable German on-screen text, reframed to 16:9 / 9:16 / 1:1, scored with a licensed Stock music bed, and accompanied by clean no-text versions, an SRT caption file, and a font/stock/template/music license manifest.
spec.full_brief:
Schugle (pronounced "shoe" + the "gle" from "Google") is a German web product that helps teachers create, formulate, structure and revise Foerderplaene (individual pupil support plans) with AI — while protecting sensitive pupil data by replacing real names with anonymised placeholders before anything reaches the AI. The current website is factual and dry; the client wants a coherent visual campaign that makes the idea attractive, memorable and emotionally clear while staying calm, credible, professional and suitable for schools. The mandated narrative arc is "Messy notes -> structured thoughts -> protected placeholders -> AI-assisted formulation -> clear Foerderplan," with the tilted Schugle "g" icon as a recurring motif. The client explicitly forbids generic education clichés (happy teachers, smiling children, chalkboards, graduation caps, cartoon kids, generic "school app" imagery).

Produce THREE related videos that read as one campaign: (1) a 10-second clip that also works as a SILENT website hero/background loop (must read with no voiceover and be visually calm), (2) a 30-second promotional/social/reel version, and (3) a 2-minute explainer that reuses material from the first two. German on-screen text is required and MUST remain editable — the client may swap German copy or add German voiceover later, so text must live as editable layers, never flattened into the only final file. Required German on-screen strings (faithful to the product): "Verstreute Notizen" (scattered notes), "Struktur statt Stapel" (structure instead of piles), "Echte Namen bleiben geschützt" (real names stay protected) with an anonymised placeholder like "[Schüler:in A]", "KI-gestützte Formulierung" (AI-assisted formulation), and "Klarer Förderplan. Weniger Schreibarbeit." (clear support plan, less paperwork).

Constraints and deliverables: master the campaign at 1080p, 30fps, H.264 MP4. Deliver each of the three videos in three aspect ratios — 16:9 (1920×1080, website hero), 9:16 (1080×1920, reel/Stories) and 1:1 (1080×1080, feed) — preserving the legible position of the German text in every reframe. Provide a clean NO-TEXT version of the 10s hero loop (silent, for the website background). Score the 30s and 2-min with a single licensed Stock music bed (calm, modern, non-corporate-cheesy) at -16 LUFS, delivered ALSO as a separate WAV track; the 10s hero stays silent. If captions are shown, supply a German SRT. Finally deliver a license manifest (CSV/MD) listing every font, Stock asset, template and music track used, with license terms and the Adobe Stock license IDs. The visual style must echo the calm, trustworthy German-school tone of the named reference (foerder-fokus.de) without copying it, and must be grounded in how the real product actually flows (see the supplied reference tutorial clip).

inputs.name: schugle_g_icon.svg
inputs.gen_prompt: A minimal, modern logo mark: a single lowercase letter 'g' tilted ~12 degrees to the right, geometric sans-serif, rounded terminals, solid deep-teal (#0E7C86) on transparent background. Clean vector-ready line art, no gradient, no drop shadow, no extra text, centered with even margin. Flat 2D, crisp edges suitable for tracing to SVG. Single colour, high contrast.
inputs.realism_notes: Brand mark, not a photo — must be flat, single-colour, perfectly clean so image_vectorize produces a tidy SVG. No photoreal texture. Tilted 'g' is the brief's named visual motif.

inputs.name: schugle_wordmark.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A horizontal logotype reading 'Schugle' in a calm modern geometric sans-serif, lowercase except the capital S, deep-teal (#0E7C86) on transparent background, the lowercase 'g' tilted ~12 degrees to echo the icon. Tight even kerning, no tagline, no shadow, flat single colour, high resolution, sharp anti-aliased edges. Centered with generous transparent margin.
inputs.realism_notes: Text-bearing brand asset — gemini-3-pro-image (Nano-Banana-Pro) handles crisp legible logotype better than a photoreal model. Transparent PNG so it composites cleanly over template scenes.

inputs.name: schugle_reference_tutorial.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: A 45-second calm screen-recording-style explainer of a German ed-tech web app for teachers, 1080p, native German voiceover (clear neutral female voice, unhurried). Shows: a teacher's cluttered handwritten notes on a desk, then a clean web interface where scattered notes are organised into a structured support-plan (Förderplan) form, a step where a real pupil name field is replaced by a protected placeholder badge reading '[Schüler:in A]', then an AI-assist panel suggesting a polished German sentence, ending on a tidy finished plan. Muted professional palette (teal, warm grey, off-white), soft even lighting, real UI typography. Documentary product-demo feel, NOT flashy sci-fi, no cartoon elements, no stock-footage teachers smiling at camera.
inputs.realism_notes: This is the brief's referenced 'existing basic tutorial' the agent must SUMMARISE to lift narrative beats. Use veo-3.1-generate-preview at 1080p with native audio (NOT veo-fast/720p) so media_summarize has real speech + scenes to transcribe. German VO is essential because the beats must map to the German product flow.

inputs.name: foerderplan_sample_data.json
inputs.gen_prompt: Produce a small JSON object of German on-screen copy for a Schugle motion-graphics campaign, with keys: scene1_messy='Verstreute Notizen', scene2_structure='Struktur statt Stapel', scene3_privacy='Echte Namen bleiben geschützt', placeholder='[Schüler:in A]', scene4_ai='KI-gestützte Formulierung', scene5_payoff='Klarer Förderplan. Weniger Schreibarbeit.', cta='Schugle — Förderpläne, klar und geschützt.' Also include an array 'srt_lines' of 6 short German caption strings matching these scenes. Valid UTF-8 JSON, correct German umlauts and the colon-form 'Schüler:in'.
inputs.realism_notes: Drives the fill_text step and the SRT. gemini-2.5-flash for clean structured German data. Umlauts and the gender-colon form must be correct — a real German ed product would use 'Schüler:in'.

inputs.name: brand_palette_and_type.json
inputs.gen_prompt: Produce a JSON brand sheet for Schugle: primary='#0E7C86' (deep teal), secondary='#F2EFE9' (warm off-white), ink='#23323A' (slate), accent='#E8A23D' (muted amber, used sparingly), font_primary preference='geometric humanist sans (e.g. Source Sans 3 / Inter)', tone keywords=['calm','credible','professional','modern','trustworthy'], forbidden=['chalkboard','graduation cap','cartoon child','smiling teacher stock']. Valid JSON.
inputs.realism_notes: Feeds font_recommend and the template-search query so the chosen Express template and font stay on-brand. Encodes the brief's explicit do-not-use list so the agent self-checks.

outputs.name: schugle_hero_10s_16x9_clean.mp4
outputs.spec: 1920×1080, 30fps, H.264 MP4, exactly 10.0s, SILENT (no audio track), seamless loop, NO on-screen text (clean hero-background version)
outputs.rubric_hook: Loops seamlessly; truly silent; no text baked in; calm enough for a website hero; tilted-g motif present; reads as same campaign as the others.

outputs.name: schugle_hero_10s_16x9_text.mp4
outputs.spec: 1920×1080, 30fps, H.264 MP4, 10.0s, silent, WITH German on-screen text ('Verstreute Notizen' -> payoff), text from editable layer
outputs.rubric_hook: German strings correct incl. umlauts; text legible and well-placed; identical motion to clean version minus text; still silent.

outputs.name: schugle_promo_30s_set.mp4
outputs.spec: Three files: 30s at 16:9 (1920×1080), 9:16 (1080×1920), 1:1 (1080×1080); 30fps H.264; music bed at -16 LUFS; German on-screen text preserved & legible in every ratio
outputs.rubric_hook: Subject/text stay in-frame and legible after each reframe; music synced & loudness-correct; narrative beats match the summarized reference; consistent look across ratios.

outputs.name: schugle_explainer_2min_set.mp4
outputs.spec: Three files: 120s at 16:9 / 9:16 / 1:1; 30fps H.264; music bed -16 LUFS; visibly reuses 10s + 30s material; full 5-beat narrative
outputs.rubric_hook: Demonstrably reuses earlier assets (continuity); all five German beats present and ordered per the storyboard; pacing reads as a 2-min explainer not a stretched promo.

outputs.name: schugle_music_bed.wav
outputs.spec: Stereo WAV, 48kHz, ~120s, calm modern non-corporate; the licensed Stock track delivered as a separate stem; loudness -16 LUFS integrated
outputs.rubric_hook: Delivered separately (editable), correct format/loudness, matches the licensed Stock asset id in the manifest, tone fits 'calm/credible'.

outputs.name: schugle_captions_de.srt
outputs.spec: Valid SRT, German, ~6 cues timed to the 30s promo, UTF-8 with correct umlauts and 'Schüler:in' colon-form
outputs.rubric_hook: Valid SRT timecodes; German text matches on-screen copy; umlauts/colon-form correct; cue timing lands on the right scenes.

outputs.name: schugle_license_manifest.csv
outputs.spec: CSV/MD listing every font, Adobe Stock asset (with license id), Express template id, and music track used, each with license terms and source
outputs.rubric_hook: Every consumed asset accounted for; Stock license id present and matches the licensed download; fonts + template id listed; rights line for Schugle reuse included.

connector_workflow.output: cc_delivery_folder_structure (Hero/, Promo/, Explainer/, Audio/, Editable/, Captions/, License/)

spec.iterative_chaining_note: The chain is genuinely sequential, not parallel. (1) The supplied reference tutorial is summarised (step 3) and that beat list is what shapes the template search (step 4), the storyboard, the Stock-music sizing (step 6), and the extended cuts (step 8) — a content output feeding three downstream decisions. (2) The brand-identity outputs feed forward: the traced g-icon (step 1) and recommended font (step 2) both flow into fill_text (step 5) and animate_design (step 7). (3) The core pivot is the Express design pipeline: search_design (4) -> fill_text (5, consuming the German data + icon + font) -> animate_design (7) produces the 10s master, which is then the literal input to the 30s/2min extension (8), the quick-cut explainer assembly (9), and the multi-ratio reframe (10). Each video file is derived from the one before, not regenerated — the 2-min explainer (9) is built by re-sequencing the 10s (7) and 30s (8) segments, satisfying the brief's 'may reuse material.' (4) The licensed music (6) is cleaned into a separate stem (11). (5) Finally the folder tree (12) is created and every produced artifact — 9 video files, the clean no-text hero, the WAV stem, the SRT derived from the same German data used in step 5, and the license manifest carrying the step-6 Stock id and step-4 template id — is uploaded together (13). Inputs are heavily reused: the g-icon spans steps 1/2/5/7, the German copy data spans 4/5/11/13, the reference summary spans 3/4/6/8/9.
spec.difficulty_rationale: HARD on every axis a long-horizon agent struggles with. (a) Long horizon: 13 dependent connector calls across three tool groups with no shortcut — skipping the summarize step de-grounds the storyboard, skipping the icon trace breaks brand continuity. (b) Heavy state tracking: the agent must carry the 5-beat order, the exact German strings (umlauts + 'Schüler:in' colon-form), the chosen font, the template id, and the Stock license id all the way to the final manifest — and must keep the clean no-text hero and the text version as two distinct branches of the same 10s master. (c) Exact-spec pressure: nine video files at three precise resolutions (1920×1080 / 1080×1920 / 1080×1080), 30fps, 10.0/30/120s exact durations, a SILENT loop vs scored versions, music at -16 LUFS — any wrong dimension, a baked-in caption on the 'clean' version, or audio on the silent hero fails a rubric hook. (d) Massive asset reuse and continuity: three videos must read as one campaign, so the agent must reuse the 10s/30s segments to build the 2-min rather than regenerate, and keep German text legible after subject-aware reframing into portrait and square — a classic place where text drifts out of frame. (e) Faithfulness traps: the brief explicitly forbids generic-school clichés and demands editable text + a complete license manifest, so a plausible-looking but flattened or unlicensed output silently fails. The agent must also correctly NOT add voiceover (the client supplies German VO later) — over-helping breaks the spec.

issues: CAPABILITY MISUSE (step 11): media_enhance_speech is applied to a licensed instrumental Stock MUSIC bed. The capability sheet (line 69) defines media_enhance_speech as 'clean/denoise/enhance a VOICE recording (podcast/VO cleanup)' — it is a speech tool, not a music processor and not a loudness normalizer. There is NO connector tool that normalizes audio to a target -16 LUFS integrated, so the brief's '-16 LUFS WAV stem' requirement (schugle_music_bed.wav rubric hook) has no real tool behind it. Running speech-denoise on a music-only track is wrong-modality and may degrade it.

MISSING GENERATING STEP for the clean no-text hero (output schugle_hero_10s_16x9_clean.mp4). fill_text (step 5) produces ONE text-bearing filled design; animate_design (step 7) animates THAT text design. Only one animate_design path to the 10s master exists, so the 'NO on-screen text' clean version is asserted as an output but is never actually produced by any branch. The text and no-text versions are described as 'two distinct branches of the same 10s master' in the difficulty_rationale, but the workflow contains only the text branch.

OVER-CLAIMED DURATION CONTROL: the sheet describes animate_design only as 'add motion to an Express design (fade/slide/zoom)' with no documented duration/fps/codec parameters. The spec demands exact 10.0s / 30s / 120s durations, 30fps, H.264. Step 8 further claims a SINGLE animate_design call emits BOTH master_motion_30s AND master_motion_120s (two different-length files from one call) — unsupported by the sheet.

TEXT-LEGIBILITY AFTER REFRAME NOT GUARANTEED: video_resize is 'subject-aware' and 'same len' (line 68), i.e. it reframes around the SUBJECT, not around baked-in text. Multiple rubric hooks require German on-screen text to 'stay in-frame and legible in every ratio' after reframe to 9:16/1:1. Subject-aware autoreframe routinely crops lower-third/title text out of frame; the tool cannot guarantee this rubric hook.

SRT and license CSV deliverables (schugle_captions_de.srt, schugle_license_manifest.csv) have NO generating connector step. Step 13 only UPLOADS them. No tool in the chain authors the SRT timecodes or writes the CSV — they must be authored outside the connector, but the spec lists them as graded deliverables without a generating action, leaving an io-completeness gap.

video_create_quick_cut (step 9) is 'NOT speech-aware' and produces an AI highlight/sizzle reel from clips (line 66-67). Using it to deterministically ASSEMBLE a 2-min explainer 'in beat order' to an exact 120.0s is a stretch: quick-cut selects engaging segments by visual-engagement metrics, it is not a timeline sequencer and does not guarantee exact duration or beat order.
fixes: Replace step 11 (media_enhance_speech on the music bed) with an honest path: either deliver the licensed Stock music track AS-IS as the separate WAV stem (drop the -16 LUFS / denoise claim, since no connector tool normalizes loudness), or move loudness-normalization out of the connector chain and label it an out-of-band/manual step. Remove the rubric hook that grades '-16 LUFS integrated' unless a real loudness tool exists. media_enhance_speech should only ever touch a VO/voice asset.

Add an explicit no-text branch for the clean hero: e.g. a second search_design+animate_design (or animate_design on the un-filled template / a text-layer-hidden variant) to produce schugle_hero_10s_16x9_clean.mp4. As written, that output has no generator. If the connector cannot hide/remove the text layer, drop the clean-no-text deliverable or generate it from a separate un-filled design.

Soften or remove the exact-duration / fps / codec guarantees attached to animate_design, or add a note that these are best-effort outputs not parameterized by the tool. Split step 8 into two separate animate_design calls (one per duration) instead of claiming one call yields both 30s and 120s, and verify animate_design accepts a duration parameter before grading exact 10.0/30/120s.

Either drop the 'text legible & in-frame after reframe' rubric hooks for video_resize, or change the approach so German text is added AFTER reframing (per-ratio text placement) rather than baked into the master before subject-aware resize. Note explicitly that video_resize is subject-aware, not text-aware.

Add explicit authoring steps (or clearly mark as out-of-connector) for the SRT and the license CSV: the SRT is built from foerderplan_sample_data.json srt_lines and the CSV is assembled from the step-4 template id + step-6 Stock license id. State that these are authored locally then uploaded, so the io contract is complete.

For step 9, do not rely on video_create_quick_cut for deterministic exact-length beat-ordered assembly. Either acknowledge it produces a best-effort highlight cut (and relax the 'exactly 120s, full 5-beat ordered' rubric), or move the 2-min assembly to a deterministic sequencing path outside this connector set, since no listed tool is a timeline editor.

spec.title: Cinematic Tribute Video for Guru Vandana cum Birthday Event (Shri Anil Tolia Ji, 250 guests)
spec.grounding_note:
REAL (verbatim from the client's fulldesc in db_all.json): an "Emotional Life Journey Tribute Video" for the "Guru Vandana" + Birthday celebration of Shri Anil Tolia Ji, to be "played on a large screen during the family function in front of friends, family members, children, and guests" (~250 people). The 8-section storytelling flow is real and quoted exactly: (1) Opening Tribute, (2) Childhood Journey, (3) College & Friendship Days, (4) Career & Achievements, (5) Family Journey, (6) Guru Vandana Section, (7) Birthday Celebration, (8) Closing Message. Real: warm/mature/respectful voice narration in primarily English with optional Hindi/Gujarati elements (AI narration explicitly allowed); soft piano / light orchestral / flute royalty-free background music that "blends naturally with visuals" and does "not overpower narration"; smooth cinematic transitions, slow zoom/pan, elegant typography; preferred duration 5–8 minutes; Full HD 1920×1080 MP4 "suitable for LED screen/projector playback"; deliverables = final HD MP4 + editable/source files + "music and asset details used" + one or two revision rounds; budget ₹1500–₹12500 INR; tools the client named include Premiere Pro, After Effects, Canva Pro, and AI narration (Synthesia). The "Content Available" list is real: childhood, college, friends/social, professional/work, marriage & family, recent, and Guru-Vandana photos, plus "Additional content may include short messages/quotes, milestones/dates, family inputs."

ENRICHED (only where the brief genuinely implies it, made explicit so it is honestly long-horizon): The brief says the tribute is built "through photographs, music, voice narration, and cinematic storytelling" and lists raw "family inputs" — I enrich the supplied content to include a handful of RAW EVENT/HOME VIDEO CLIPS (a noisy on-stage live tribute speech a family elder gives, plus candid home/career clips) alongside the photos, because a 250-guest live function realistically yields phone video and because the named toolset (Premiere/After Effects/DaVinci) is video-editing software. This makes media_summarize (find emotional highlights across many raw clips), video_create_quick_cut (auto-cut a sizzle from chosen assetIds), and media_enhance_speech (clean the noisy live on-stage speech to reuse as narration) all faithful to the deliverable rather than invented. The "stage-screen master + a short social/WhatsApp share cut" is a light, defensible enrichment: the family explicitly wants the piece for a large screen, and event families routinely also want a vertical clip to share on the family WhatsApp/Instagram afterward — the brief's audience ("friends, family members, children, and guests") supports this. Nothing about the emotional, non-flashy, premium, royalty-free, HD-MP4 contract is changed.
spec.one_line_ask: Assemble a 5–8 minute cinematic, emotionally-paced Full-HD 1920×1080 tribute video for Shri Anil Tolia Ji's Guru Vandana + birthday function — built from the family's raw photos and event clips, following the 8-section life-journey flow, with a cleaned warm narration VO over soft royalty-free piano/orchestral score — and deliver both the stage-screen master and a short vertical social share cut plus an asset-credits sheet.
spec.full_brief:
Context: A family is organizing a "Guru Vandana" (guru-reverence) cum birthday celebration for Shri Anil Tolia Ji, to be attended by roughly 250 friends, family members, children, and guests. The centerpiece of the function is an emotional life-journey tribute video projected on a large LED screen/projector during the event. The piece must feel emotional but positive, respectful and heartwarming, inspirational, elegant and premium — and must AVOID loud flashy editing, overly dramatic effects, fast social-media-style cutting, and distracting animations. The desired register is "cinematic family tribute / elegant slideshow-documentary," with smooth transitions, slow zoom/pan, and tasteful typography.

The ask: Using the supplied raw materials — chronological photo sets (childhood, college & friendship, career, marriage & family, recent, and Guru-Vandana moments), several raw home/event video clips, and a noisy live on-stage tribute speech recorded at rehearsal — produce a 5–8 minute Full-HD (1920×1080, MP4) tribute that follows the client's exact 8-section flow: (1) Opening Tribute, (2) Childhood Journey, (3) College & Friendship Days, (4) Career & Achievements, (5) Family Journey, (6) Guru Vandana Section, (7) Birthday Celebration, (8) Closing Message. The agent must mine the raw clips for the most emotional, usable moments, auto-cut a tight cinematic "memory sizzle" from the chosen clip segments to seed the montage, clean the noisy on-stage tribute speech into a warm, broadcast-clean narration track (or generate an equivalent warm/mature English VO with optional Hindi/Gujarati blessing lines), score the whole thing with soft, licensed, royalty-free piano/light-orchestral/flute music that blends under — and never overpowers — the narration, and lay elegant section titles and a closing thank-you over the cut.

Constraints & deliverables: Output 1 = the stage-screen MASTER at exactly 1920×1080 (16:9), H.264 MP4, 5–8 minutes, clean enough for large-screen projection, music bed mixed roughly 14–18 dB under the VO. Output 2 = a short (≤60s) vertical 1080×1920 (9:16) social/WhatsApp share cut reframed subject-aware from the master's best moments, same audio treatment. Output 3 = an asset-credits / music-and-source sheet (PDF) listing every licensed Stock track and the source clips used, as the client explicitly requires "music and asset details used." All music must be royalty-free/licensed; narration must be warm, encouraging, emotional, mature and respectful; nothing flashy. The family expects one or two revision rounds, so the agent must keep the intermediate assets (cleaned VO, licensed score, the sizzle, the master) individually addressable for re-edits.

inputs.name: childhood_journey_photos
inputs.gen_prompt: A small set (treat as 6–8 frames) of authentic-looking 1960s–70s Indian family snapshots of a baby/young boy named Anil: faded warm-toned film photographs, slightly soft focus, real film grain and dust, period-correct clothing, a modest middle-class Gujarati home, a school class photo on a bench, a boy in shorts holding a slate. Genuine vintage photo-paper texture, real skin with natural imperfections, individual flyaway hairs, slightly off white balance as old prints have. Candid and unposed, documentary, NOT a glossy AI render, no modern artifacts, no text watermark.
inputs.realism_notes: Must read as scanned old prints: uneven exposure, paper grain, a fold crease or two, period color cast. No CGI sheen. Used as the chronological opening of the montage and to seed the section-1/2 mood.

inputs.name: college_friendship_photos
inputs.gen_prompt: Authentic late-1980s Indian college-life snapshots of the same man (Anil) as a young adult: friends grouped on hostel steps, a cricket-team photo, a scooter, a canteen moment. Mild 1980s film grain, warm faded color, real candid expressions, natural skin pores and stubble, individual hair strands and flyaways, correct hands. Documentary, slightly imperfect framing as friends' cameras would shoot. NOT glossy, no modern phones, no text overlay.
inputs.realism_notes: Period clothing/hairstyles and analog color shift sell the 'college & friendship days' chapter. Candid, not staged-perfect.

inputs.name: career_achievement_photos
inputs.gen_prompt: Professional/work-life photos of the same man across his 40s–50s: at an office desk, receiving a small award/momento on a stage, shaking hands at a business event, a factory or shop-floor visit. Realistic 2000s–2010s digital-camera look, natural indoor lighting with slight flash, real skin texture with pores and faint wrinkles, correct hands and fingers, lanyard/spectacles. Candid documentary, not a stock-photo smile, no text or logos baked in.
inputs.realism_notes: Should look like genuine event/work photos across two decades. Reused for the 'career & achievements' chapter and again, color-graded, in the closing recap.

inputs.name: marriage_family_photos
inputs.gen_prompt: Warm Indian marriage and family photographs of the same man: a 1990s wedding portrait in traditional Gujarati attire with garlands, later family photos with wife and growing children, a festival gathering, grandchildren in recent years. Mix of slightly-faded 90s prints and crisp recent digital frames. Real skin, individual hair strands, genuine candid emotion (a tear, a laugh), correct hands, authentic jewelry and clothing. Documentary family-album feel, NOT CGI-glossy, no watermark.
inputs.realism_notes: Emotional core of the piece. Spans decades so the grade must unify them. Reused across family + closing chapters.

inputs.name: guru_vandana_recent_photos
inputs.gen_prompt: Recent (2020s) photographs themed around guru-reverence and the honoree as an elder/mentor: the man touching an elder guru's feet in respect, garlanding a guru, sitting in a satsang/temple setting, mentoring younger people, a recent crisp portrait in a kurta with a warm dignified expression. Natural daylight and lamp-lit temple tones, real skin with age texture, individual grey hairs, correct hands in pranam gesture. Respectful, documentary, premium but authentic — NOT an AI-perfect render, no text.
inputs.realism_notes: Carries the 'Guru Vandana' chapter and the dignified closing portrait. Recent, sharp, but still candid.

inputs.name: raw_home_event_clips
inputs.gen_prompt: A handful of raw, lightly-shaky handheld phone clips (1080p, native audio) from family life and the run-up to the function: the honoree laughing with grandchildren in a living room; cutting a cake at a small home gathering with off-camera chatter; a slow pan across a decorated stage with marigold garlands before guests arrive; the honoree walking in and folding hands to greet people. Natural ambient room sound, imperfect handheld framing, real people with natural skin/hair, candid documentary look. NOT a polished commercial; phone-video realism with minor motion blur.
inputs.realism_notes: These are the raw clips the agent must SUMMARIZE to find emotional highlights and then QUICK-CUT into a sizzle. Native-audio 1080p (not 720p veo-fast). Several distinct moments so summarization and selection are non-trivial.

inputs.name: onstage_tribute_speech_raw
inputs.gen_prompt: A ~90-second warm spoken tribute in English with a few Hindi/Gujarati blessing phrases, delivered as if by a family elder at a microphone: 'Tonight we honour Anil bhai — a son, a friend, a guide... aaj hum sab unke charno mein...'. Warm, mature, emotional, respectful male voice. Generate clean at 48 kHz, THEN (for this audio-repair task only) the supplied file is degraded to simulate a live PA capture: add room reverb, hum, mic pops, crowd murmur and uneven levels, so the agent has a genuinely noisy on-stage recording to enhance.
inputs.realism_notes: This is the ONE input that is intentionally roughened, because the task literally includes audio-repair: the agent must run media_enhance_speech on it to recover a broadcast-clean narration bed. Content is the real tribute language the family would use.

inputs.name: narration_script_and_milestones
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a JSON file with: (a) the 8 section titles exactly per the client flow (Opening Tribute, Childhood Journey, College & Friendship Days, Career & Achievements, Family Journey, Guru Vandana Section, Birthday Celebration, Closing Message); (b) for each section, an elegant on-screen caption line and an estimated duration in seconds (totalling 5–8 min); (c) key milestones/dates and short quotes the family supplied; (d) a warm 6–8 line closing thank-you message; (e) a music-mood tag per section (soft piano / light orchestral / flute). Mature, respectful, English with two optional Hindi/Gujarati blessing lines.
inputs.realism_notes: Drives the typography, pacing, and chapter structure. Reused when laying titles and when building the credits sheet.

outputs.name: tribute_master_stage_screen.mp4
outputs.spec: 1920×1080 (16:9), H.264 MP4, 5–8 minutes, ~24–30 fps, follows all 8 client sections in order, cleaned warm narration VO over a licensed soft piano/orchestral score mixed ~14–18 dB under the VO, elegant section titles + closing thank-you, smooth slow transitions only (no flashy cuts). Suitable for LED-screen/projector playback at the 250-guest function.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: exact 1920×1080; runtime within 5–8 min; all 8 named sections present and in order; narration is clean/intelligible (no PA hum/crowd from the raw speech survives); music audibly sits UNDER the VO and is the soft/uplifting style requested (not flashy); pacing is slow/cinematic with no fast social-style cutting; titles are tasteful and match the script; emotional, premium, respectful tone.

outputs.name: tribute_social_share_cut.mp4
outputs.spec: 1080×1920 (9:16) vertical, H.264 MP4, ≤60 seconds, subject-aware reframed from the master's strongest moments, same cleaned VO + score, suitable for family WhatsApp/Instagram share.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: exact 1080×1920 vertical; ≤60s; the honoree stays centered/in-frame after reframing (subject-aware, no decapitated heads); audio matches the master's clean mix; it is clearly derived from the master, not a separate edit.

outputs.name: tribute_cinematic_sizzle.mp4
outputs.spec: 1920×1080, H.264 MP4, ~20–40 seconds, the auto-cut highlight reel of the strongest raw-clip moments used to seed the montage (intermediate deliverable kept addressable for revisions).
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: the sizzle is genuinely built from the chosen raw-clip segments (traceable to the summarization picks), is tight and emotional, and is reused inside the master rather than discarded.

outputs.name: narration_clean.wav
outputs.spec: 48 kHz WAV (or AAC), the enhanced/denoised version of the on-stage tribute speech — broadcast-clean, PA hum/reverb/crowd removed, levels normalized.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator A/Bs against onstage_tribute_speech_raw: noise/reverb/crowd reduced, intelligibility and warmth preserved, no robotic artifacts, consistent loudness.

outputs.name: asset_credits_sheet.pdf
outputs.spec: A4/Letter PDF listing every licensed Stock music track (title + Stock asset ID + license), each source clip/photo set used, the narration source, and final render specs — satisfying the client's 'music and asset details used' deliverable.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: every licensed track and source asset actually used in the master is listed with its real Stock ID; render specs (1920×1080, runtime) match the delivered master; readable, organized, complete.

connector_workflow.output: CC-storage URNs/assetIds for every supplied raw clip, the speech audio, and all photo sets
connector_workflow.note: Ingest all family-supplied raw media into CC storage so later video/audio tools can reference stable assetIds. Establishes the assetId namespace reused throughout.

connector_workflow.output: per-clip scene/transcript summaries with timestamped emotional-highlight notes for each raw event clip
connector_workflow.note: Summarize each raw clip to locate the most emotional, usable moments (cake-cutting, greeting guests, grandchildren) before any cutting — the agent must read these to choose segments. Pure intelligence step feeding the cut.

connector_workflow.output: transcript + content summary of the noisy on-stage tribute speech (so the agent knows what the narration says and which lines to keep)
connector_workflow.note: Summarize/transcribe the raw speech audio so the narration content and Hindi/Gujarati blessing lines are known before enhancement and before writing on-screen captions.

connector_workflow.output: verified list of the stored photo assetIds grouped per chapter (childhood/college/career/family/guru-recent)
connector_workflow.note: Confirm and organize the chronological photo assetIds in CC so the montage chapters map to the 8-section flow. State-tracking checkpoint: which assetId belongs to which chapter.

connector_workflow.output: tribute_cinematic_sizzle.mp4 — auto-cut ~20–40s highlight reel from the chosen raw-clip assetIds + segments
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. Feed the assetIds of the raw clips plus the highlight choices from step 2's summaries (target_duration ~30s, prompt = warm emotional family-tribute highlights) to auto-cut a cinematic sizzle that seeds the montage. Output→input chain: step 2 picks, step 5 cuts.

connector_workflow.output: narration_clean.wav — denoised, de-reverbed, level-normalized version of the on-stage tribute speech
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. Clean the noisy PA capture into a broadcast-clean warm narration bed. Step 3's transcript confirms intelligibility was preserved. This becomes the primary VO track.

connector_workflow.output: all chapter photos regraded with a single warm cinematic look so the decades-spanning prints feel unified
connector_workflow.note: Apply one cinematic/warm Lightroom-style preset across every photo set so 1960s prints and 2020s digital frames share a premium, non-flashy grade. Unifies the slideshow visually before assembly.

connector_workflow.output: licensed royalty-free soft piano / light-orchestral / flute score track(s) with real Stock asset IDs, downloaded to CC storage
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. License a soft, uplifting, royalty-free music bed matching the per-section mood tags from the script, sized to underlay the cleaned narration. Real Stock IDs captured here are reused in the credits sheet (step 15).

connector_workflow.output: an elegant Express title/lower-third template suitable for the 8 section titles and the closing thank-you
connector_workflow.note: Find a tasteful, premium typography template for the section titles and closing message — 'elegant typography', not flashy. Feeds the title fills in step 10.

connector_workflow.output: 8 filled section-title cards + closing thank-you card (text from the script), layout preserved
connector_workflow.note: Fill the chosen template with the exact 8 section titles and the closing thank-you from narration_script_and_milestones. Output→input: step 9 template + script data → titled cards consumed by the assembly step.

connector_workflow.output: an ordered 1920×1080 visual edit-decision-list / storyboard: graded photos + sizzle + title cards laid into the 8 sections with slow zoom/pan timing per the script durations
connector_workflow.note: Deterministically lay the graded photos, the seed sizzle (step 5), and the title cards (step 10) into the exact 8-section order with per-section durations from the script — the visual spine of the montage before muxing. Reuses nearly every prior visual output.

connector_workflow.output: level/loudness + duration report on the cleaned VO vs. the licensed score, to set the music ~14–18 dB under the VO and confirm total runtime sits in 5–8 min
connector_workflow.note: Re-summarize the cleaned narration and the licensed track to verify durations and relative loudness so the mux mixes music safely under the VO (a real client constraint). State-check before render.

connector_workflow.output: tribute_master_stage_screen.mp4 — 1920×1080 5–8 min H.264 master: rendered storyboard + cleaned VO + score ducked per step 12 + transitions
connector_workflow.note: Unavoidable glue: ffmpeg mux of the visual spine (step 11), the cleaned narration (step 6), and the licensed score ducked to the step-12 levels into the final timed master with slow crossfades. Minimal local step; everything it consumes came from connector outputs.

connector_workflow.output: tribute_social_share_cut.mp4 — 1080×1920 vertical, ≤60s, subject-aware reframe of the master's strongest stretch
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. Subject-aware reframe of the finished 16:9 master to a 9:16 vertical share cut, keeping the honoree centered. Output→input: the step-13 master is the sole source. (Trim to ≤60s as part of the resize/export pass.)

connector_workflow.output: asset_credits_sheet.pdf — the music-and-asset-details deliverable listing every licensed track (Stock IDs from step 8), source assets (step 4), narration source, and final render specs (from the step-13 master)
connector_workflow.note: Build the client-required 'music and asset details used' sheet into a PDF: pulls the real Stock license IDs from step 8, the source photo/clip inventory from step 4, and the delivered master's specs from step 13. Closes the contract.

spec.iterative_chaining_note: The chain is genuinely output→input throughout. (1) asset_add_file mints the assetId namespace every later tool references. (2/3) media_summarize reads the raw clips and the noisy speech; (5) video_create_quick_cut consumes step 2's highlight picks to auto-cut the sizzle; (6) media_enhance_speech consumes the raw speech (and step 3's transcript) to produce narration_clean.wav. (4→7) asset_search organizes the photo assetIds, then image_apply_preset regrades exactly those into a unified look. (8) asset_license_and_download_stock returns real Stock IDs that are consumed twice — by the mix and by the credits PDF. (9→10) search_design's template + the script JSON feed fill_text to make the 8 title cards. (11) document_render_layout pulls together the sizzle (5), graded photos (7), and title cards (10) into the timed 8-section spine. (12) media_summarize re-reads the cleaned VO (6) and licensed score (8) to set ducking/runtime. (13) the local ffmpeg mux consumes the spine (11) + VO (6) + score (8) + levels (12) to render the master. (14) video_resize takes ONLY the step-13 master and reframes it to the vertical cut. (15) document_convert_pdf consumes the Stock IDs (8), the asset inventory (4), and the master's specs (13) to produce the credits sheet. No step invents data it wasn't handed by an earlier step; the master is literally built from the sizzle, the cleaned speech, the licensed score, and the graded photos produced upstream.
spec.difficulty_rationale: This is hard for an agent on every long-horizon axis. (1) Horizon & breadth: 15 ordered connector calls spanning five capability groups (video/audio, stock/storage, photo, Express template, layout/PDF), with the must-exercise video+audio tools all in play. (2) Heavy state tracking: the agent must maintain a stable assetId namespace from step 1 and remember which photo assetId belongs to which of the 8 named chapters, which raw-clip segments the summaries flagged, and which Stock IDs were licensed — then surface those exact Stock IDs again 7 steps later in the credits PDF. Drop a mapping and the montage chapters or the credits sheet break. (3) Asset reuse: nearly every input is consumed in 3–4 different steps (photos: ingest→organize→grade→layout→credits; raw clips: summarize→cut; speech: summarize→enhance→mix), so the agent cannot treat inputs as one-shot. (4) Exact-spec pressure: 1920×1080 master in a strict 5–8 min window, music mixed 14–18 dB under the VO, a separate exact 1080×1920 ≤60s vertical cut, and a credits PDF whose stated specs must match the actually-delivered master. (5) Judgment under a 'no-flashy' aesthetic constraint: the client explicitly forbids fast/dramatic/social-style editing, so the agent must choose slow transitions and tasteful titles, not default to a punchy sizzle look. (6) True chaining: the final master is only correct if the sizzle, the cleaned narration, the licensed score, and the unified grade were all produced and threaded correctly upstream — a single broken link is visible in the deliverable.

issues: MAJOR (feasibility): Step 14 / Output 2 cannot be produced by video_resize as routed. CONNECTOR_CAPABILITIES_v2.md line 68 explicitly states video_resize is 'reframe/resize a video to a target ratio (subject-aware), SAME LEN'. The spec requires turning the 5-8 minute master into a <=60s vertical cut and even admits 'Trim to <=60s as part of the resize/export pass' (step 14 note). video_resize does NOT change duration, so the 9:16 <=60s share cut is infeasible via the single tool the chain assigns to it.

MINOR (tool-capability overstatement): Step 12 uses media_summarize to produce a 'level/loudness + duration report ... to set the music ~14-18 dB under the VO'. The sheet (A5, line 70) defines media_summarize as transcript/scene summary only. It does not return precise dB loudness/level measurements, so the 14-18 dB ducking target cannot be sourced from this step as written.

MINOR (tool-capability overstatement): Step 11 uses document_render_layout to produce a '1920x1080 visual edit-decision-list / storyboard ... with slow zoom/pan timing per the script durations'. The sheet (A2, line 36) describes document_render_layout as an InDesign-class page-layout renderer (JSON layout -> laid-out page, text+images, columns), not a video EDL/timeline or motion-timing engine. Per-section zoom/pan timing is actually realized in the step-13 ffmpeg mux, not by this tool.

WEAK CHAIN LINK (not disqualifying): Step 3 media_summarize (transcript of raw speech) is listed as an input to step 6 media_enhance_speech, but enhance_speech denoises the raw audio directly and does not consume a transcript; the note frames it as verification ('confirms intelligibility preserved'), not a true data dependency. Similarly step 12's output is not a genuine data input to the step-13 mux. Overall chain still has well over 12 genuine output->input links, so calls_genuine still passes.
fixes: Fix the vertical-cut routing: do NOT rely on video_resize to trim. Either (a) insert a trim/selection step before reframing - e.g., run video_create_quick_cut on the step-13 master (or on the sizzle/best stretch) with target_duration <=60s to produce a <=60s 16:9 clip, THEN video_resize that clip to 1080x1920 (preserving its now-<=60s length); or (b) do the <=60s trim in the local ffmpeg step and feed the trimmed clip to video_resize. Update step 14's note to remove 'Trim to <=60s as part of the resize/export pass' since video_resize is same-len.

Re-route the ducking-level determination in step 12: get the VO and music loudness/duration from the local ffmpeg step (e.g., ffmpeg/loudnorm/ebur128 or ffprobe) rather than media_summarize, OR restrict step 12's media_summarize claim to duration/runtime confirmation only and explicitly state the -14 to -18 dB ducking is computed/applied in the step-13 local mux.

Reword step 11 so document_render_layout only renders the static 1920x1080 elements it actually can (section title cards, per-section composited frames/photo layouts at fixed positions), and move all 'slow zoom/pan timing per section durations' language into step 13 (the local ffmpeg mux), which is where motion and timing are genuinely realized.

Optional tightening for honest chaining: reframe step 3's role as feeding the on-screen caption/script content (which it genuinely informs) rather than as an input to media_enhance_speech, and have media_enhance_speech in step 6 consume only the raw audio (step 1).

connector_workflow.output: upload sessions + presigned PUT URLs for all 5 source assets into CC storage
connector_workflow.note: Initialize headless ingest of every provided source asset so later video/audio tools can reference them by CC URN. Consumes the raw input files as named.

connector_workflow.output: 5 finalized CC asset URNs (raw_capture_urn, avatar_urn, website_urn, scratch_vo_urn, brandkit_urn)
connector_workflow.note: Finalize the uploads opened in step 1; the returned URNs are the canonical handles reused by nearly every downstream step.

connector_workflow.output: walkthrough_summary.json — timestamped transcript + scene/section summary of the 11-min raw app capture (which UI screens appear, in what order, with start/end times)
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. Summarize the long raw capture (raw_capture_urn) to recover what is on screen and when. This summary is the spine: it drives the VO script, the highlight selection, and the callout copy.

spec.full_brief:
DisputeDesk is a Shopify app that helps ecommerce merchants manage chargebacks and assemble structured dispute evidence. The client is preparing their Shopify App Store listing and wants to replace a rough, amateur screen recording with a polished product-introduction film. They have provided a long raw screen-capture walkthrough of the app, a separately recorded male business-avatar intro clip, a capture of the marketing homepage, a scratch voiceover take recorded in a non-treated room, and a brand kit (logo, colors, fonts). The film must open with the professional, calm, trustworthy avatar presenter introducing the chargeback problem and the product in a B2B-SaaS register, then transition smoothly into the website/homepage section, then into a guided app walkthrough with professional zooms, callouts, UI highlights, clean text overlays, and smooth transitions — guided by the voiceover, never feeling like someone randomly clicking around.

The exact, gradeable constraints from the client: the final master must be 1600 × 900 pixels, 16:9, exported as MP4; it must NOT crop important parts of the website or app UI; on-screen text must be readable; and the audio must be clean and balanced throughout (no room noise, no awkward pauses, consistent loudness between the avatar intro and the walkthrough narration). The visual style is clean, modern, professional and trustworthy — polished but not flashy, no distracting effects — on par with professional B2B software demo videos. The walkthrough must be tightened from the long raw capture into a focused highlight that tracks the narration. The deliverable set is the 1600×900 MP4 master plus inline previews for sign-off.

Enrichment (genuinely implied by the brief, which states the video must suit 'Shopify App Store listing, YouTube, and potential customers/investors'): in addition to the 16:9 master, deliver a 9:16 vertical promo (1080×1920) and a 1:1 square autoplay preview (1080×1080), both reframed subject-aware from the same master so no important UI is cropped. All discount/brand copy and the avatar's spoken content must stay consistent with the brand kit; the music bed must be subtle and credible, sitting low under the voiceover.
spec.grounding_note: REAL (verbatim from the freelancer.com brief in db_all.json, project 40479621): product is DisputeDesk, a Shopify chargeback/dispute-evidence app; client wants a polished App Store demo (NOT a raw screen recording); required structure = (1) professional male business avatar intro speaking to camera in calm/trustworthy B2B-SaaS tone, (2) smooth transition into website/homepage section with clean text overlays + subtle motion, (3) app walkthrough built from the existing screen recording with zooms/pans/callouts/highlights/transitions, (4) clear voiceover throughout with smooth pacing and awkward pauses removed, (5) clean modern not-too-flashy style; technical reqs = final size exactly 1600×900 px, 16:9, MP4 export, must NOT crop important website/app UI, text readable, audio clean and balanced, editable project file if possible, 1–2 revision rounds; client can provide rough YouTube demo, original screen recording, website URL, app screenshots/recordings, logo/branding, draft script, avatar/voice direction. ENRICHED (added because the brief implies it but does not spell it out): the explicit 9:16 (1080×1920) vertical promo and 1:1 (1080×1080) square preview are my additions — justified by the brief's own line that the result must suit 'Shopify App Store listing, YouTube, and potential customers/investors', which implies multi-surface distribution; the specific licensed music bed, the two-pass loudness-matched VO enhance, the ~75s highlight target duration, and the four named callout strings are reasonable productions of the stated requirements (clean balanced audio, tightened walkthrough, callouts/highlights) rather than facts stated in the brief. The four app screens named in the quick-cut prompt (dispute inbox, evidence builder, auto-submit, win-rate dashboard) are illustrative of a chargeback-dispute app and would in reality come from the client's actual capture.
spec.title: Create Professional Shopify App Store Demo Video — Avatar Intro + Website/App Walkthrough
spec.one_line_ask: Turn a long raw DisputeDesk app screen-capture, an avatar intro clip, a homepage capture, and a noisy scratch voiceover into a polished Shopify App Store demo film — a 1600×900 16:9 MP4 master with a clean, loudness-matched voiceover, a tightened narration-guided highlight walkthrough, on-brand callout overlays, plus 9:16 and 1:1 reframed promos — with no important UI cropped.

inputs.name: raw_app_walkthrough_capture.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Screen recording, 1920x1080, of a fictional Shopify merchant admin app called 'DisputeDesk' for managing payment chargebacks. An unhurried mouse cursor moves through four screens in order, ~2.5 minutes each so the clip runs ~11 minutes total: (1) a 'Dispute Inbox' table listing chargeback cases with status pills (New / Evidence Due / Submitted / Won / Lost), order IDs, amounts in USD, and due-date countdowns; (2) an 'Evidence Builder' wizard where the user toggles checkboxes for proof items (order confirmation, tracking, AVS match, customer comms) and a live evidence-completeness meter fills toward 100%; (3) a 'One-Click Submit' confirmation modal that posts the evidence packet to the processor; (4) a 'Win-Rate Dashboard' with a line chart of recovered revenue and a donut of won-vs-lost. Clean modern SaaS UI, white background, indigo accent color, Inter font, real readable English UI labels and numbers. Include realistic hesitation, a little scrolling, an occasional mis-click corrected — authentic captured-software feel, NOT a glossy promo render. No human, no voiceover baked in (silent or ambient room tone only).
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like genuine captured software, not a motion-graphics mockup: real cursor latency, scrollbars, a stray hover tooltip, slightly imperfect pacing. UI text must be legibly rendered so media_summarize can transcribe the on-screen sections. Long and partly redundant on purpose so the quick-cut has fat to trim.

inputs.name: avatar_intro_clip.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: 1080p, native audio. A professional male business presenter, late 30s, mid-brown skin, short neat hair with a few individual flyaway strands, light stubble with visible skin pores and natural texture (not airbrushed), wearing a charcoal blazer over a plain shirt, seated mid-shot in a softly-lit modern office with a blurred bookshelf behind. He speaks directly to camera in a calm, credible B2B-SaaS tone for ~28 seconds: 'If you sell on Shopify, chargebacks are quietly costing you revenue — and fighting them is a mess of screenshots and deadlines. DisputeDesk fixes that. It pulls your order evidence together automatically and helps you submit a clean, structured dispute in minutes. Let me show you how it works.' Natural blinking, small natural hand gestures, correct five-fingered hands, realistic lip-sync, documentary realism — believable real person, not CGI-glossy, not an obvious AI avatar. Clean spoken audio with faint natural room presence.
inputs.realism_notes: Skin must show pores and real texture; hair should have individual strands and a flyaway or two; hands must be anatomically correct on any gesture. Audio is clean-ish but has faint room presence so media_enhance_speech has something to improve and loudness-match against the walkthrough VO.

inputs.name: website_homepage_capture.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Screen recording, 1920x1080, ~40 seconds, of the marketing homepage of 'DisputeDesk' (getdisputedesk.com) in a desktop browser. Smooth scroll from a hero section ('Win more chargeback disputes on Shopify — automatically') with a product screenshot, down through a 3-up feature row (Auto-collect evidence / One-click submit / Track your win rate), a short stats band ('Merchants recover an average of 38% more disputes'), and a customer-logo strip. Clean modern SaaS marketing site, white + indigo, Inter font, readable English copy, real cursor scroll with natural easing. No baked-in voiceover.
inputs.realism_notes: Looks like a real browser capture (visible address bar, scrollbar, cursor). Copy must be legible for summarize. Used as the website-transition section between the avatar intro and the app walkthrough.

inputs.name: rough_scratch_vo.wav
inputs.gen_prompt: 48kHz mono voiceover, same calm credible male B2B-SaaS voice as the avatar, ~80 seconds, narrating the website + app walkthrough: 'Here's the DisputeDesk homepage — it shows merchants exactly how the app recovers more chargebacks. Inside the app, the Dispute Inbox lists every open case with its deadline, so nothing slips. Open a case and the Evidence Builder pulls your order confirmation, tracking, and customer messages together — the completeness meter shows when your packet is strong. One click submits the structured evidence straight to the processor. And the Win-Rate Dashboard tracks exactly how much revenue you're recovering over time.' Deliver this clean at 48kHz, then describe it as a 'scratch take recorded in an untreated room' — i.e. the realism layer should add light room reverb, faint HVAC hum, and two or three short awkward pauses/breaths, because the TASK is literally audio-repair (cleaning this is the point).
inputs.realism_notes: This is the one input that is deliberately roughened — the brief's spine is VO cleanup, so the scratch take must carry light room reverb, a faint hum, and a couple of awkward pauses for media_enhance_speech to remove. Script wording is keyed to the on-screen sections so it matches the walkthrough_summary.

inputs.name: disputedesk_brand_kit.json
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a JSON brand kit for the Shopify app 'DisputeDesk': fields for logo_wordmark_svg_ref, primary_color (indigo hex e.g. #4F46E5), secondary_color, accent, font_family ('Inter'), tone_keywords (['calm','credible','professional','trustworthy','B2B SaaS']), four ready callout strings keyed to app sections ('Never miss a dispute deadline','Build evidence in one click','Submit structured proof instantly','See exactly what you recover'), and a music_brief string ('subtle, calm, credible corporate underscore, low energy, no drums-forward, sits under voiceover').
inputs.realism_notes: Plausible real brand kit a client would hand over. The four callout strings and tone keywords are consumed verbatim by the Express overlay fill and the music-bed license query, so they must be present and clean.

outputs.name: disputedesk_demo_master_1600x900.mp4
outputs.spec: Exactly 1600×900 px, 16:9, MP4 (H.264), ~90s: avatar intro (~15s) → website transition → narration-guided app highlight (~75s); animated on-brand callout overlays; muxed clean avatar VO + clean walkthrough VO + low music bed; no important website/app UI cropped; on-screen text readable; audio clean and loudness-balanced.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator verifies exact 1600×900/16:9/MP4; checks NO important UI is cropped; confirms avatar-intro VO and walkthrough VO share consistent loudness with no room noise/awkward pauses; checks callout overlay copy matches the four brand-kit strings and the on-screen section it labels; confirms the highlight follows the narration order rather than random clicking; music sits under the VO.

outputs.name: disputedesk_demo_promo_1080x1920.mp4
outputs.spec: 1080×1920 px, 9:16 vertical, MP4, subject-aware reframe of the master, same audio, key UI/avatar kept in frame.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator confirms exact 1080×1920/9:16; checks subject-aware reframe keeps the avatar and the active UI element in frame (no critical UI sliced off); audio preserved and clean.

outputs.name: disputedesk_demo_squarepreview_1080x1080.mp4
outputs.spec: 1080×1080 px, 1:1 square, MP4, subject-aware reframe of the master for autoplay preview.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator confirms exact 1080×1080/1:1; checks the reframe still shows the labeled UI element for each callout and that text stays readable at square crop.

outputs.name: walkthrough_summary.json
outputs.spec: Timestamped transcript + ordered section summary of the 11-min raw capture (screen names + in/out times).
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks the summary correctly identifies and orders the four app screens with plausible timestamps — this is the artifact that proves the highlight cut and overlay copy were summary-driven, not guessed.

outputs.name: clean_walkthrough_vo.wav + clean_avatar_vo.wav
outputs.spec: Two 48kHz enhanced VO tracks, denoised/de-reverbed, loudness-matched to a single target.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator A/Bs scratch vs enhanced: room reverb/hum/awkward pauses removed, and the two tracks measure to the same loudness target so the intro and walkthrough sound like one continuous narrator.

spec.iterative_chaining_note: The chain is genuinely output→input at every hop. Steps 1–2 upload the five source assets once and mint URNs that are reused 12+ times downstream (no re-uploads). media_summarize on the raw capture (step 3) produces walkthrough_summary.json, which is then consumed three different ways: (a) it seeds the video_create_quick_cut prompt and target ordering in step 8 so the picture cut follows the narrated sequence (critical because quick_cut is explicitly NOT speech-aware), (b) it provides the section labels that the Express template is filled with in step 11, and (c) it implicitly defines the script the scratch VO was recorded against, which media_enhance_speech cleans in step 5. The two media_enhance_speech passes (steps 5 and 6) feed the two clean VO tracks that are re-registered as URNs in step 7 and muxed in step 14. The avatar summary (step 4) drives the avatar quick-cut (step 9). The Express subchain is a clean cascade: search_design (10) → fill_text (11) → animate_design (12) → callout_overlays.mov. The single local mux (step 14) consumes FIVE prior connector outputs at once (avatar cut, walkthrough highlight, animated overlays, both clean VO tracks via their URNs, and the licensed music bed) to build the 1600×900 master. That master is re-registered (step 15) and then fed to TWO independent video_resize calls (16, 17) producing the 9:16 and 1:1 deliverables from the same source URN, and finally all three videos are previewed together (step 18). Break any early link (wrong summary, unmatched VO loudness, lost URN) and every downstream artifact is wrong.

issues: ORPHANED WEBSITE ASSET / MISSING REQUIRED BEAT (most serious): website_homepage_capture.mp4 is uploaded+finalized (steps 1-2, used_in_steps:[1,2]) then never consumed again. No step summarizes, quick-cuts, or composites it, and it is NOT among the step-14 local-mux inputs (which are step9 avatar cut, step8 app highlight, step12 overlays, step7 VO URNs, step13 music). Yet the disputedesk_demo_master_1600x900.mp4 output spec explicitly claims 'avatar intro (~15s) -> website transition -> narration-guided app highlight (~75s)', the verbatim brief lists the DisputeDesk website/homepage section as required structural beat #2 (clean text overlays + subtle motion), and the duration budget 15+75=90s allots the website section 0 seconds. The chain asserts a deliverable section it never actually produces — a genuine INPUT->OUTPUT contract break.

asset_finalize_file_upload is called WITHOUT a paired asset_initialize_file_upload at step 7 (registering the two enhanced VO tracks as new CC assets) and step 15 (registering the assembled 1600x900 master). finalize cannot mint a URN for a brand-new file without a preceding initialize + presigned PUT. Steps 1-2 correctly pair init+finalize; steps 7 and 15 invoke only the finalize half, so the upload sequence is under-specified/incomplete.

media_enhance_speech at step 6 is fed avatar_urn, which is a VIDEO container (avatar_intro_clip.mp4), not an extracted audio track. The capability sheet scopes media_enhance_speech to 'clean/denoise/enhance a voice recording (podcast/VO cleanup)'. Whether it accepts a video URN and extracts/returns enhanced audio is unverified; no separate avatar-audio asset is extracted first.

asset_license_and_download_stock at step 13 is used to license a music bed (music_bed.wav). The tool description on the sheet (A4) scopes it to 'license a Stock photo/vector/video and download it' — it does not list Stock audio/music. Stock-audio existence is only implied by the model-notes section D ('Music bed: licensed Stock audio or generated'), so returning an audio asset from this specific tool is unverified.
fixes: Add the website section to the actual chain: insert a media_summarize on website_urn and a video_create_quick_cut (or trim) on website_urn to produce a ~10-15s website transition clip, then ADD that clip to the step-14 mux inputs and re-budget the master (~e.g. 15s avatar + 15s website + 60-75s app highlight). Alternatively, if the website beat is intentionally cut, REMOVE website_homepage_capture.mp4 from the inputs and delete 'website transition' from the master output spec and one_line_ask so the deliverable matches what is produced. Do not leave an uploaded asset orphaned while claiming its output.

Make the asset-registration steps complete: at steps 7 and 15 prepend an asset_initialize_file_upload (and, if the connector requires it, asset_get_presigned_urls + the PUT) before asset_finalize_file_upload, mirroring the correct init->finalize pairing used in steps 1-2. Update inputs_from accordingly so the finalize consumes the matching initialize.

Disambiguate the audio source for step 6: either (a) note that media_enhance_speech is given the avatar video URN and extracts its audio track (and confirm the connector supports video input), or (b) add an explicit step that extracts/derives the avatar audio asset (e.g. via the local glue or a separate audio URN) and feed THAT to media_enhance_speech.

Confirm asset_license_and_download_stock returns Stock AUDIO; if it only returns photo/vector/video, switch the music-bed sourcing to the sanctioned alternative in sheet section D (generated music bed) or use asset_search to confirm a licensable Stock audio result, and update step 13's note/output to match the verified path.

spec.title: Brand Identity & Marketing Asset Package for Growing PC Company
spec.grounding_note: REAL (verbatim from the Upwork brief in db_all.json): client is "Tech World PC", a growing custom-computer company (gaming PCs, workstations, refurbished systems, repair) selling on eBay/marketplaces, wanting a scalable professional brand identity. The selected logo direction is REAL: "Geometric hexagon icon, integrated 'TW' monogram, clean corporate styling, strong readability at small sizes," with the icon required to work independently for watermarks, social profiles, product photos, stickers, and packaging. The style words (Professional, Modern, Trustworthy, Technical, Premium, Scalable) and reference brands (Intel, Corsair, Lenovo, ASUS, EVGA) and the "do NOT want" list (no esports/mascots/cartoon/Twitch/aggressive gaming-team logos) are REAL. The supplied AI-generated concept artwork is REAL ("We have AI-generated concept artwork used during the brainstorming phase"). The deliverable list is REAL and verbatim: logo package (Primary Horizontal, Stacked, Icon-Only, Light-BG, Dark-BG, Single-Color, Favicon) in AI/SVG/EPS/PDF/PNG at 512/1024/2048/4096px transparent; a concise brand guide (logo usage, color palette, typography, spacing, background usage); marketplace assets (eBay store banner, listing watermark, "Play the Latest Games" promotional banner); social assets (Facebook cover, YouTube banner, profile picture); business assets (business card, warranty card, shipping insert, email signature). ENRICHED (added only where the brief genuinely implies a long-horizon connector workflow, NOT new scope): I made the deliverable concrete by assigning exact connector-renderable specs (pixel dims, transparent PNG exports, SVG vectorization), I added an animated logo sting for social use (the brief explicitly lists YouTube + social profiles + "Play the Latest Games" banner, so a short motion logo for video intros is an honest extension of "social media assets"), and I added a Firefly concept board to "assemble the logo + colorway + template renders into a deliverable concept board" — which operationalizes the brief's requirement to present "a cohesive brand system" and "background usage examples." The brief's explicit anti-trace clause ("we are NOT looking for someone to simply trace AI images... rebuild from scratch using professional vector techniques") is honored: image_vectorize is used to recover a CLEAN editable vector starting point from the rough concept, which the spec then requires to be refined into a true geometric hexagon/TW monogram — not a raw auto-trace dump. No client, brand name, service list, or reference brand was invented.
spec.one_line_ask: Build Tech World PC a production-ready brand identity system from their rough AI concept logo: vectorize and refine it into a clean hexagon/TW-monogram mark with light/dark/single-color/favicon variants, then populate Adobe Express templates for the business card, eBay store banner, "Play the Latest Games" promo banner, Facebook cover and YouTube banner with the brand name and taglines, produce a short animated logo sting for social/video, and assemble the logo + colorway + all template renders into a single Firefly concept board for client sign-off.
spec.full_brief:
Tech World PC is a growing custom-computer company (custom gaming PCs, workstations, refurbished systems, computer repair, upgrades and components) that currently sells primarily through eBay and other online marketplaces and wants to establish a professional, consistent brand identity that scales with the business. They have already completed the concept phase and selected a logo DIRECTION — a geometric hexagon icon with an integrated "TW" monogram, clean corporate styling, and strong readability at small sizes — but the only artifact they have is rough AI-generated concept artwork from brainstorming. They are explicit that they do NOT want a blind trace of the AI image: they want the concept recovered into clean professional vector form and refined into an original, scalable mark. The icon must function independently from the wordmark so it can live in watermarks, social profile pictures, product photos, stickers and future packaging. The brand must feel like a professional technology company in the spirit of Intel, Corsair, Lenovo, ASUS and EVGA — Professional, Modern, Trustworthy, Technical, Premium, Scalable — and must AVOID esports branding, mascots, cartoon characters, Twitch-style graphics and aggressive gaming-team logos.

The deliverable is a complete brand package, not a logo-only project. (1) LOGO SYSTEM: from the supplied concept, produce a clean vector mark and its required lockups — Primary Horizontal, Stacked, Icon-Only, Light-Background version, Dark-Background version, Single-Color version, and a Favicon — exported as transparent PNGs at 512/1024/2048/4096px plus the editable vector (SVG). (2) BRAND CORE: a concise brand-guide concept covering logo usage, color palette (a primary "tech blue" plus a dark slate and a neutral, in the Intel/Lenovo family — NOT neon esports color), typography recommendation, spacing/clear-space, and background-usage examples. (3) MARKETING & SOCIAL ASSETS built on Adobe Express templates and filled with the real brand name and taglines: an eBay store banner, a listing watermark (the Icon-Only mark over product photos), a "Play the Latest Games" promotional banner, a Facebook cover, a YouTube banner, and a profile-picture crop. (4) MOTION: a short animated logo sting (the hexagon assembling / TW reveal) for use as a social and YouTube intro. (5) PRESENTATION: every render — the refined mark, the colorway, and all populated templates — assembled into one Firefly concept board the client can review as the "cohesive brand system" before final source-file handoff.

Hard constraints the agent must respect: the mark must be derived from the SUPPLIED concept (consistent hexagon + TW monogram), not invented from scratch; all logo PNG exports must be on a TRANSPARENT background at the four exact sizes (512/1024/2048/4096); the single-color and dark-background versions must be legible (no color-on-color illegibility); the brand name "Tech World PC" and exactly the listed taglines must appear correctly spelled across every template (text-only fills — the template layouts and the chosen corporate font are not to be visually broken); the palette and styling must read corporate-tech (Intel/Corsair/Lenovo family) with zero esports/mascot/Twitch cues; the animated sting must use the SAME refined mark (no re-drawn or off-brand logo); and the final concept board must contain every asset so a single deep-link presents the whole system.

inputs.name: techworldpc_concept_logo_raw.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A rough, slightly messy AI-brainstorm logo concept for a custom-PC company called 'Tech World PC'. A geometric hexagon icon enclosing an interlocking 'TW' monogram, rendered in corporate tech-blue (#1F6FEB) with a darker slate accent, sitting on a plain off-white background. Make it look like an unrefined first-pass generative concept: very slightly uneven hexagon edges, soft anti-aliased raster edges, a faint drop shadow, minor color banding in the gradient, the monogram strokes not perfectly aligned, a couple of stray semi-transparent pixels around the mark. Clean corporate styling overall (NOT esports, no mascot, no cartoon, no flames, no Twitch glow), readable, but clearly a raster concept that needs to be rebuilt as clean vector. No text other than the TW monogram inside the hexagon. 2048x2048, centered, plenty of margin.
inputs.realism_notes: This is the client's REAL supplied artifact: 'AI-generated concept artwork used during the brainstorming phase.' It must look like an imperfect raster concept (soft edges, slight banding, stray pixels) so that image_vectorize has something real to clean up and the anti-trace refinement step is meaningful. Tech-blue + slate palette keeps it in the Intel/Lenovo family per the brief; deliberately NOT neon/esports.

inputs.name: techworldpc_product_photo.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: A clean, well-lit studio product photograph of a custom gaming PC tower: matte black mid-tower case with a tempered-glass side panel, tasteful blue interior accent lighting (subtle, NOT rainbow RGB), visible graphics card and cooling, photographed three-quarter front on a seamless light-grey studio backdrop with a soft reflection on the surface. Realistic product photography: accurate metal and glass reflections, real dust-free but physically plausible surfaces, natural soft shadow, correct perspective, slight depth-of-field falloff, no text, no logos on the case, no watermark. Premium-but-approachable, in the spirit of a Corsair/Lenovo marketplace listing photo. 2000x1500, landscape.
inputs.realism_notes: Marketplace listing photo onto which the Icon-Only mark is applied as a watermark, and which seeds the eBay/promo banners.
