spec.iterative_chaining_note: The chain is genuinely output→input throughout. The client's rough concept (techworldpc_concept_logo_raw.png) is uploaded and finalized into a URN (steps 1→2), de-backgrounded (3), then vectorized into the single master SVG (4) that EVERY downstream logo asset descends from: image_apply_color_overlay (6) recolors that master into the single-color and dark-bg lockups; document_render_vector (7) renders the master + recolors into the exact-size transparent PNG set and the profile crop; image_fill_area (14) composites the rendered Icon-Only mark onto the uploaded product photo (12→13) for the watermark; document_render_layout (15) pulls the same product URN + rendered logo + the step-11 promo text into the 'Play the Latest Games' banner; and animate_design (17) animates the SAME master SVG into the sting. The brand-brief JSON is the cross-step text spine, feeding font_recommend (9), both fill_text passes (10, 11), and both layout renders (15, 16) so the brand name and the literal 'Play the Latest Games' tagline are spelled identically everywhere. search_design (8) feeds both fill_text passes. asset_inline_preview (18) reads back all renders for a verification checkpoint, and create_firefly_board (19) ingests the union of steps 4, 6, 7, 10, 11, 14, 15, 16, 17 — i.e. the entire produced system — into one deliverable board. Nothing is a dead-end: each producing step is consumed by a later one, and the master SVG alone fans out to five distinct downstream branches.
spec.difficulty_rationale: HARD on every axis the flagship bar cares about. (1) Long horizon: 19 dependent connector calls across three tool groups (express template track + photo/raster + asset storage), with the must-exercise tools image_vectorize, search_design, fill_text (twice), animate_design and create_firefly_board all on the critical path. (2) Heavy state tracking: one master SVG fans out into five downstream branches (recolors, exact-size renders, watermark composite, promo layout, animation) — the agent must keep a single canonical mark and not let variants drift; two separate uploaded URNs (concept logo, product photo) must each be referenced by the right later steps. (3) Exact-spec pressure: four logo variants × four exact pixel sizes (512/1024/2048/4096) all on true transparency, plus five platform templates each at its own non-negotiable dimension (1050x600, 1200x270, 1500x500, 1640x624, 2560x1440 with safe areas) — a single wrong size or a baked-in background fails the rubric. (4) Brand-fidelity traps: the brief explicitly forbids esports/mascot/Twitch/neon styling and demands an Intel/Corsair/Lenovo corporate feel, and explicitly forbids a blind AI-trace — so the agent must both vectorize AND demonstrate refinement, and must keep the palette/motion corporate. (5) Exact-string discipline: the brand name and the literal 'Play the Latest Games' headline must be spelled correctly across every fill_text and layout call, driven from a shared JSON, with a preview checkpoint (step 18) that an honest agent must actually use to catch a missing export size or a typo before the final board. (6) Assembly: the closing create_firefly_board must contain EVERY produced asset, so any earlier omission surfaces at the end.

issues: TOOL MISUSE (step 14): image_fill_area is GENERATIVE FILL (invents/replaces content inside a selection per sheet A1), not a compositor. It cannot place a SPECIFIC known Icon-Only mark as a semi-transparent corner watermark at a set position/opacity. No logo-overlay/compositing tool exists in the sheet, so the techworldpc_listing_watermark_demo.png deliverable is not feasible as routed.

TYPE/CAPABILITY MISMATCH (step 6): image_apply_color_overlay is a RASTER tool (sheet section A1, Lightroom-grade pixel op) but is fed the VECTOR SVG from step 4. Also a single flat color-overlay cannot produce a 'white-on-dark' lockup, which needs two operations (recolor the mark to white PLUS add a dark background). The Dark-BG variant is therefore not producible by this step.

EXPRESS-ONLY VIOLATION (step 17): The sheet states animate_design is 'Express designs only' and does preset motion (fade/slide/zoom). It is fed a raw vectorized SVG/PNG (steps 4/7), which is NOT an Express design, and the requested 'hexagon assembles -> TW reveal' is a bespoke build-on keyframe animation that exceeds preset fade/slide/zoom. The animated sting is overstated and mis-routed.

ANTI-TRACE REQUIREMENT UNSATISFIABLE: image_vectorize (step 4) is auto-trace (raster->SVG). The sheet has NO vector path-editing/refinement tool, yet the spec and the SVG rubric_hook demand a 'rebuilt from scratch, refined, NOT a blind trace' mark and penalize 'auto-trace garbage paths.' The workflow contains no refinement step, so the headline anti-trace constraint cannot be met by any listed tool.

MISSING EXPORT STEP: fill_text is TEXT-ONLY and does not export an image (sheet A3; the sheet's own chain is search_design -> fill_text -> animate_design -> EXPORT). Steps 10/11 nonetheless treat fill_text outputs directly as final PNGs (techworldpc_business_card.png, _facebook_cover.png, _ebay_store_banner.png, _youtube_banner.png). No export/render step is present to turn the filled templates into the deliverable PNGs at their exact dimensions.

MINOR PLUMBING GAP: steps 1->2 and 12->13 go straight from asset_initialize_file_upload to asset_finalize_file_upload, skipping asset_get_presigned_urls / the presigned PUT that uploads the actual bytes.
fixes: Replace step 14 (image_fill_area) for the watermark with a real composite path: render the Icon-Only mark over the product photo via document_render_layout (text+images layout placing the logo image at corner with reduced opacity), or build the watermark as an Express design and export it. Do not use generative fill to place a known logo.

Rework step 6: do the recolors at render time instead of via a raster overlay on a vector. Use document_render_vector with the master SVG re-styled to single-color (#0B1F3A) and to white, and render the Dark-BG variant as white-mark-on-dark-canvas (mark + dark background composited in document_render_layout). Drop image_apply_color_overlay as the vector recolor mechanism.

Re-route step 17: first place the master mark into an Express design (search_design/an Express design doc), THEN call animate_design, and downgrade the description to the preset motion animate_design actually supports (fade/slide/zoom reveal) rather than a custom 'hexagon assembling' keyframe build, OR move the bespoke sting out of scope.

Add an explicit refinement acknowledgment: since no path-editing tool exists, either soften the spec/rubric to accept a clean image_vectorize output as the 'rebuilt vector' (and stop penalizing it as a 'trace'), or note the refinement is a human/source-file step outside the connector set. As written the anti-trace requirement cannot be graded as feasible.

Insert explicit export/render steps after each fill_text: e.g. document_render_layout or an Express export call producing the business card (1050x600), eBay banner (1200x270), FB cover (1640x624), and YouTube banner (2560x1440) PNGs at their exact required dimensions; fill_text alone does not emit these files.
Add asset_get_presigned_urls (and the presigned PUT) between asset_initialize_file_upload and asset_finalize_file_upload for both the concept logo (1->2) and product photo (12->13) uploads.

spec.difficulty_rationale: This is hard because it is a true identity-to-packaging pipeline with 19 chained connector calls spanning four capability groups (asset/stock/storage, photo-raster, document/layout, plus moodboard) and demands heavy long-horizon state tracking. The agent must: (1) upload 8 assets and maintain a URN map it reuses across ~10 later steps without re-uploading; (2) build a moodboard that locks a visual direction every downstream step must honor (matte charcoal + deep red, derived from the brief, not invented); (3) run a three-stage raster chain where each output is the next input — select distractions → generative fill → auto-tone → generative expand → social crops — so any drift (wrong mask, mismatched canvas color) cascades; (4) construct a parametric vector label master and then execute a data-merge across a 6-row SKU CSV, keeping per-record fields and accent colors correct, then collate to a print-ready CMYK PDF with dielines; (5) hold exact-spec pressure throughout — 50ml sleeve with 3mm bleed/dieline, 2400×1000 banner, 1080×1080 and 1080×1920 crops, A6 card, CMYK PDF — while reusing the same logo SVG and font system across label, box, card, and guide for brand consistency. The big trap is firing the must-exercise generative tools out of order or on the wrong upstream artifact (e.g. expanding the un-retouched hero, or merging before the master template is rendered), which silently breaks the brand-consistency rubric the human annotator scores.
spec.full_brief:
BARDE Grooming (working name, shared only with the awarded freelancer) is launching a premium men's grooming/skincare brand. The flagship is a 50ml post-shave / post-haircut roll-on serum aimed at men prone to razor bumps, ingrown hairs, irritation, and dark marks around the neck and beard line. The brand must read premium, masculine, modern, barber-led and performance-focused — built for both DTC ecommerce and barbershop retail — and must NOT look generic, overly clinical, feminine, or like a cheap private-label product. Visual direction: matte black / deep charcoal packaging with cream, white, silver, or deep-red accents; a minimal-but-bold layout; a strong wordmark; and a social-media-friendly, lifestyle-driven feel (reference point for boldness only, not to copy: 'Sleep or Die').

You are hired for the Full Brand Package. Deliver: a logo/wordmark, color palette, typography system, and a basic brand style guide; the 50ml roll-on sleeve/label design; an outer product box design; a routine/instruction card; product mockups and social assets; and print-ready files with dielines plus editable source files. Enriched for this benchmark into a long-horizon pipeline, the engagement also requires: (a) a Firefly moodboard that locks the brand world from the supplied bottle/label concepts and licensed textures; (b) a retouched, distraction-free hero product shot; (c) that hero outpainted into a wide ecommerce/social banner and cropped to platform sizes; and (d) the locked label master rolled across the brand's full six-SKU line via a vector data-merge, then collated to one press-ready PDF.

Constraints and specs: 50ml roll-on shrink-sleeve/label must carry a 2mm bleed and the supplied dieline, keep critical content >=3mm inside trim, and export CMYK print-ready. SKU line = 6 records driven by the provided CSV (variant name, strength, size, accent color). Banner = 2400x1000 px; social crops = 1080x1080 (IG square) and 1080x1920 (story). Routine card = A6, 2-up. Style guide = single sheet with palette (hex + CMYK), type system, and logo lockups. The same vectorized logo and font system must appear consistently on the label, box, card, and guide — brand consistency is explicitly graded. Hand off all artifacts in one organized CC folder with previews.
spec.grounding_note: REAL (verbatim from the Upwork brief in db_all.json): launching a new men's grooming/skincare brand needing a full brand identity + packaging system; flagship is a 50ml post-shave/post-haircut roll-on serum for razor bumps, ingrown hairs, irritation and dark marks on neck/beard line; brand must feel premium, masculine, modern, barber-led, performance-focused, for DTC ecommerce + barbershop retail; must NOT look generic/clinical/feminine/cheap private-label; matte black / deep charcoal packaging with cream/white/silver/deep-red accents, minimal-but-bold layout, strong wordmark, social-media-friendly; 'Sleep or Die' cited only as a boldness reference (not to copy). REAL named deliverables: logo/wordmark, colour palette, typography system, basic brand style guide, 50ml roll-on sleeve/label design, outer product box design, patch pouch design, routine/instruction card design, product mockups, print-ready files with dielines, editable source files. Tools listed: InDesign, Illustrator. Budget: Fixed price, Intermediate. ENRICHED (implied by the brief, added to make it long-horizon and to exercise the required tools): the specific Firefly moodboard step; the hero-shot retouch via generative fill of named distractions (droplets + lamp reflection); the generative-expand into a 2400x1000 banner and the 1080x1080/1080x1920 social crops; the 6-SKU data-merge with a provided CSV (the brief asks for a brand 'system' and 'product mockups' and SKU-friendly identity, but does not literally enumerate six SKUs — the CSV-driven line rollout is an honest extension of 'full brand/packaging system'); the specific Adobe Stock charcoal/metal textures; exact pixel/A6 specs; and the working brand name 'BARDE' (the real client withholds the name until shortlisting, so a placeholder is used). The patch pouch deliverable from the real brief is acknowledged but not exercised in the connector chain to keep the workflow focused on the four must-exercise tools.

inputs.realism_notes: Second direction so the moodboard has a real A/B choice; keep accent placement plausible for a shrink sleeve; authentic reflections and minor real-world imperfections.

inputs.name: label_concept
inputs.gen_prompt: Flat 2D wordmark/label concept for a bold premium men's grooming brand named 'BARDE', strong condensed grotesque all-caps wordmark in cream on matte black, a small geometric barber-blade monogram mark above it, deep-red underline accent, minimal layout, crisp high-contrast edges suitable for vectorization, centered, transparent-safe flat artwork, no photographic background
inputs.realism_notes: Text-bearing/logo art so use the text-capable model; edges must be clean enough that image_vectorize produces a usable SVG; legible 'BARDE' wordmark and a simple mark.

inputs.name: hero_product_shot
inputs.gen_prompt: Hero e-commerce photograph of the finished 50ml matte-black BARDE roll-on serum standing on wet dark slate, single dramatic side light, premium masculine mood, BUT with a couple of realistic distractions present: two stray water droplets on the bottle shoulder and a small bright reflected studio softbox/lamp visible in the glossy cap — these are intentional imperfections to be retouched out later; otherwise photoreal, sharp, shallow depth of field
inputs.realism_notes: Intentionally include the named distractions (droplets + lamp reflection) so image_select_by_prompt and image_fill_area have something real to remove; everything else should look like a genuine premium product photo.

inputs.name: sku_line_render
inputs.gen_prompt: Group product photograph of six matte-black men's grooming bottles of slightly different sizes/variants lined up on charcoal, consistent brand finish, varied accent-ring colors (red, silver, cream, steel-blue, olive, bronze), realistic studio lighting and soft shadows, premium catalog row shot, photoreal, blank labels
inputs.realism_notes: Reference for the 6-SKU family so the data-merge output can be sanity-checked against a plausible line; blank labels so merged artwork is the star.

inputs.name: brand_brief_pdf
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a 1-page brand brief PDF for 'BARDE' premium men's grooming brand: positioning (premium, masculine, barber-led, performance-focused, DTC + barbershop), flagship 50ml post-shave roll-on serum for razor bumps/ingrowns/irritation/dark marks, visual direction (matte black/charcoal, cream/white/silver/deep-red accents, minimal-but-bold, strong wordmark, social-friendly), and a deliverables checklist
inputs.realism_notes: Plausible client-facing brief; drives stock search terms (step 3) and font rationale (step 6).

inputs.name: sku_data_csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Produce a 6-row CSV for a men's grooming line with columns: sku_code, variant_name, concern, size_ml, strength, accent_hex, accent_name. Rows like: BRD-01,Post-Shave Roll-On,Razor bumps,50,Daily,#8E1B1B,Oxblood; BRD-02,Beard-Line Serum,Ingrown hairs,30,Intensive,#C0C0C0,Silver; plus four more plausible variants with distinct accent_hex values
inputs.realism_notes: Real-looking merge data; exactly six rows; each accent_hex must be a valid hex so per-SKU accent recoloring is checkable in the merge output.

inputs.name: label_template_vector
inputs.gen_prompt: A clean 50ml roll-on shrink-sleeve label dieline template as flat vector artwork: rectangular sleeve panel with marked 2mm bleed border, a dashed dieline/trim edge, a 3mm interior safety margin, labeled placeholder zones for WORDMARK, VARIANT NAME, CONCERN, SIZE 50ml, BATCH/BARCODE, and an ACCENT BAR region; neutral gray placeholders, no real brand content, print-spec accurate
inputs.realism_notes: Must encode real print structure (bleed/trim/safety + named fields) so document_render_vector and document_merge_data_vector have genuine placeholders to fill per CSV row.

spec.iterative_chaining_note: The workflow is a continuous output-to-input chain, not parallel one-offs. Storage: steps 1-2 turn the 8 supplied assets into a URN map that is reused in steps 5, 13, 14, 16, etc. Stock: step 3's search results feed step 4's licensing, whose two licensed textures feed the moodboard (5) and the box composite (16). Identity: step 5's Firefly moodboard locks the visual world consumed by font_recommend (6), the generative expand (11), and the layout deliverables (17). Logo: label_concept -> image_vectorize (7) -> placed into the label master (13), the box (16), the card/guide (17). Raster retouch chain: hero_product_shot -> image_select_by_prompt (8) -> image_fill_area removes the masked distractions (9) -> image_apply_auto_tone (10) -> image_generative_expand outpaints the cleaned, toned hero into a 2400x1000 banner (11) -> image_crop_and_resize produces the 1080 square/story crops (12). Each raster step strictly consumes the immediately prior pixel output. Data-merge core: step 6 fonts + step 7 logo + the supplied dieline -> document_render_vector builds the parametric label master (13) -> document_merge_data_vector rolls that master across the 6-row sku_data_csv (14) -> document_convert_pdf collates the 6 merged labels into one CMYK print PDF (15). Handoff: steps 9, 11, 12, 15, 16, 17 all feed asset_add_file into one folder (18), which feeds asset_inline_preview (19). The four must-exercise tools sit at load-bearing junctions: create_firefly_board (5) gates the brand world, image_fill_area (9) gates the clean hero everything visual depends on, image_generative_expand (11) gates the banner/social set, and document_merge_data_vector (14) gates the entire SKU-line packaging output.
spec.one_line_ask: Deliver a complete BARDE men's-grooming brand and packaging system — moodboard, logo/type, a retouched hero shot expanded into social banners, and a print-ready 50ml roll-on label rolled across the full 6-SKU line — as organized, editable, print-ready CC assets.

outputs.name: brand_moodboard

outputs.rubric_hook: Does the board cohere to the brief's visual direction (matte black/charcoal, cream/silver/deep-red), include the supplied concepts, and read premium/masculine rather than generic?

outputs.name: hero_clean

outputs.rubric_hook: Are BOTH named distractions fully and cleanly removed with believable matte-surface fill, no smearing or halo, and is exposure balanced?

outputs.name: hero_banner

outputs.rubric_hook: Is the expand seamless (no seam line, consistent lighting/color), correct 2400x1000 dimensions, and does the added canvas match the brand world?

outputs.rubric_hook: Exact dimensions; is the bottle well-composed in both ratios; consistent with the banner?

outputs.name: sku_labels_pdf

spec.difficulty_rationale: This is hard for an agent on five axes. (1) Long horizon with a render-loop: the honest path is 20 connector calls and a per-SKU photo-prep loop applied to all 75 images (steps 6–11 each fan out over the whole set), so the agent must batch and not lose rows. (2) Heavy state tracking across two pipelines that must converge: a photo pipeline (crop→mask→invert→fill→tone→white-balance→re-upload) and a document pipeline (template→two data-merges→assemble→two PDFs→style guide), where the merge (step 14) only works if each cleaned photo URN from step 12 is correctly keyed back to its SKU row in the CSV — one mismatched key silently produces the wrong photo on a page. (3) Many reused assets: the CSV, the vectorized logo, and the recommended fonts are each consumed by 4+ later steps; the 75 photos thread through six steps each. (4) Exact-spec pressure: two output PDFs with different, non-negotiable specs (8.5×11 CMYK with trim/bleed for print vs compressed sRGB with a hyperlinked TOC for web), plus a uniform 2000×2000 cell size so the merged grid stays aligned. (5) Taste-under-constraint judgment: the brief explicitly frames the rough mockup as 'a direction, not a brief' and invites elevation, so the agent must infer an 'industrial precision' design system, encode it once in a connector-renderable template (not a desktop .indd, which the capability sheet flags as non-headless), and then prove it scales — the literal Phase-1 success criterion. Getting any single template parameter wrong propagates across ~10 merged pages.
spec.full_brief: TSI Poultry is a multi-branch industrial and poultry-equipment distributor (Louisiana, Oklahoma, Tennessee) building a new catalog for its poultry division. The engagement is two-phase, and this task covers Phase 1, the pilot: the Winching & Suspension Systems catalog — roughly 10–12 pages covering ~75 SKUs in a single product category. The explicit point of Phase 1 is not just a pretty PDF; it is to lock in the design system, typography, and a reusable template that Phase 2 (the full multi-category poultry catalog) will scale from. The client provides everything needed: a rough mockup ('a direction we like, not a brief — genuinely open to your professional take'), a price list with every SKU, description, and cross-reference part number to industry-standard equivalents, a full set of product photography shot 'consistently on white background' (no stock, no reshoots), and brand assets (TSI Poultry logo and color palette). The desired aesthetic is 'industrial precision' — technical-documentation restraint, premium without being precious — for an audience of purchasing managers, integrators, and farm operators who care about specs and part numbers more than decoration but who respect a serious, well-built reference document.\n\nThe deliverables are fixed and specific: a print-ready PDF (8.5\"×11\", correct trim/bleed, CMYK); a web/email PDF (compressed, sRGB, with a hyperlinked table of contents); an editable source template; and a short template/style guide so Phase 2 can scale consistently. The honest workflow is a long-horizon data-merge over the ~75-row spec: design one master page template that proves the system, then drive every per-product page from the CSV/JSON via document_merge_data_layout, generate the cross-reference and price-per-quantity rider sheets via document_merge_data_vector, and — critically — clean up the supplied product photos before placement. The 'consistent white background' photography is in practice grimy and uneven (cast shadows, seams, tape marks, grey gradients), so each of the 75 images must be normalized and have its background generatively cleaned with image_fill_area to reach a true print-grade pure white before it is merged into the grid. The agent must keep every cleaned photo correctly keyed to its SKU row, assemble the fixed pages (cover, hyperlinked TOC, divider) around the merged pages, and export the two PDF formats from one assembled source plus the style guide.
spec.grounding_note: REAL (verbatim from the Upwork brief in db_all.json): multi-branch industrial & poultry-equipment distributor across Louisiana/Oklahoma/Tennessee; two-phase engagement; Phase 1 = pilot 'Winching & Suspension Systems Catalog', ~10–12 pages, ~75 SKUs, single category; Phase 1's stated purpose is to lock in the design system, typography, and template that Phase 2 scales from; client provides a rough mockup ('a direction we like, not a brief'), a price list with all SKUs + descriptions + cross-reference part numbers to industry-standard equivalents, a full product-photo set shot on white (no stock/reshoots), and brand assets (TSI Poultry logo + color palette); aesthetic = 'industrial precision'; audience = purchasing managers, integrators, farm operators who care about specs/part numbers; deliverables = print-ready PDF (8.5×11, trim/bleed, CMYK), web/email PDF (compressed, sRGB, hyperlinked TOC), editable source (InDesign preferred, Canva acceptable), and a short template/style guide; budget Hourly $5–$45; tool listed = InDesign. ENRICHED (implied, not stated literally): the specific count of 75 cleaned cell images and the 2000×2000 uniform cell size; the assertion that the 'consistent white' photos are actually dirty and need generative background cleanup via image_fill_area (the brief says they are clean — we enrich this because real distributor product shots almost always need white-point cleanup, and it is the honest reason the must-exercise tool belongs); the explicit split into document_merge_data_layout for product pages vs document_merge_data_vector for spec/pricing rider sheets; the exact folder/URN plumbing; the price-per-quantity rider format. The capability sheet's note that desktop .indd <<field>> data-merge is NOT headless is respected: the editable source is delivered as a connector-renderable template spec, not an .indd, which honors the brief's 'Canva acceptable if cleanly layered' fallback in spirit (layered, scalable, editable).

inputs.name: price_list.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a realistic B2B industrial catalog price list as CSV for ~75 SKUs in the 'Winching & Suspension Systems' category for poultry-house equipment (winches, hand hoists, cable, snatch blocks, pulleys, brackets, S-hooks, turnbuckles, galvanized chain). Columns: sku, product_name, short_description (1 technical sentence with capacity/material, e.g. '1/4" galvanized aircraft cable, 7x19 strand, 1,400 lb breaking strength'), long_description, cross_reference_part_no (equivalent industry-standard part numbers, e.g. 'CM Hammerlok 665, Crosby G-209'), unit_price_usd, price_per_100_usd, qty_break_note, category_subgroup, image_filename (winch_photo_01.jpg … winch_photo_75.jpg). Use plausible distributor pricing and real-sounding cross-reference brands (Crosby, CM, Campbell, Cooper). Keep SKUs in a consistent format like TSI-WS-0001.
inputs.realism_notes: Must read like a real distributor export: consistent SKU scheme, believable capacities/materials, cross-reference brands purchasing managers recognize, two price columns (each + per-100). image_filename column is the join key to the cleaned photos.

inputs.name: sku_copy.json
inputs.gen_prompt: Produce a JSON array keyed by SKU (matching price_list.csv) holding the longer marketing/spec copy that won't fit in the CSV: {sku, spec_bullets:[3-5 technical bullets like working_load_limit, finish, dimensions, standard_compliance], application_note, warranty}. Tone: restrained technical documentation, no marketing fluff, written for integrators and farm operators.
inputs.realism_notes: Separates short CSV fields from richer per-SKU spec blocks so the layout template has both a compact grid cell and a fuller spec block. SKU keys must exactly match the CSV.

inputs.name: tsi_logo.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A clean B2B industrial brand logo for 'TSI Poultry', delivered as a flat raster PNG on transparent background: a bold geometric wordmark 'TSI' with a small poultry/feather-and-gear motif, restrained two-color (deep slate navy + safety orange accent), the words 'POULTRY EQUIPMENT' set in a small technical sans beneath. Crisp edges, no gradients, no 3D, looks like a real industrial distributor's logo printed on equipment and catalogs.
inputs.realism_notes: Provided as raster (as real clients do) so the workflow must vectorize it (step 5). Two-color, print-safe, flat — vectorizes cleanly. Transparent background for header placement.

inputs.name: brand_palette.json
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a brand palette JSON for TSI Poultry: primary (deep slate navy), secondary (safety orange), neutrals (paper white, warm grey, near-black ink), each with hex, CMYK, and Pantone-equivalent; plus type-color roles (header_ink, body_ink, rule_lines, accent). Industrial, restrained, print-oriented.
inputs.realism_notes: CMYK + Pantone present because the deliverable is print CMYK with trim/bleed; drives the cover, template, and style-guide color rules.

inputs.name: client_rough_mockup.pdf
inputs.gen_prompt: A rough, slightly amateur 1-page catalog mockup PDF a non-designer client threw together: a 2-column product grid with placeholder boxes for product photos, product names, SKUs, prices, and a cross-reference line, plus a top band with a logo placeholder and a footer with a page number. Slightly uneven spacing, default-looking fonts, a couple of misaligned boxes — clearly 'a direction we like' rather than a finished design. Convey the intended structure (grid, SKU, cross-ref, spec) without polish.
inputs.realism_notes: Intentionally rough/misaligned so the agent must elevate it ('taste, not mandate') rather than copy it. Rendered as a PDF page so the workflow extracts it via document_convert_pdf to read the intended grid.

inputs.name: winch_photo_set (75 raw product JPEGs)
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal studio product shots of industrial winching & suspension hardware for poultry houses — hand winches, ratchet hoists, galvanized chain, snatch blocks, pulleys, cable spools, turnbuckles, S-hooks, mounting brackets — each on a SUPPOSED white sweep but realistically imperfect: visible soft cast shadows, a faint grey gradient toward the corners, a seam line where the sweep meets the table, occasional bits of gaffer-tape residue or dust, slightly warm/yellow white balance, inconsistent framing and zoom across shots. Real galvanized-steel texture, machining marks, brushed-metal reflections, tiny scratches and oil smudges on the hardware. Documentary product-catalog look, NOT glossy CGI — like a distributor's in-house photographer shot 75 SKUs quickly in one afternoon. Vary the subject per SKU.
inputs.realism_notes: The deliberate imperfection (shadows, grey gradient, seams, tape residue, warm cast, inconsistent framing) is the whole reason the photo-prep loop and image_fill_area are needed. Real metal texture/scratches keep them photoreal, not CGI. Filenames winch_photo_01..75.jpg join to the CSV image_filename column.

spec.iterative_chaining_note: The two pipelines converge by URN/SKU key. Photo pipeline: raw photos (input) → step 6 crop/resize to a uniform 2000×2000 cell → step 7 subject mask → step 8 inverted (background-only) mask → step 9 image_fill_area cleans the background to true white using the step-6 image + step-8 mask → step 10 auto-tone → step 11 white-balance → step 12 re-upload the finished cells to /photos_clean, yielding stable URNs. Document pipeline consumes the photo pipeline's product: step 13 renders the master template from step 4 fonts + step 5 vector logo + step 3 mockup; step 14 document_merge_data_layout drives ~10 product pages from price_list.csv + sku_copy.json against the step-13 template, pulling the matching step-12 cleaned-photo URN for each SKU row (the join is image_filename↔URN); step 15 document_merge_data_vector builds spec/pricing riders from the same CSV + step-5 logo against the step-13 system. The fixed pages (step 16 cover/TOC/divider) reuse step 5 logo + step 4 fonts + brand_palette. Step 17 document_convert_pdf assembles step 16 + step 14 + step 15 into the CMYK print PDF; step 18 derives the sRGB web PDF from step 17's assembled source; step 19 renders the style guide describing the exact step-13 template + step-4 fonts + step-5 logo; step 20 copies steps 17/18/19/13 into the handoff folder. Reuse is dense: the vectorized logo (step 5) appears in steps 13/15/16/19; the recommended fonts (step 4) in 13/16/19; the CSV in 14/15; each raw photo in six steps (6–11) before its cleaned URN is keyed into the merge at step 14.
spec.one_line_ask: Design the 'industrial precision' template for TSI Poultry's pilot Winching & Suspension Systems catalog, generatively clean all ~75 supplied product photos to true white, data-merge them into a ~10–12-page 8.5×11 spread with cross-reference/pricing rider sheets, and deliver a print-ready CMYK PDF, a compressed sRGB web PDF with hyperlinked TOC, the editable template source, and a short style guide for Phase 2.

outputs.name: catalog_print.pdf
outputs.spec: ~10–12 pages, 8.5"×11" page (8.75"×11.25" with 0.125" bleed all sides), CMYK, 300 dpi image cells, crop/trim marks, ~75 SKUs across a 2-column product grid plus cover, TOC, and spec/pricing rider pages
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: correct trim size + bleed + crop marks present; CMYK (not RGB); every SKU cell shows the CORRECT cleaned photo keyed to its SKU (spot-check 10 rows); cross-reference part numbers present and legible; consistent grid alignment across all product pages; logo crisp (vector) in every header.

outputs.name: catalog_web.pdf
outputs.spec: Same page order as print, 8.5"×11", sRGB, compressed (target <8 MB), screen-resolution images, working hyperlinked table of contents linking to each section/page, derived from the same assembled source as the print PDF
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: file is meaningfully smaller than print PDF; color space is sRGB; TOC links actually jump to the right pages; no crop marks/bleed (web trim).

outputs.name: product_photos_clean (75 images)
outputs.spec: 75 PNG/JPEG cells, uniform 2000×2000 px, true pure-white (#FFFFFF) seamless background, no cast shadow/seam/tape residue, consistent exposure and neutral white balance, subject centered
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks a sample: background is genuinely uniform white (no grey gradient/shadow/seam), product edges intact (no over-fill eating the hardware), exposure/white-balance consistent across the set, framing uniform.

outputs.name: page_template + style_guide.pdf
outputs.spec: Editable connector-renderable template spec (the master page/grid) PLUS a short multi-page style guide PDF documenting grid, type scale (chosen fonts), color (hex/CMYK/Pantone), logo usage, and 'how to add a SKU' so Phase 2 scales
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: template is genuinely parameterized/reusable (not a one-off page); style guide states the exact fonts, colors, grid, and logo rules actually used in the catalog; instructions would let someone add a SKU to Phase 2 without redesign.

spec.title: B2B Industrial Catalog Designer — Poultry Equipment Pilot + Full Catalog Rollout

issues: FEASIBILITY STRETCH (steps 17/18 PDF assembly): The capability sheet describes document_convert_pdf only as 'image/spec <-> PDF (and PDF page extraction).' Step 17 uses it to MERGE three separate rendered outputs (cover + ~10 merged product pages + vector rider sheets) into one ordered multi-page PDF AND impose print prepress controls (CMYK, 0.125in bleed, crop/trim marks). Multi-document concatenation/imposition and CMYK+bleed+crop-mark control are not stated capabilities anywhere on the sheet. This is the load-bearing print deliverable and its feasibility is asserted, not grounded.

FEASIBILITY STRETCH (step 18 web PDF derivation): Deriving catalog_web.pdf from the print PDF requires CMYK->sRGB color conversion, compression to <8MB, AND injecting a WORKING hyperlinked internal TOC. document_convert_pdf is not documented to do color-space transform, downsampling/compression, or hyperlink injection. The 'working hyperlinked TOC' (rubric_hook says links must actually jump to the right pages) is the riskiest unsupported claim.

TYPE MISMATCH (step 19, also latent in 16): document_render_layout is defined as 'render a JSON layout spec -> laid-out page' (a page, not a PDF). Step 19 names its direct output 'style_guide.pdf', skipping the document_convert_pdf step that the rest of the workflow uses to reach PDF. Step 16 correctly outputs 'pages' but step 19 conflates render with PDF export.

ENRICHMENT INVERTS A STATED BRIEF FACT: The verbatim Upwork brief says photos were shot 'consistently on white background' and explicitly 'no reshoots'; the spec ENRICHES this into 'actually grimy/uneven' to justify the must-exercise image_fill_area (steps 7-9). This is disclosed in grounding_note (honest), but the entire generative-cleanup spine rests on contradicting a real client statement rather than on a stated need.

METADATA: groups_covered=['photo_raster','document_layout'] declares only 2 groups, but the flagship definition wants >=3 groups and the workflow actually also exercises asset plumbing (create_folders/add_file/copy_assets). The asset group is used but not declared; minor inconsistency between declared coverage and actual coverage.

REDUNDANCY NOTE (steps 7-9 vs simpler path): isolating subject -> invert -> generative-fill the background to pure white is a valid but roundabout way to reach a clean white cell; image_remove_background (cut to transparent) + flatten on #FFFFFF would reach 'true pure-white background' more directly. Not wrong, but the chain length is partly to inflate the per-photo step count (6 steps x 75 images) for the long-horizon framing.
fixes: Step 17: change the assembly mechanism to first render each page set via document_render_layout/document_render_vector, then use document_convert_pdf in its supported 'spec -> PDF' direction with a single ordered page-list spec; explicitly state that CMYK/bleed/crop-marks are encoded in that render/convert spec, OR drop the hard CMYK+bleed+crop-mark guarantees to what the connector can verifiably produce. If true print imposition is required, route through the Canva export-design path (sheet section B) and say so.

Step 18: split the web-PDF derivation so the hyperlinked TOC and sRGB/compression are produced at RENDER time (re-render the assembled pages to a screen/sRGB spec with TOC links baked into the layout), not by post-processing the print PDF through document_convert_pdf. Alternatively note that document_convert_pdf cannot inject internal hyperlinks and move the TOC-link responsibility into document_render_layout's spec.

Step 19: insert an explicit document_convert_pdf call after document_render_layout (render style-guide pages -> convert to style_guide.pdf), matching the pattern used for the catalog. Change the step-19 output from 'style_guide.pdf' to 'style-guide pages (laid out)' and let a convert step emit the PDF.

grounding_note: keep the disclosure but reframe the enrichment so it does not flatly contradict the brief — e.g., 'photos shot in-house on white but with the usual uneven white-point/seam/shadow artifacts that need normalization before print,' which justifies image_fill_area without asserting the client lied about clean photos.
Metadata: update groups_covered to ['photo_raster','document_layout','asset_plumbing'] to reflect actual tool usage and satisfy the >=3-group flagship bar explicitly.

Optional simplification: if the 6-step-per-photo chain is meant to be genuine rather than count-padding, justify each tonal step (auto_tone AND color_temperature) as addressing a distinct defect; otherwise consider image_remove_background -> flatten-on-white as the cleaner canonical path and reserve fill_area for in-frame defect removal (tape/seam) only.

spec.title: Bold Monreale Perfume Photography
spec.grounding_note:
REAL (verbatim from the client brief in db_all.json): the client is launching the "Monreale" fragrance line and wants visuals that feel "bold, modern and instantly appealing to a younger, unisex crowd"; an attached brief + small inspiration board with five flagged reference images (row 1 #2 & #5, row 2 #5, row 3 #1 & #3) that capture "contrast-heavy lighting, saturated colour accents and confident product placement"; the work explicitly "splits into two parts: clean product imagery that shows every faceted edge of the bottle, and lifestyle frames that place the scent in an energetic, real-world context"; a PDF carries "Preferred palettes, lighting notes, and framing suggestions" plus "the file types I need for print and web"; the client asks for "a quick preview" of the lighting set / first few frames before the full shoot to "confirm you're moving in the right direction"; tools named are Photoshop, Illustrator, Lightroom; budget $250–$750 USD. Deliverables verbatim: "High-resolution product shots (number as per brief)", "Lifestyle images matching the mood board cues", "Fully retouched files supplied as RAW + layered PSD + web-ready JPEG/PNG", "Rights cleared for unlimited global use".
ENRICHED (added by me, faithful to what the brief implies but not numerically stated in it): the exact frame count (8 hero/product + story crops), the exact pixel/format/dpi specs for each web + print deliverable, the named brand palette hexes and the "Monreale" hero color (deep ultramarine + brushed-gold accent), the specific wide-hero (16:9) and vertical-story (9:16) canvas sizes that "print and web" implies, the mood-board moodboard/Firefly board as the "quick preview" artifact, and the Adobe-Stock-licensed studio backdrop/prop set standing in for a physical shoot set (the connector cannot light a physical studio, so a rights-cleared Stock backdrop + generative expand/fill is the honest headless equivalent of "set your lighting and shoot"). Because the connector is headless and cannot run a camera, the "RAW" deliverable is honestly substituted with a high-bit-depth 16-bit master TIFF (documented as the RAW-equivalent master); the layered-PSD intent is honored via the master + per-region edit layers exported flat. The five flagged reference images become a single concatenated inspiration sheet used to drive the grade. Rights-cleared / unlimited-global-use is satisfied by licensing the backdrop through asset_license_and_download_stock (standard license).
spec.one_line_ask: Deliver a consistently-graded Monreale fragrance campaign: from one hero bottle frame, license a Stock studio backdrop, generative-expand the shot into a 16:9 wide hero and a 9:16 vertical-story canvas, generative-fill away reflections/dust and build the lifestyle scene, then run the whole 8-frame set through one Lightroom-grade tonal/preset chain so product and lifestyle frames match, exporting print-master + web-ready files plus a preview moodboard.
spec.full_brief:
Monreale is launching a new unisex fragrance line and needs a campaign image set that feels "bold, modern and instantly appealing to a younger, unisex crowd," built around contrast-heavy lighting, saturated color accents, and confident product placement (per the attached inspiration board and the five flagged reference frames). The work splits into two parts exactly as the client states: (1) clean PRODUCT imagery that shows every faceted edge of the faceted-glass bottle, and (2) LIFESTYLE frames that place the scent in an energetic, real-world context. You are given a single tight studio capture of the Monreale bottle on a plain sweep, a concatenated inspiration/mood sheet (the five flagged references), a brand sheet PDF with the palette, lighting notes and required file types, and a hand model/lifestyle plate. You must license a rights-cleared studio backdrop/prop set from Adobe Stock (standard license, unlimited global use), then use generative expand to extend the single bottle capture into a wide 16:9 hero canvas and a vertical 9:16 story canvas, and generative fill to remove the dust specks and the harsh reflection on the bottle's left facet and to integrate the bottle into the licensed backdrop and the lifestyle plate.

The campaign palette is deep ultramarine #1B2A6B as the hero ground, brushed-gold #C9A14A as the accent, and warm-neutral skin tones in the lifestyle frames; the look must read high-contrast with saturated blue/gold accents and crushed-but-clean shadows — the same grade must be applied identically to EVERY frame so the product and lifestyle images feel shot on one set. Deliver 8 frames total: 3 product hero frames (the bottle on the licensed backdrop), 2 wide 16:9 hero banners, 1 vertical 9:16 story frame, and 2 lifestyle frames (bottle in-hand / on a surface). Each frame ships as a print-master and a web-ready file. Before the full run, produce the client's requested "quick preview": a moodboard/concept board that pulls the graded first frames together with the references so the client can confirm the lighting direction.

Constraints: web-ready files are sRGB JPEG, long edge 2048 px, 72 dpi, quality ~85; print-masters are 16-bit TIFF, 300 dpi, A4-equivalent long edge (≈3508 px for the 16:9 hero, sized to spec per frame), color-managed; the 16:9 hero banners are exactly 3840×2160 (web) / scaled master, the 9:16 story is exactly 1080×1920 (web). All eight frames must share one preset + one auto-tone + the same exposure/contrast/HSL/temperature offsets (the grade is defined once and reused). Brand accents must survive the grade — the gold accent must not skew green and the ultramarine must not crush to black. The backdrop must be the licensed Stock asset (provide license proof), and rights must be cleared for unlimited global use.

inputs.name: monreale_bottle_studio_capture.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Professional product studio photograph of a single luxury unisex perfume bottle, heavy faceted clear-glass body with sharp geometric beveled edges, deep ultramarine-blue tinted fragrance visible through the glass, brushed-gold rectangular cap and a thin gold collar, minimal embossed sans-serif wordmark reading 'MONREALE' on the front facet. Shot tight on a seamless mid-grey paper sweep, single hard key light from upper left producing crisp specular highlights on the facets and one slightly-too-harsh blown reflection on the lower-left facet, faint real dust specks and two tiny lint fibers on the sweep near the base, subtle soft shadow pooled under the bottle. Razor-sharp focus on the glass edges, true-to-life refraction and caustics through the faceted glass, photographic micro-detail in the brushed-gold cap (fine machining lines), 8k, color-accurate, not glossy CGI, real studio capture with believable minor imperfections to be retouched. Centered with generous negative space, plain background, no props.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like a real raw studio frame, not a finished ad: include the deliberately harsh blown reflection on the lower-left facet and real dust/lint on the sweep so there is genuine retouching work for image_fill_area to remove. Faceted-glass refraction and gold machining lines are the believability anchors. Plain sweep + negative space is required so generative_expand has clean room to outpaint.

inputs.name: monreale_inspiration_sheet.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A single contact-sheet / mood board image, 3 rows by 2 columns of small framed reference tiles on a clean light-grey background, each tile a different fragrance-campaign mood: (1) high-contrast dark editorial product shot with a hard blue rim light, (2) saturated gold-and-cobalt color-block backdrop, (3) energetic street-lifestyle frame with motion and warm skin tones, (4) crushed-shadow moody bottle on stone, (5) bold geometric color accents behind a perfume, (6) youthful unisex hand holding a bottle in raking sunlight. Thin neat borders, faint handwritten-style annotation 'contrast • saturated accents • confident placement' along the bottom margin, the tiles in row1#2, row2#1 and row3#1 subtly marked with a small gold corner tag. Editorial mood-board aesthetic, realistic photographic tiles, no text inside the photos.
inputs.realism_notes: Stands in for the client's flagged five-reference inspiration board. Text-bearing/annotated layout => gemini-3-pro-image. The gold corner tags map to the client's 'five flagged reference images' and drive the target grade (contrast-heavy, saturated blue/gold). Used as a reference for the preset/grade decisions and pulled into the preview moodboard.

inputs.name: monreale_brand_sheet.pdf
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a one-page brand/spec sheet PDF for the 'Monreale' fragrance launch. Sections: (1) Palette — Hero Ultramarine #1B2A6B, Accent Brushed-Gold #C9A14A, Shadow Ink #0C1226, Warm Neutral Skin #E8C9A8, with hex swatches; (2) Lighting notes — 'hard key, high contrast, crushed but clean shadows, saturated blue/gold accents, no green skew on gold'; (3) Required file types & sizes — Web: sRGB JPEG, long edge 2048px, 72dpi, q85; Print master: 16-bit TIFF, 300dpi, color-managed; Wide hero 16:9 = 3840x2160 web; Vertical story 9:16 = 1080x1920 web; (4) Frame plan — 3 product hero, 2 wide 16:9 hero, 1 vertical 9:16 story, 2 lifestyle = 8 total; (5) Rights — unlimited global use, all stock standard-licensed. Clean minimal typographic layout, swatches as filled rectangles.
inputs.realism_notes: This is the brief's referenced PDF carrying 'preferred palettes, lighting notes, framing suggestions ... and the file types I need for print and web.' It is the contract the agent must read to pick exact dimensions, dpi, palette hexes and frame counts — a state-tracking anchor. Data/spec sheet => gemini-2.5-flash.

inputs.name: monreale_lifestyle_hand_plate.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Candid documentary lifestyle photograph: a young adult of ambiguous unisex styling holding out an open hand as if about to receive a perfume bottle, photographed in warm raking late-afternoon sunlight against an out-of-focus urban concrete-and-glass backdrop. Real human skin with visible pores, fine vellus hair on the forearm, natural knuckle creases, short clean unpolished nails, a couple of faint freckles, slightly uneven warm skin tone — NOT airbrushed. The hand is empty and open, palm angled up, leaving a clear bottle-sized gap in the composition. Shallow depth of field, authentic motion-candid feel, true photographic grain, correct five-fingered anatomy with natural finger spacing, no rings. Color: warm neutrals with cool city shadows. Shot like a real campaign B-roll still, not glossy CGI.
inputs.realism_notes: Real skin pores, forearm vellus hair, correct five-finger anatomy and an EMPTY open hand are mandatory so image_fill_area can composite the graded bottle into the gap believably. The empty bottle-sized gap is the deliberate hook for the fill step. Warm skin + cool shadow makes the consistent-grade test meaningful (gold must not green-skew on skin).

outputs.name: monreale_product_hero_01-03_master.tif
outputs.spec: 3 product-hero frames, bottle on the licensed Stock backdrop, 16-bit TIFF, 300 dpi, color-managed (sRGB/Adobe RGB tagged), long edge >=3508 px; identical grade applied to all 3.
outputs.rubric_hook: Are all 3 product masters 16-bit/300dpi, dust+lower-left-facet reflection fully removed, bottle cleanly seated on the licensed backdrop, and the grade visually identical across the three (same contrast/blue-gold accent)?

outputs.name: monreale_wide_hero_01-02.jpg
outputs.spec: 2 wide 16:9 hero banners, generative-expanded canvas, web JPEG sRGB exactly 3840x2160, 72 dpi, q~85; matching 300dpi TIFF master each.
outputs.rubric_hook: Are both exactly 3840x2160, is the outpainted region seamless (no seam/repeat artifacts at the original-frame boundary), does negative space carry brand ultramarine, and does the grade match the product frames?

outputs.name: monreale_story_vertical.jpg
outputs.spec: 1 vertical 9:16 story frame, generative-expanded from the bottle capture, web JPEG sRGB exactly 1080x1920, 72 dpi, q~85; matching TIFF master.
outputs.rubric_hook: Is it exactly 1080x1920, does the vertical outpaint keep the bottle correctly placed/scaled with believable extended backdrop, and does it carry the identical grade?

outputs.name: monreale_lifestyle_01-02.jpg
outputs.spec: 2 lifestyle frames (bottle composited into the hand plate / on a surface), web JPEG sRGB long edge 2048 px, 72 dpi, q~85; matching 16-bit TIFF masters at 300 dpi.
outputs.rubric_hook: Is the bottle composited into the empty hand gap with correct scale, lighting direction and a plausible contact shadow, skin still photoreal (pores/hair intact), and the SAME grade as the product set (gold not green-skewed on warm skin, ultramarine not crushed)?

outputs.name: monreale_grade_recipe.json
outputs.spec: A small JSON recording the single reusable grade: chosen preset name + auto_tone flag + exposure/contrast/highlights/shadows/temperature/HSL/vibrance offsets, so the same recipe is provably reused on all 8 frames.
outputs.rubric_hook: Does one explicit recipe exist and is it the same recipe applied to every one of the 8 frames (consistency is provable, not eyeballed)?

outputs.name: monreale_preview_moodboard
outputs.spec: Firefly concept board (deep-link) assembling the graded first frames + the inspiration sheet as the client's requested 'quick preview' before the full run.
outputs.rubric_hook: Does the preview board exist as a shareable board pulling the graded product frame(s) alongside the references, matching the client's explicit 'send me a quick preview' ask?

connector_workflow.output: brand_sheet_pages.png + extracted spec text (palette hexes, sizes, dpi, frame plan)
connector_workflow.note: Read the contract first: extract the exact palette hexes, the 3840x2160 / 1080x1920 / 2048px / 300dpi specs and the 8-frame plan that every later step must honor. Establishes the state the agent must track.

connector_workflow.output: candidate Stock backdrop/prop results (URNs + thumbnails)
connector_workflow.note: Search Adobe Stock for a high-contrast saturated blue/gold studio backdrop/prop set that matches the inspiration sheet mood and the palette from the brand sheet.

connector_workflow.output: licensed_backdrop.tif (standard license, rights-cleared) + license id
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. License + download the chosen backdrop so unlimited-global-use rights are cleared. This licensed file becomes the ground the bottle is seated on.

connector_workflow.output: upload session + presigned target for the bottle capture
connector_workflow.note: Begin moving the local raw bottle capture into CC storage so all generative/edit tools can act on it by URN.

connector_workflow.output: bottle_capture URN (in CC storage)
connector_workflow.note: Finalize the upload → stable URN for the bottle. This URN is the spine reused by nearly every downstream step.

connector_workflow.output: bottle_clean URN (dust + lint + harsh lower-left-facet reflection removed)
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. Generative fill to retouch the raw capture: remove the dust specks/lint on the sweep and tame the blown reflection on the lower-left facet. Cleaned bottle feeds expand + composite.

connector_workflow.output: wide_hero_raw_16x9 URN (bottle outpainted into a 16:9 canvas blended toward the licensed backdrop)
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. Outpaint the cleaned bottle into a wide 16:9 hero canvas, extending the licensed backdrop's saturated ground into the new negative space. First of two expand targets.

connector_workflow.output: available preset names
connector_workflow.note: List Lightroom-style presets so a single cinematic high-contrast preset matching the inspiration mood can be chosen and locked as the campaign grade.

connector_workflow.output: bottle_graded_base URN + chosen preset name (the grade seed)
connector_workflow.note: Apply the chosen preset to the cleaned product frame to DEFINE the look once. The preset name is recorded into monreale_grade_recipe.json and reused identically on every other frame (consistency contract).

connector_workflow.output: lifestyle_composited URN (graded bottle placed into the empty hand gap with a contact shadow)
connector_workflow.note: Second generative-fill use: composite the graded bottle into the empty bottle-sized gap in the hand plate, generating a plausible grip/contact shadow. Builds the lifestyle frame from the product frame.

connector_workflow.output: story_raw_9x16 URN (graded bottle outpainted to a 1080x1920-ratio vertical canvas)
connector_workflow.note: Second expand use: outpaint to the 9:16 vertical story ratio pulled from the brand sheet, extending backdrop above/below the bottle. Feeds the story export.

connector_workflow.output: wide_hero_autotoned URN
connector_workflow.note: Begin applying the SAME recipe to the wide-hero expand: auto-tone first, exactly as recorded in monreale_grade_recipe.json, so the outpainted hero matches the product base. (Same auto_tone+offset chain is conceptually applied to story_raw and lifestyle too.)

connector_workflow.output: grade-locked set: lifestyle + wide_hero + story all carrying identical HSL offsets
connector_workflow.note: Apply the recorded HSL offsets across the frames so the ultramarine stays saturated and the brushed-gold does NOT skew green on warm skin — the explicit consistency test across product + lifestyle + hero + story. Same offsets, multiple frames.

connector_workflow.output: monreale_wide_hero_01-02 (3840x2160), monreale_story_vertical (1080x1920), monreale_lifestyle_01-02 (2048px), product masters — exact-spec web JPEGs + 300dpi TIFF masters
connector_workflow.note: Resize/crop every graded frame to the EXACT brand-sheet dimensions and export both web JPEG (sRGB, q85) and 16-bit/300dpi TIFF master per frame. Exact-spec pressure: 3840x2160 and 1080x1920 must be pixel-exact.

connector_workflow.output: rendered previews of the finished graded frames
connector_workflow.note: Render previews of the exported frames to verify the grade reads consistently and dimensions/look are correct before the client preview is assembled.

connector_workflow.output: monreale_preview_moodboard (Firefly board deep-link)
connector_workflow.note: Assemble the graded base frame + finished previews alongside the inspiration sheet into the concept board that satisfies the client's explicit 'send me a quick preview to confirm direction' ask.

spec.iterative_chaining_note: The chain is a true output→input spine, not parallel one-shots. The single raw bottle capture is uploaded (step 4→5) to a URN that is consumed by the retouch (step 6), and the cleaned-bottle URN from step 6 is what feeds BOTH the 16:9 generative expand (step 7) AND the preset that defines the grade (step 9). The graded base URN from step 9 then forks into two new frames: it is composited into the lifestyle hand plate (step 10) and outpainted into the 9:16 story canvas (step 11). The licensed Stock backdrop from step 3 is fed into the expand (step 7) so the outpaint blends into real licensed ground. The preset NAME chosen at step 9 is recorded into monreale_grade_recipe.json and the SAME recipe (auto_tone step 12 + HSL offsets step 13) is replayed identically across the wide-hero, story and lifestyle frames — the consistency contract is enforced by reusing one recorded recipe, not by independently grading each frame. Finally the exact-spec exports (step 14) feed the previews (step 15), which feed the Firefly preview board (step 16) together with the original inspiration sheet. Every generative step (fill, expand, composite) consumes an earlier generated URN; nothing downstream regenerates from scratch.
spec.difficulty_rationale: Hard for an agent on several axes. (1) Long horizon: 16 dependent connector calls where a mistake early (wrong retouch, wrong preset) silently corrupts every downstream frame. (2) Heavy state tracking: the agent must read exact specs from the brand-sheet PDF (palette hexes, 3840x2160, 1080x1920, 2048px, 16-bit/300dpi, 8-frame plan) and hold them across the whole run; it must also track which URN is the cleaned bottle vs graded base vs each expanded/composited frame. (3) Asset reuse: the bottle capture is reused in 10 steps, the graded base forks into lifestyle + story, and one grade recipe must be provably reused on all 8 frames — independent re-grading is the obvious failure mode and is wrong. (4) Exact-spec pressure: the 16:9 and 9:16 outputs must be pixel-exact and dual-format (web JPEG + 16-bit TIFF master), with a color-fidelity trap (gold must not green-skew, ultramarine must not crush) that only shows up if the same recipe is honestly reused. (5) Tool-selection judgment: distinguishing when to fill vs expand vs composite, and remembering the stock license is what clears the brief's mandatory 'unlimited global use' rights. (6) It must also satisfy a soft deliverable — the client's explicit mid-run 'quick preview' — which an agent focused only on final files will forget.

issues: CONSISTENCY-CONTRACT NOT EXECUTED: The spec's central claim is 'one preset + one auto-tone + the same exposure/contrast/HSL/temperature offsets applied identically to all 8 frames,' and monreale_grade_recipe.json records exposure/contrast/highlights/shadows/temperature/HSL/vibrance offsets. But the connector_workflow only contains image_apply_preset (step 9, on the base only), image_apply_auto_tone (step 12, on the 16:9 hero only), and image_adjust_hsl (step 13). There is NO image_adjust_exposure, image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast, image_adjust_highlights, image_adjust_color_temperature, or image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation step anywhere. The recorded recipe references knobs the workflow never turns, so the 'provably reused recipe' is not actually provable from the steps.

AUTO-TONE COVERAGE GAP / HAND-WAVE: auto_tone is applied at step 12 to the wide-hero only; step 12's own note admits the same chain is just 'conceptually applied to story_raw and lifestyle too' and the 3 product-hero masters never get an explicit auto_tone step. The 'same auto-tone on every frame' contract is asserted in prose but not realized as distinct calls, undermining the rubric_hook that consistency is 'provable, not eyeballed.'

OUTPUT KIND MISLABELED: monreale_preview_moodboard is declared kind:'vector', but create_firefly_board produces a Firefly Boards deep-link (a board), not a vector artifact. Should be kind 'board'/'link' (or 'data'), not 'vector'.

INTERNAL SPEC INCONSISTENCY ON HERO MASTER SIZE: print-master spec says A4-equivalent long edge '≈3508 px for the 16:9 hero,' while the wide-hero output and step 14 describe a 3840x2160-derived 'scaled master.' 3508 != 3840, so the 16:9 print-master long-edge target is internally contradictory.

THREE PRODUCT-HERO FRAMES NOT BRANCHED: outputs promise 3 distinct product-hero frames (monreale_product_hero_01-03), but the workflow derives a single graded base (step 9) and never forks it into 3 differentiated product frames; how the 3 distinct frames arise (angle/crop variation) is unspecified.

DELIVERABLE-VS-STEP MATH: 8 frames x (TIFF master + web JPEG) = 16 image files plus recipe + board, all funneled through a single image_crop_and_resize step (14). spec.difficulty_rationale: This is hard for an agent on every axis the flagship bar cares about. (1) Long horizon: 19 ordered connector calls across three capability groups (express_template_track, asset_stock_storage, plus document/layout glue) with real output→input dependencies — the vector master from step 4 must be threaded through eight later steps, and losing track of it breaks the whole identity. (2) Heavy state tracking and asset reuse: one source artwork URN (step 2), one vector master (step 4), one type recommendation (step 5), and one palette (step 6) are each reused across multiple downstream steps; the agent must remember which filled-template output feeds the animation vs. the board vs. the preview. (3) Exact-spec pressure: the brief enumerates a precise deliverable matrix — primary + simplified icon, horizontal + stacked, B/W, light + dark, business-card, social-icon, scalable vector + transparent PNG — and the agent must map each to the right tool and size (3.5x2in card, 1:1 avatar, A4 letterhead, 1920x1080 then 1:1 intro) without inventing capabilities. (4) Constraint adherence under taste: the client's long list of forbidden directions (no literal/realistic eye, no occult/psychedelic/conspiracy/New-Age-cliché/all-seeing-eye, not loud/flashy/corporate/life-coach) means a naive agent that just generates a flashy mystical logo fails — the refine path (cutout→vectorize the EXISTING motif rather than concept-from-scratch) is mandated and easy to violate. (5) Tool discipline: it must use search_design/fill_text/animate_design/create_firefly_board correctly (fill_text is text-only, animate_design is Express-only) and keep 'local' glue out entirely. The combination of long chain, mandated reuse, a forbidden-aesthetic minefield, and a strict deliverable matrix makes both autonomous execution and human scoring demanding.
spec.full_brief:
Gateway Spiritualism is a spiritual/philosophical project (podcasting, writing, Substack articles, YouTube videos and public-facing content) centered on inner change, emotional honesty, and awakening from self-judgment and endless self-improvement. The client already has an existing logo/artwork concept (attached) but it reads as symbolic cover art, not a true brand identity. They explicitly do NOT want a new concept from scratch and do NOT want any piece of the original reused verbatim — instead, refine and distill the existing imagery into a professional, elegant, timeless logo and brand symbol that works cleanly across Substack, podcast profiles, YouTube, business cards, social media, and website branding. The core symbolism to PRESERVE is: gateway/doorway (must remain central), awakening, awareness/perception, transformation, and inner passage/transition. The eye-as-awareness idea may be used only if abstract, elegant, subtle and integrated into the gateway — never a literal/realistic eye, and never anything occult, psychedelic, conspiracy-oriented, fantasy/New-Age cliché, or 'all-seeing-eye.' The brand must feel thoughtful, emotionally intelligent, spiritually reflective, calm, elegant, minimalist, timeless, and quietly profound — and must NOT feel loud, flashy, corporate, 'life-coach,' mystical-fantasy, overly New Age, or visually busy. Visual style: clean lines, negative space, restrained elegance, symbolic simplicity, modern minimalist spirituality; the mark must stay recognizable and powerful at small sizes.

Deliverables requested by the client: (1) a refined primary logo; (2) a simplified icon/symbol version; (3) typography recommendations for 'Gateway Spiritualism'; (4) versions for light and dark backgrounds. File deliverables: fully scalable vector files (AI/SVG/EPS), transparent PNG files, horizontal and stacked logo variations, black-and-white versions, a social profile/icon version, a business-card-compatible version, typography/font recommendations, and logo files suitable for both web and print. Ownership: full commercial and proprietary rights to all final approved artwork plus editable source files. The client also asks: avoid stock icons and recycled logo templates; treat the attached artwork as inspiration/source rather than reusing it directly.

The long-horizon version asks the freelancer to take this through one continuous connector chain: ingest and clean the attached artwork, refine and vectorize the gateway mark into a single scalable master, recommend brand type and lock a restrained palette, then APPLY the refined identity onto real Express templates (business card, square social/profile icon, A4 letterhead) by filling the wordmark and mantra copy, render a full light/dark + horizontal/stacked + B&W variation sheet to PDF, animate a gentle 'doorway-opening' brand reveal (16:9 for YouTube plus a 1:1 reframe for podcast/Substack), and assemble everything — original-vs-refined mark, palette, type, applied templates, and the motion intro — into a single Firefly concept board for client review, with all finals organized in a delivery folder.
spec.grounding_note: REAL (verbatim from the client's fulldesc in db_all.json): the project name 'Gateway Spiritualism'; that it spans podcasting, writing, Substack, YouTube and public-facing content about inner change/emotional honesty/awakening; that an existing logo/artwork concept is attached and must be REFINED not replaced (and not reused piece-by-piece); the requirement that it work across Substack, podcast profiles, YouTube, business cards, social media, website branding; the core symbolism to preserve (gateway/doorway central, awakening, awareness/perception, transformation, inner passage); the eye guidance (abstract/elegant/subtle/integrated, NOT literal/occult/psychedelic/conspiracy/New-Age-cliché/all-seeing-eye); the overall feeling and the explicit 'do NOT feel' list; the visual style notes; small-size recognizability; the four numbered asks (refined primary logo, simplified icon, typography recommendations, light/dark versions); the full file-deliverable list (scalable vector AI/SVG/EPS, transparent PNG, horizontal + stacked, B&W, social profile/icon, business-card, web+print); the ownership/editable-source requirement; and the 'avoid stock icons/recycled templates' note. The listed source tools are Premiere Pro + Illustrator (consistent with a logo + a video intro). ENRICHED (implied, not literally stated): the specific brand mantra/tagline copy used in fill_text ('Awakening through the inner passage' is an illustrative placeholder the agent should set with the client); the exact animation spec (gentle doorway-opening reveal, ~5s, 1920x1080 plus a 1:1 reframe) — the brief implies YouTube/video use and lists Premiere Pro but does not specify a motion-logo, so the animated brand intro is an enrichment justified by the YouTube/podcast scope; the choice to APPLY the identity onto specific Express templates (business card, square avatar, A4 letterhead) and to present it on a Firefly concept board — the brief asks for a business-card-compatible and social-profile version and for deliverables suitable for review, so applied mockups and a single review board are a faithful long-horizon expansion rather than a new ask. The restrained palette (indigo/stone/off-white) is an illustrative interpretation of the stated 'calm/elegant/timeless' direction.

inputs.name: original_artwork_concept
inputs.gen_prompt: A symbolic spiritual 'cover-art' style illustration for a project called 'Gateway Spiritualism' — the kind of slightly busy, painterly concept art a client would have made first and now wants refined. Centered composition of an ancient stone doorway / archway opening onto a soft glowing dawn, with a very subtle abstract eye-like shape suggested in the negative space of the arch (NOT a literal realistic eye, NOT an all-seeing eye, no occult or conspiracy symbols). Muted contemplative palette of deep indigo, warm stone grey, and pale dawn light. Hand-illustrated texture, gentle gradients, a little visually busy and uneven on purpose so it reads as 'cover art that needs distilling into a real logo.' Square format, no text, no lettering anywhere in the image.
inputs.realism_notes: This is the client's ATTACHED source artwork, so it should look like an authentic first-pass concept/cover illustration — slightly over-designed, textured, painterly, NOT a clean finished logo. Deliberately imperfect/busy so the refine→vectorize chain has something real to distill. Absolutely no embedded text and no literal eye, to respect the brief's prohibitions.

inputs.name: brand_voice_notes
inputs.gen_prompt: Write a short JSON brand brief for 'Gateway Spiritualism' capturing the client's stated direction: an array of 'preserve' symbols (gateway/doorway central, awakening, awareness/perception, transformation, inner passage), a 'forbidden' array (literal realistic eye, occult, psychedelic, conspiracy, fantasy/New-Age cliché, all-seeing-eye, loud, flashy, corporate, life-coach, visually busy), a 'feeling' array (thoughtful, emotionally intelligent, spiritually reflective, calm, elegant, minimalist, timeless, quietly profound), a candidate tagline/mantra string, and a 'platforms' array (Substack, podcast, YouTube, business card, social, website). Keep it faithful to a calm minimalist spiritual brand; no marketing hype.
inputs.realism_notes: A small structured brief the agent uses to drive font_recommend, palette search, and the mantra copy in fill_text. Plain JSON, realistic field names, values lifted from the real client description so the agent has a single source of truth for tone and forbidden directions.

spec.iterative_chaining_note: The chain is genuinely output→input. The client's attached artwork is uploaded (1) and finalized to a URN (2); that URN is background-removed (3) to isolate the gateway motif; the cutout is vectorized (4) into gateway_mark.svg — the single master consumed by EIGHT later steps. font_recommend (5) and the palette search (6) read brand_voice_notes and produce type + palette tokens reused in the template fills and the variation sheet. Each search_design (7,9,11) returns an Express template immediately consumed by the matching fill_text (8,10,12); each fill_text consumes BOTH the chosen template AND the step-4 vector mark (plus step-5 type for text-bearing lockups). The filled horizontal lockup (12) plus the step-4 mark feed animate_design (15) → the 16:9 brand intro, which is itself the input to video_resize (16) for the 1:1 reframe. document_render_layout (13) consumes step-4 + step-5 + step-6 to build the variation matrix, which document_convert_pdf (14) turns into the usage PDF. asset_inline_preview (17) consumes the filled outputs (8,10,12) and the PDF (14) for board tiles. create_firefly_board (18) pulls the original URN (2), the vector master (4), palette (6), the three applied templates (8,10,12), and the animation (15) into ONE board — the convergence of every branch. asset_create_folders (19) gathers step-4, step-14, and both animation ratios into the delivery folder. The step-4 vector master threaded from creation to board to folder is the spine of the chain."
spec.one_line_ask: Refine the client's attached 'Gateway Spiritualism' cover-art into a clean, scalable minimalist logo system (primary, icon, horizontal/stacked, B&W, light/dark), apply it to real Express stationery and social templates, animate a gentle doorway-opening brand intro, and present the whole refined identity on a single Firefly concept board with an organized delivery folder.
