spec.difficulty_rationale: Long horizon (18 connector calls) across three target groups with hard output->input coupling: the device photo is retouched (fill), then outpainted, then cropped, then white-balanced, then preset-graded, and ALSO independently background-removed - six derivatives of one source that must stay consistent, and the agent must remember which URN feeds which page. State tracking is brutal: 6 input assets + 3 licensed Stock + ~10 intermediate URNs all live simultaneously, and the data-merge step must bind the RIGHT graded image and the RIGHT cut-out to each of 6 templated pages while reading spec rows from a structured JSON. Exact-spec pressure is everywhere - A4 + 3mm bleed + 300dpi + CMYK for press but a separate RGB/150dpi digital derivative, exactly 8 pages in a fixed running order (cover, 6 named interiors, closing), and a European medical-device visual register the judge can score against named comparators (Zimmer, Storz, BTL, Chattanooga). The agent must also correctly split work: bespoke cover/closing via render_layout vs. the repeatable 6 interiors via data-merge - choosing the wrong tool for a page is a real failure mode. Finally, the fill step is generative and must actually remove cables without leaving artifacts that survive into the print PDF, so an early pixel error propagates through nine downstream steps.
spec.full_brief:
Biotech - a manufacturer with 32 years in rehabilitation equipment - is launching RADIVA, a next-generation Solid-State Radial Pressure Wave Therapy (RPWT) system, and needs a premium ~8-page product brochure that positions it against leading medical-device brands (Zimmer, Storz Medical, BTL, Chattanooga). This is explicitly NOT a generic product catalog: the brochure must communicate Innovation, Intelligence, Clinical Confidence and Premium Technology, with a clean, modern, minimal, technology-driven European medical-device aesthetic. RADIVA's selling points must be surfaced as structured spec/feature content: Solid-State Electromagnetic Technology, Soft Shock Technology (patient comfort), the BIOS (Biotech Operating System) running an RTOS on a quad-core 64-bit ARM processor, a 10-inch capacitive touchscreen, a built-in clinical protocol library with user-created and QuickLink protocols, and real-time parameter adjustment during treatment.

The brochure has a fixed 8-page running order: (1) Cover, (2) RPWT Overview, (3) Solid-State Technology, (4) Soft Shock Technology, (5) BIOS Software Platform, (6) Intelligent Clinical Workflow, (7) Real-Time Treatment Control, (8) Closing / Company Experience. The client provides raw product photographs, software screenshots, the company logo, and detailed content; the freelancer must source any supporting clinical/lifestyle imagery, retouch the device photos to a clean studio standard (the raw hero has visible cables and desk clutter that must be removed), and lay out the spec tables and feature pages from the supplied content so every interior page is visually consistent.

Constraints: A4 portrait (210x297 mm) with 3 mm bleed and crop marks; print master at 300 dpi, CMYK, press-ready; a second screen-optimized digital PDF at 150 dpi, RGB, hyperlinked; a single cohesive image grade (cool, high-key, low-saturation) across both client-shot and Stock-sourced imagery; brand-appropriate European-medical typography; and exactly 8 pages in the order above. Deliverables (per the original brief) are the print-ready PDF and the digital PDF, plus an asset/concept board capturing the retouch-to-final provenance for client sign-off.
spec.grounding_note: REAL (verbatim from the Freelancer.com brief in db_all.json): client is Biotech; product is RADIVA - Solid-State Radial Pressure Wave Therapy System; the comparator brands Zimmer, Storz Medical, BTL, Chattanooga; the explicit ~8-page scope with the exact page list (Cover, RPWT Overview, Solid-State Technology, Soft Shock Technology, BIOS Software Platform, Intelligent Clinical Workflow, Real-Time Treatment Control, Closing/Company Experience); the named features (Solid-State Electromagnetic, Soft Shock, BIOS/RTOS, quad-core 64-bit ARM, 10-inch capacitive touchscreen, clinical protocol library, user-created & QuickLink protocols, real-time parameter adjustment); '32 years' of Biotech experience; the requested style words (Premium, Modern, Minimal, Technology-driven, European medical device aesthetic); the perception goals (Innovation, Intelligence, Clinical Confidence, Premium Technology); the deliverables list including 'Print-ready PDF' and 'Digital PDF'; and the client statement that they will PROVIDE product photographs, software screenshots, company logo, reference brochures and content. ENRICHED (implied, not stated verbatim): exact print specs (A4 210x297mm, 3mm bleed, 300dpi CMYK for press vs 150dpi RGB digital) - the brief says 'print-ready' + 'digital' but not the numbers, so I fixed industry-standard values to make the task scorable; the specific cable/clutter retouch on the hero photo (the brief's 'premium/minimal' + 'we will provide product photographs' strongly implies raw shots needing cleanup, but the exact clutter is invented for the task); the use of licensed Stock for clinical/lifestyle imagery (the brief supplies device/UI assets but a premium clinical brochure needs human-in-context shots, so sourcing them is implied not stated); the structured brochure_content.json and brand_kit.json as concrete files (the brief promises 'detailed content and marketing guidance' and brand rules but not a JSON schema); and the Firefly concept board for sign-off (a reasonable pro deliverable, not requested). The original also asks for editable source files (AI/PSD/INDD) which the headless connector cannot emit; that deliverable is intentionally dropped per the capability sheet's note that desktop .indd data-merge is not headless.

inputs.name: device_hero_photo.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal studio product photograph of a next-generation radial pressure-wave physiotherapy console: a sleek matte-white floor-standing medical device about 1.2m tall on castors, with a 10-inch glossy capacitive touchscreen tilted toward the viewer showing a faint teal UI, a coiled hand-applicator resting in a side cradle. Shot on a seamless light-grey studio backdrop with soft large-softbox lighting and a subtle floor reflection. CRUCIALLY include realistic clutter that a retoucher would remove: a tangled black coiled power cable trailing across the floor to a wall socket, a loose grey applicator cord draped over the front panel, and a couple of stray desk items (a clipboard and a coffee cup) at the edge of frame. Real material textures - fine brushed-aluminium trim, faint dust and fingerprints on the white plastic, accurate screen glare and subtle subsurface plastic sheen. Neutral daylight white balance, sharp focus, no text on the device body, no logos. Documentary product-shot realism, not a glossy CGI render.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like a real raw studio capture WITH removable clutter (cables, cord, clipboard, cup) so image_fill_area has genuine objects to erase; include authentic plastic imperfections, screen glare and a real soft floor shadow so the retouch is verifiable. Avoid CGI-perfect surfaces.

inputs.name: screenshot_bios_ui.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A crisp, legible flat-UI screenshot of a medical-device operating system home screen called 'BIOS - Biotech Operating System' on a 10-inch capacitive touchscreen, 1280x800, dark-teal-on-near-black theme with clean white sans-serif labels. Show a top status bar (battery, RTOS clock, connectivity), a central grid of large rounded tiles labeled 'Clinical Protocols', 'QuickLink', 'User Protocols', 'Real-Time Control', 'Patient History', 'Settings', and a bottom dock. Subtle teal accent (#16a0a0), realistic soft inner shadows and 1px hairlines, faint glass reflection overlay at top. Text must be sharp and correctly spelled. No photographic background - pure UI capture.
inputs.realism_notes: Text-bearing UI, so use gemini-3-pro-image for crisp legible labels. Must read as a real device screenshot (status bar, dock, hairlines, glass glare) so it can sit on the BIOS Software Platform page at print resolution.

inputs.name: screenshot_protocol_library.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A clean, legible UI screenshot of a 'Clinical Protocol Library' screen for a physiotherapy device, 1280x800, same dark-teal theme as the BIOS home screen. A scrollable list of named treatment protocols (e.g. 'Plantar Fasciitis', 'Lateral Epicondylitis', 'Achilles Tendinopathy', 'Calcific Shoulder', 'Trigger Points') each row showing energy mJ, frequency Hz, pulse count, and a small QuickLink star. A right-hand panel previews live parameters with a teal value slider and an 'Adjust during treatment' toggle. Sharp correctly-spelled medical text, 1px separators, soft teal highlights. Pure UI, no photographic background.
inputs.realism_notes: Second text-bearing screenshot for the Intelligent Clinical Workflow / Real-Time Control pages; needs accurate medical protocol names and numeric parameter columns that survive at 300dpi. gemini-3-pro-image for legibility.

inputs.name: radiva_logo.svg
inputs.gen_prompt: A minimal, premium wordmark logo for 'RADIVA' in a geometric grotesque uppercase, tight tracking, deep teal (#0f7d7d) with a single subtle radial-wave glyph (three concentric arcs emanating from the 'A') as the mark. Flat, two-color, no gradients, plenty of clear space, suitable as a vector. Render on pure white, crisp edges, centered. European medical-device brand feel - confident, clean, restrained.
inputs.realism_notes: Provided by client as vector; generate as a clean two-color logo so placement reads cleanly. Must look like a real corporate medical wordmark, not clip-art. Delivered as SVG; if generated raster, vectorize before use.

inputs.name: brand_kit.json
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a realistic brand-kit JSON for a premium medical-device brand 'RADIVA' by Biotech: palette (primary teal #0f7d7d, accent #16a0a0, ink #0b1f24, paper #f5f8f8, plus a warning amber for clinical callouts), typography intent (heading: geometric grotesque; body: humanist sans; numerals: tabular), spacing scale, allowed logo clear-space, and tone words (premium, minimal, clinical-confident, European). Include print constraints: CMYK build of the teal, minimum body size 8.5pt, max 2 accent colors per page. Valid JSON, realistic field names.
inputs.realism_notes: Stands in for the brief's 'brand rules / marketing guidance'. Must be valid, internally consistent JSON the layout/merge steps and font_recommend can consume.

inputs.name: brochure_content.json
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a structured content JSON for an 8-page RADIVA medical-device brochure. Top-level 'pages' array in the exact order: cover, rpwt_overview, solid_state_technology, soft_shock_technology, bios_software_platform, intelligent_clinical_workflow, real_time_treatment_control, closing_company_experience. Each interior page object has: title, subtitle, body_paragraphs[], a feature_rows[] array (each {feature, benefit}), and a spec_table[] array (each {label, value, unit}) - populate spec_table with real plausible numbers: processor 'Quad-core 64-bit ARM', OS 'BIOS (RTOS)', display '10-inch capacitive touchscreen', energy range '0.05-0.68 mJ/mm2', frequency '1-22 Hz', protocol library count '120+ protocols', QuickLink slots, weight, dimensions, power. Cover has title 'RADIVA', tagline, and the line 'Solid-State Radial Pressure Wave Therapy System'. Closing page has the Biotech '32 years of rehabilitation equipment manufacturing' company story and a contact block. Valid JSON with consistent keys across pages so a data-merge template can iterate it.
inputs.realism_notes: This is the data-merge backbone. Field names MUST be uniform across the 6 interior pages so document_merge_data_layout can iterate one template over them; spec numbers should be plausible for a real RPWT device and match the screenshots' theme.

spec.iterative_chaining_note: The device hero photo is the spine of a long output->input chain: device_hero_photo.jpg -> (step5 select mask) -> (step6 image_fill_area removes cables/clutter = device_clean_v1) -> (step7 generative_expand to portrait = wide hero) -> (step8 crop_and_resize to A4+bleed) -> (step9 color_temperature) -> (step10 apply_preset) -> consumed by BOTH the step13 cover render AND the step14 data-merge. In parallel, device_clean_v1 (step6) is ALSO fed to step11 image_remove_background to make the floating device cut-out, which step14 binds onto the Solid-State Technology page - so one fill output forks into two distinct downstream uses. The 3 licensed Stock images (step4) ride the same grade pipeline (step8->9->10) so they color-match the device, then land in specific merged pages. brochure_content.json is read at step2, then drives the cover copy (step13), all 6 interior spec/feature pages (step14) and the closing story (step15); brand_kit.json drives font_recommend (step12) whose output is consumed by every render/merge step (13,14,15). Finally the three rendered PDFs (steps 13,14,15) are concatenated by step16 into the 8-page press master, which step17 down-converts into the digital PDF, and step18 pins the whole raw->retouched->graded->laid-out->final trail onto a Firefly board. No step invents pixels from nothing - every later step consumes a concrete earlier URN.

FIREFLY BOARD INPUTS INCLUDE PDFs: Step 18 create_firefly_board lists step 13 (cover_page.pdf) and step 16 (RADIVA_brochure_PRESS.pdf) as pinned inputs. spec.difficulty_rationale: Hard on every long-horizon axis. Horizon and state: 18 ordered calls across 5 groups (asset plumbing, photo, express-template, document/data-merge, video/audio) with strict ordering — the data-merge in step 10 is only valid if the RSVP master was correctly filled in step 9, which depends on the right template chosen in step 6 and the gold monogram from step 4, which depends on the cutout in step 3 and the upload in steps 1-2; one mis-tracked URN or template ID poisons the whole downstream chain. Asset reuse: four uploaded URNs and several intermediates (gold monogram, toned portrait, three filled cards) are each consumed by 2-4 later steps, so the agent must hold a growing dependency graph. Exact-spec pressure: mockups exactly 2700x2025 px, demo 20-30s and square, guide 1-2pp PDF, data-merge emits exactly one card per CSV row with correct field mapping — all gradable and easy to get subtly wrong. Cross-asset consistency: couple names, wedding date, and the burgundy/cream/gold palette must stay identical across Save-the-Date, details card, RSVP master, merged variants, mockups, and buyer's guide. Tool-discipline traps: fill_text is text-only (restyling in it fails), data-merge needs a connector-renderable template not a desktop file, and the only legitimate 'local' step is the final A/V mux. The must-exercise quartet sits at four different depths of the chain, so the agent cannot shortcut to them.
spec.full_brief:
A solo Etsy seller is launching a digital-products shop selling 'Save the Date' wedding-website template kits, and needs a complete, sellable listing package for the first SKU in an 'Old Money / Luxury Wedding' style. The fixed palette is dark burgundy, cream/ivory, and gold accents with lace-detail textures; the overall feel must read high-end and editorial, never basic or generic. The kit centers on a 7-page wedding-website template (Hero/Monogram with couple names + wedding date; Envelope Reveal; Save the Date; Wedding Details with venue/time/dress code; Our Story with photo + timeline; RSVP form; live Countdown Timer) that buyers receive as an editable share link and that must work on mobile. The seller has nine more listings ready to assign if the first is strong, so consistency and a reusable, personalizable system matter more than one-off art.

The agent is given the seller's raw source assets — a couple engagement portrait, a hand-drawn gold monogram crest, a guest/RSVP data file, a screen recording of the template scrolling through all pages, and a short raw voiceover clip — and must produce the full listing package faithful to the real brief: (a) the personalized stationery set that fronts the kit (Save-the-Date, Wedding Details, and an RSVP card), built by searching the matching Express templates and filling the 'old money' couple-name/date placeholders, plus a data-merged batch of one personalized RSVP/guest card per row of the provided guest list; (b) 8-10 Etsy listing mockup images at exactly 2700 x 2025 px (JPG/PNG) showing phone-frame mockups of the template pages on a dark-burgundy background with feature badges (RSVP, Countdown Timer, Demo Link) and close-up detail shots; (c) a 20-30s premium MP4 demo video of the template scrolling through all pages with smooth transitions (silent per the brief, plus an enriched narrated alternate using the cleaned voiceover); and (d) a 1-2 page luxury PDF Buyer's Guide explaining how to access, edit, and share the Canva template, designed to match the kit aesthetic.

Hard constraints: keep the burgundy/cream/gold palette and the couple's names + wedding date identical across every artifact; mockups exactly 2700 x 2025 px; demo video 20-30 seconds, MP4, premium product-preview quality with no required voiceover; buyer's guide 1-2 pages PDF in the matching luxury aesthetic; the merged RSVP set must contain exactly one personalized card per guest row. All text personalization must be done through the template-fill path (text-only, layout preserved), and only the final audio/video mux may fall back to local glue.
spec.grounding_note: REAL (verbatim from the client's full description in db_all.json, line 921): the seller is launching an Etsy shop selling Canva 'Save the Date Wedding Website Templates'; needs a complete first-listing package with 9 more listings to follow; style = Old Money / Luxury Wedding; palette = dark burgundy, cream/ivory, gold accents, lace details; the 7-page template spec (Hero/Monogram with couple names+date, Envelope Reveal, Save the Date, Wedding Details with venue/time/dress code, Our Story photo+timeline, RSVP Form, live Countdown Timer); deliverables 1-4 verbatim: Canva editable template link, 8-10 Etsy mockup images at exactly 2700x2025 px (JPG/PNG) with phone-frame mockups, dark-burgundy background, feature badges (RSVP/Countdown/Demo Link) and close-up detail shots; a 20-30s MP4 demo video that is a screen recording scrolling all pages with smooth transitions and NO required voiceover, premium product-preview feel; and a 1-2 page PDF buyer's guide (how to access/edit/share the template) matching the luxury aesthetic; constraints 'mobile view' and 'clean luxury high-end'. ENRICHED (added to make it a long-horizon connector task, genuinely implied by the brief): the specific provided source assets (engagement portrait, hand-drawn monogram crest, guest/RSVP CSV, template-scroll screen recording, raw VO clip) — the brief references sharing references privately and a screen recording, so these inputs are plausible; the data-merge of one RSVP card per guest row (the brief's RSVP Form implies guest personalization, and a sellable kit demos with sample guests); rendering the stationery set as Express templates + data-merge rather than literally in the Canva Website editor (the connector cannot build a live Canva Website with a countdown timer headless — so the kit's print/stationery front-end is produced via the Adobe connector while the live-website + share-link delivery remains the human's Canva step); and the optional NARRATED alternate of the demo video using media_enhance_speech (the brief says no VO is required, so the cleaned-VO cut is explicitly an enrichment, not a replacement for the silent deliverable). The Canva-native editable share link and the live countdown-timer page are acknowledged as out-of-scope for headless connector execution and left as the human handoff.

inputs.name: couple_portrait_engagement.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Candid documentary-style engagement portrait of a real young couple standing close together outdoors at golden hour in front of a soft out-of-focus old-money estate garden (boxwood hedges, stone urn). Natural editorial photography, NOT glossy CGI. Show real skin pores, fine facial hair and individual flyaway hair strands catching the backlight, slightly asymmetric genuine soft smiles, natural skin texture with a faint blush, correct anatomically accurate hands (the woman's hand resting on the man's lapel, all five fingers visible and correct). She wears a cream silk slip dress, he wears a muted burgundy knit; subtle gold jewelry. Soft 85mm f/1.8 shallow depth of field, warm muted color, gentle film grain, slight lens vignette. Realistic mixed natural light with catchlights in the eyes. Frame with headroom and side space so it can be cropped to portrait and landscape.
inputs.realism_notes: Must survive a luxury-editorial preset and a close-up crop on the 'Our Story' page; the failure modes to avoid are plastic skin, fused/extra fingers, and over-saturated HDR look. Real pores, flyaways, correct hands, and a candid (not posed-studio) feel are load-bearing because the rubric scores whether the portrait reads premium-editorial.

inputs.name: couple_monogram_sketch.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A single elegant interlocking two-letter wedding monogram crest ('E & T') hand-drawn in fine gold ink on warm cream textured paper, old-money heraldic style with a thin laurel/lace border and a small ampersand at center. Slightly imperfect hand-inked line weight, faint paper grain and a soft natural shadow where the paper isn't perfectly flat. Centered, generous margin, photographed flat top-down under soft window light so the background is a clean but real cream paper (not pure white), enabling clean background removal.
inputs.realism_notes: This is text-bearing line art, so use the text/logo model. The cream paper (not pure white) and a real soft shadow make background removal a genuine step rather than trivial; the thin gold line work must stay crisp after cutout and color-overlay so it reads as a luxury crest at small mockup sizes.

inputs.name: guest_rsvp_data.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a realistic 14-row CSV of sample wedding guests for an RSVP/save-the-date data merge. Columns: guest_full_name, salutation, party_size, table_or_group, meal_choice, rsvp_by_date, personal_note. Use varied realistic names across cultures, party_size 1-4, meal_choice in {Beef, Halibut, Wild Mushroom Risotto (V)}, all rsvp_by_date = 'September 12, 2026', short warm personal_note per guest. Keep one consistent couple/wedding context (couple 'Eleanor & Theodore', wedding date 'October 18, 2026') referenced in a header comment so merged cards stay consistent with the filled templates.
inputs.realism_notes: Field names and value domains must be clean enough to map deterministically in document_merge_data_vector; exactly 14 rows so the grader can check the merge emits exactly 14 personalized cards. Couple names/date must match the strings used in fill_text steps for cross-artifact consistency.

inputs.name: template_scroll_screencap.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: A clean 1080p screen-recording-style clip of a finger smoothly scrolling top-to-bottom through a luxury 'old money' wedding website on a phone: dark burgundy and cream pages with gold serif monogram, a Save-the-Date page, wedding details, an Our Story timeline with a couple photo, an RSVP form, and a live countdown timer ticking. Smooth, steady scroll, soft UI motion, premium and elegant, realistic phone screen with subtle reflections, no harsh cuts. Native 1080p, slow deliberate pacing, ~12 seconds of usable scroll.
inputs.realism_notes: Use full veo-3.1 at native 1080p (never veo-fast/720p) so the source survives a square reframe and quick-cut without looking soft. It is the raw footage the demo video is built from; smooth continuous scroll matters because the brief demands 'smooth transitions, premium product preview'.

inputs.name: listing_demo_vo_raw.wav
inputs.gen_prompt: A warm, unhurried female voiceover, ~18 seconds, reading: 'Meet the Eleanor suite — a luxury, old-money wedding website template you can personalize in minutes. Seven elegant pages, a live countdown, and a built-in RSVP — all yours to edit in Canva, free.' Natural, premium, boutique-brand tone. Render clean at 48 kHz; include a faint amount of real room ambience and a couple of natural breaths so the speech-enhancement step has something genuine to clean up.
inputs.realism_notes: Per section D this is NOT an audio-repair task, so generate a clean 48 kHz voice — the light room tone/breaths are just enough for media_enhance_speech to demonstrably improve, not a deliberately roughened clip. Script names the couple ('Eleanor') to stay consistent with the templates and CSV.

spec.iterative_chaining_note: The workflow is a genuine output→input dependency graph. Steps 1-2 upload the four provided assets and mint URNs that seed everything downstream. The monogram chain is deepest: step-2 URN → step 3 background removal (cutout) → step 4 gold color-overlay (brand-normalized crest), and that single step-4 artifact is consumed four more times (step 9 RSVP master, step 11 buyer's guide, step 14 mockups, and indirectly the merged cards via step 10). The stationery chain: step 6 search_design returns the template IDs that feed three separate fill_text calls (steps 7, 8, 9); step 9's filled RSVP master becomes the literal template argument to the step 10 data-merge, which also pulls the step-2 CSV URN to emit one card per guest row. The document chain: filled cards (7,8), toned portrait (step 5, derived from step 2), and gold monogram (step 4) feed step 11's buyer's-guide layout, which step 12 converts to the delivered PDF. The A/V chain: step-2 VO URN → step 13 enhance-speech → muxed in step 18; the step-2 screencap → step 15 quick-cut (intercut with step 14 mockups) → step 16 square resize → both step 17 (summary→listing copy) and step 18 (narrated alternate). Every must-exercise tool sits on a real chain: search_design (6)→fill_text (7-9)→document_merge_data_vector (10); media_enhance_speech (13)→final mux (18).
spec.one_line_ask: Deliver a complete, on-brand 'Old Money' Etsy listing package for a Canva wedding-website template kit: a personalized Save-the-Date / Wedding-Details / RSVP stationery set with a data-merged one-card-per-guest RSVP batch, 8-10 mockup images at exactly 2700x2025 px, a 20-30s premium MP4 demo video (silent plus a cleaned-VO narrated alternate), and a 1-2 page luxury PDF buyer's guide — all sharing one burgundy/cream/gold identity.

outputs.name: etsy_mockups[]

outputs.name: demo_sizzle_etsy_1x1.mp4
outputs.spec: MP4, 1080x1080 square, 20-30 seconds, premium product-preview cut of the template scrolling all pages with smooth transitions, NO voiceover (silent deliverable per the brief).

spec.title: Canva Wedding Website Template Designer — Full Etsy Listing Package (Old Money Style)

issues: TOOL-DISCIPLINE VIOLATION (step 9, major): the step routes the step-4 gold monogram image INTO fill_text ('Fill the RSVP card master... and place the gold monogram from step 4'). Capability sheet line 48 is explicit that fill_text is 'TEXT ONLY - no font/size/color/align change; preserves layout' - it fills placeholder TEXT, it cannot insert/place an image element. This is exactly the trap the spec's own difficulty_rationale brags about ('restyling in it fails'), yet the spec commits it. The step-4 -> step-9 image dependency is not consumable by fill_text.

CROSS-TRACK HANDOFF UNPROVEN (step 9 -> step 10, major): step 9 produces a filled EXPRESS design (search_design + fill_text track, section A3), but step 10 feeds it as the 'vector template' to document_merge_data_vector (section A2). The sheet's data-merge note (lines 42-43) says merge 'needs a connector-renderable template spec (NOT a desktop .indd)' and is 'Headless-proven via document_render_layout/vector loops' - it ties data-merge to the render-vector/layout path, NOT to an Express fill_text design ID. An Express design handle is not an established document_merge_data_vector template input, so the 14-card merge (the core graded deliverable) sits on an unproven cross-track bridge.

DURATION MATH IMPOSSIBLE (step 15, major): the source screencap gen_prompt yields '~12 seconds of usable scroll', but step 15 demands a '~24s' output (20-30s window) via video_create_quick_cut. quick_cut is a highlight/SHORTENING tool (line 66: 'AI highlight/sizzle reel', takes target_duration to cut a longer clip DOWN) - it cannot lengthen 12s of source to 24s. The step-14 mockups are STILL images (document_render_vector specs), and quick_cut takes video assetIds, so intercutting stills cannot pad runtime either. The 20-30s deliverable is unreachable from the declared inputs.

WRONG/UNDER-SPECIFIED TOOL FOR PIXEL-EXACT RASTER (step 14, minor): the 2700x2025 mockup set (the most heavily graded, exact-pixel deliverable) is produced by document_render_vector, which the sheet describes as 'render a vector/spec -> print artwork' (line 37). A vector-render tool's native output is a spec/vector with no guaranteed exact-pixel raster export; the pixel-exact tool on the sheet is image_crop_and_resize. As written the step does not lock the exact 2700x2025 JPG/PNG dimension.

MINOR INCONSISTENCY (step 14): the step output label hardcodes '8 burgundy phone-frame mockup composition specs' while the note body and the etsy_mockups[] deliverable spec require 8-10 images. Within range but the fixed '8' label is sloppy.

MINOR (grounding_note wording): claims 'verbatim from the client's full description in db_all.json, line 921'. db_all.json is single-line JSON (0 newlines); the record is actually at array index 3795. The brief content IS genuinely real and verbatim, but the 'line 921' citation is inaccurate and unverifiable as written.
fixes: Step 9: remove the image-placement responsibility from fill_text. Keep step 9 as a TEXT-ONLY fill of the couple-name placeholders on the RSVP master. Move the gold-monogram placement to a render step (document_render_vector/layout) that composites the step-4 monogram onto the filled card, OR have search_design pick a template whose monogram slot is itself a fillable image asset placed during the render, not during fill_text.

Step 9->10: make the merge template a connector-renderable vector/layout spec, not a raw Express design ID. Insert (or repurpose step 14's) document_render_vector to emit the RSVP master as a renderable vector template with <<field>> placeholders, then feed THAT to document_merge_data_vector. This matches the sheet's 'document_render_layout/vector loops' headless-proven path and keeps the 14-card merge valid.

Step 15: fix the duration source. Either (a) regenerate template_scroll_screencap.mp4 with >=30s of usable scroll so quick_cut can trim to a 20-30s cut, or (b) keep ~12s scroll but build the 20-30s demo by concatenating/animating multiple scroll passes + mockup b-roll via a path that can LENGTHEN (e.g., animate_design on the Express deck, or a local concat in the already-allowed final step), since quick_cut alone cannot upscale 12s to 24s.

Step 14: add an explicit pixel-lock. After document_render_vector composes each mockup, route through image_crop_and_resize to force EXACT 2700x2025 px JPG/PNG output, so the most-graded dimension constraint is guaranteed rather than left to a vector renderer.
Step 14: change the output label from '8 ... specs' to '8-10 ...' to match the deliverable's 8-10 range and the close-up-detail-shot requirement.

grounding_note: correct the citation to reference the real location (db_all.json array index 3795, title 'Canva Wedding Website Template Designer - Full Etsy Listing Package (Old Money Style)') instead of the nonexistent 'line 921', since the file is single-line JSON.

connector_workflow.output: lecture_summary.json — a structured transcript+scene summary of the reference Arabic algebra lecture (the solving steps, the pedagogical order, the check-the-answer beat)
connector_workflow.note: Spine step 1: summarize the supplied reference lecture so the agent can extract the exact 5-beat solve structure (problem → isolate variable → divide coefficient → state x → substitute-and-check) that the script and on-screen scenes must follow. Output drives the scene copy in steps 6–9 and the SRT text in step 17.

connector_workflow.output: upload_session + presigned targets for the 4 supplied source assets
connector_workflow.note: Plumbing: open an upload session so the raw GCC-accent VO, the reference lecture, the live-action boy clip, and the background plate all live in CC storage as URNs that later steps can reference. Consumes the input filenames; emits a session handle used in step 3.

connector_workflow.output: 4 CC asset URNs (vo_raw_urn, lecture_urn, boy_urn, bg_urn)
connector_workflow.note: Finalize the uploads from step 2 → stable URNs. media_summarize in step 1 used the lecture directly; from here every media/template step references these URNs instead of local paths.

connector_workflow.output: lesson_frame_template_id — an Express animated-explainer / lesson-frame template (16:9, large central equation zone, side call-out lane, RTL-friendly)
connector_workflow.note: Express track begins. Search for a clean educational lesson-frame template whose layout matches the 5 solve beats surfaced in step 1 (a big equation slot + a step-callout strip). The chosen template_id is reused as the base for every scene fill in steps 6–9.

connector_workflow.output: arabic_font_choice — a recommended Arabic-script display+body font pairing legible at 1080p for equations and call-outs
connector_workflow.note: Pick brand/legibility-appropriate Arabic fonts for the lesson frame so equation glyphs and RTL call-out text render cleanly. Informs the fill_text scenes (6–9) and keeps typographic consistency across all animated scenes (acceptance criterion #2).

connector_workflow.output: scene1_filled — template populated with Scene 1 Arabic copy (the problem statement: ٢س + ٣ = ١١ and the title)
connector_workflow.note: Fill the chosen template (step 4) with Scene-1 Arabic equation/step copy taken from the step-1 lecture structure, in the step-5 font. fill_text is TEXT-ONLY so layout is preserved. This filled design is the input to animate_design in step 10.

connector_workflow.output: scene2_filled — Scene 2 copy (isolate the variable: subtract 3 from both sides → ٢س = ٨), with the move-the-constant call-out
connector_workflow.note: Second scene fill on the same base template, advancing the solve one beat. Reuses template_id (4) and font (5); copy sourced from the step-1 summary. Feeds animate_design step 11.

connector_workflow.output: scene3_filled — Scene 3 copy (divide by the coefficient: ٢س ÷ ٢ = ٨ ÷ ٢ → س = ٤)
connector_workflow.note: Third scene fill, the divide-coefficient beat. Same template/font reuse, copy from step 1. Feeds animate_design step 12.

connector_workflow.output: scene4_filled — Scene 4 copy (substitute & check: ٢(٤)+٣ = ١١ ✓ — the answer-verification beat)
connector_workflow.note: Fourth scene fill, the substitute-and-check beat that satisfies acceptance criterion #3 (learner can solve AND check). Same template/font; copy from step 1. Feeds animate_design step 13.

connector_workflow.output: scene1_anim.mp4 — animated Scene 1 (title + problem fade/slide-in, equation zoom-in), 1080p, ~12s
connector_workflow.note: Motion build for Scene 1 from the filled design (6). animate_design turns the static Express scene into a motion-graphics clip. Output is a scene clip consumed by the assembly in step 14.

connector_workflow.output: scene2_anim.mp4 — animated Scene 2 (the −3 call-out slides in, terms reflow), 1080p, ~14s
connector_workflow.note: Motion build for Scene 2 from filled design (7). Animation style must match scene 1 (criterion #2: no style inconsistency). Consumed in step 14.

connector_workflow.output: scene3_anim.mp4 — animated Scene 3 (÷2 call-out, coefficient cancels, x resolves), 1080p, ~14s
connector_workflow.note: Motion build for Scene 3 from filled design (8). Same animation grammar as 10/11. Consumed in step 14.

connector_workflow.output: scene4_anim.mp4 — animated Scene 4 (substitute, checkmark beat, x=4 emphasized), 1080p, ~12s
connector_workflow.note: Motion build for the check-answer Scene 4 from filled design (9). Closes the lesson. Consumed in step 14.

connector_workflow.output: animation_montage.mp4 — the four animated scenes plus the live-action boy clip woven into a single ~95s engagement-paced cut
connector_workflow.note: Assemble the four scene clips (10–13) and the live-action boy URN (from step 3) into one interwoven cut, satisfying criterion #2 (live-action + animation interwoven, no abrupt style breaks). The agent supplies a prompt describing the math-explainer pacing. Output is the picture-locked video that the VO is laid against; feeds steps 16 and 17.

connector_workflow.output: vo_clean.wav — denoised, leveled, broadcast-clean version of the raw GCC-accent Arabic narration
connector_workflow.note: VO-polish spine step: clean/denoise/level the raw Arabic VO (vo_raw_urn from step 3) so the GCC accent reads clearly (criterion #1). Crucially the enhancement must NOT alter pronunciation/accent. Output is the final narration track, muxed in step 18.

connector_workflow.output: master_1080p_169.mp4 — the montage conformed to an exact 1920×1080 16:9 frame, subject-aware, length preserved
connector_workflow.note: Conform the assembled montage (14) to the exact delivery frame (1920×1080) without changing length, locking the picture spec before audio mux. Feeds the final mux in step 18.

connector_workflow.output: subtitles_ar.srt — RTL Arabic SRT timed to the VO, rendered from the step-1 transcript and the step-15 VO timing
connector_workflow.note: Generate the separate Arabic SRT deliverable: take the transcript/step text from step 1 and time-align it to the cleaned VO (15). Rendered deterministically as a timed text artifact. This is a required separate deliverable and is also sidecar-muxed in step 18.

connector_workflow.output: FINAL_arabic_algebra_explainer_2min.mp4 (+ sidecar .srt) — 1080p H.264, exactly 2:00 ±5s, VO muxed, SRT attached
connector_workflow.note: Minimal unavoidable glue: ffmpeg mux of the locked picture (16) + cleaned VO (15) + SRT (17) into the final 2:00 master, trimming/padding the cut to land inside 2:00 ±5s (exact-duration criterion). No creative work here — pure container assembly of three connector-produced artifacts.

spec.difficulty_rationale: Long horizon: 18 ordered calls across two tool groups, with the whole build pivoting on a single derived artifact (the step-1 lecture summary) that the agent must keep coherent across the template search, four separate scene fills, and the SRT. State tracking is heavy: one template_id and one font choice must be reused across four fill_text calls, four filled designs map one-to-one onto four animate_design calls, and four URNs from a two-step upload must be threaded to the right downstream consumers (boy_urn→montage, vo_raw_urn→enhance_speech) — mis-binding any of them silently breaks the chain. Many assets are reused across multiple steps (the reference lecture spans steps 1–3; the raw VO spans 2,3,15; the boy clip spans 2,3,14), so the agent cannot treat steps as independent. Exact-spec pressure is high and adversarially graded: runtime must land in 2:00 ±5s, frame must be exactly 1920×1080, the GCC accent must survive enhancement (an easy place to over-process), live-action and animation must interweave with NO style break, and the on-screen math must be correct AND self-checking. The agent also has to make genuine content judgments (decompose the solve into four beats, write correct RTL Arabic equation copy, time the SRT) rather than just calling tools — and a wrong number on screen or a flattened accent fails acceptance even if every tool call 'succeeds'.
spec.full_brief:
Context: A client is commissioning a concise, two-minute educational video that teaches linear (one-variable) equations in clear, age-appropriate Arabic delivered with a genuine GCC (Gulf) accent. The piece is explicitly a MIXED format: short live-action segments featuring a boy aged 12–15, blended seamlessly with animated graphics that illustrate the algebra steps. The client will supply curriculum references; the freelancer handles scriptwriting, voice-over, casting/stock for the boy, animation, editing, and final mastering. The visual style must stay vibrant and engaging without distracting from the math — dynamic call-outs and simple motion graphics that walk a viewer through solving one basic linear equation and checking the answer.

The exact ask: deliver a finished explainer built on an Adobe Express animated lesson-frame template. Use the supplied reference lecture to derive the precise solving sequence (problem → isolate the variable → divide by the coefficient → state x → substitute and check). Produce four animated scenes (one per solve beat) carrying Arabic equation/step copy, interweave them with the live-action boy footage into a single cut, lay the polished Arabic narration over the picture, and master to spec. The narration's GCC accent must be preserved through cleanup (no accent-altering processing), and the math shown on screen must be correct and verifiable.

Constraints / deliverables: (1) Final 1080p MP4 (1920×1080, H.264), runtime exactly 2:00 ±5 sec. (2) A SEPARATE Arabic SRT subtitle file (RTL, timed to the VO). (3) Editable source / scene artifacts for future edits (the filled Express designs and the per-scene animation clips). Acceptance criteria, all enforced: spoken Arabic matches a modern GCC accent throughout; live-action and animation are interwoven with NO abrupt cuts or style inconsistencies (style breaks are a reject); and by the end a learner can solve a one-variable linear equation AND check the answer.
spec.grounding_note: REAL (verbatim from the original Freelancer.com brief in db_all.json, fulldesc): a concise two-minute educational video teaching linear equations in age-appropriate Arabic with a genuine GCC accent; mixed format of short live-action segments featuring a boy aged 12–15 blended with animated graphics illustrating the algebra steps; client supplies curriculum references, freelancer handles scriptwriting, voice-over, casting/stock for the boy, animation, editing, mastering; vibrant-but-not-distracting style with dynamic call-outs and simple motion graphics; deliverables = final 1080p MP4 exactly 2:00 ±5s, separate Arabic SRT subtitles, and editable project source files; acceptance criteria = (1) spoken Arabic matches a modern GCC accent throughout, (2) live-action and animation interwoven with abrupt cuts/style inconsistencies rejected, (3) by the end a learner can solve a one-variable linear equation and check the answer. ENRICHED (added to make it an honest ≥12-call connector workflow, faithful to what the brief implies): the specific worked example (2x+3=11 → x=4) and the five-beat solve decomposition are invented illustrative content consistent with 'a basic linear equation' and the 'solve and check' criterion; binding the build to an Adobe Express animated lesson-frame template (search_design→fill_text→animate_design) is the connector-feasible realization of the brief's 'animated graphics / motion graphics' ask, not a client-named tool (the client only named After Effects/Premiere/Animate); using media_summarize on the supplied reference lecture to structure the script operationalizes the client's 'I will supply any curriculum references you need'; the four-scene split, specific scene durations, the Arabic font-recommend step, the document_render_layout SRT render, and the final ffmpeg mux are workflow enrichments. The mixed live-action+animation requirement, GCC accent, exact 2:00 runtime, separate SRT, and editable-source deliverables are all REAL.

inputs.name: reference_lecture_clip
inputs.gen_prompt: A real Gulf-Arab math teacher (man, late 30s, neatly trimmed beard, wearing a plain light-grey kandura or a simple buttoned shirt) standing at a whiteboard in a modest school classroom, explaining how to solve a one-variable linear equation in spoken Modern Standard Arabic with a genuine GCC/Khaleeji accent. He writes '2x + 3 = 11' on the whiteboard with a marker and talks through isolating x, dividing, and checking the answer. Documentary classroom realism: natural fluorescent-plus-window mixed lighting, slight whiteboard glare, real marker squeak, a few students' heads visible out of focus in the foreground, authentic skin texture with visible pores and stubble, natural hand movement holding the marker with a correct five-fingered grip, no glossy CGI sheen. Native 1080p, native audio with the teacher's real voice and faint room tone. 35–45 seconds.
inputs.realism_notes: Used only as a STRUCTURE/script reference (fed to media_summarize) — never appears in the final cut, so its audio just needs intelligible Khaleeji Arabic and a correct on-board solve. Demand authentic classroom acoustics and a real teacher cadence, not a polished studio read; whiteboard handwriting must show the actual equation so the summary can recover the solve beats.

inputs.name: arabic_vo_raw
inputs.gen_prompt: A warm, friendly young-adult MALE narrator reading an Arabic explainer script for a kids' math video, in a modern GCC (Gulf/Khaleeji) accent — encouraging teacher-to-student tone, clear diction, moderate pace suitable for 12–15 year olds. Script walks through: the problem (٢س + ٣ = ١١), subtract 3 from both sides, divide both sides by 2, x = 4, then substitute back to check (٢×٤ + ٣ = ١١). 48 kHz, clean studio voice. Render as a slightly imperfect field recording: keep faint room ambience and one or two natural breaths so it reads as a real take that still needs leveling/denoise cleanup — but do NOT distort or alter the accent or pronunciation.
inputs.realism_notes: This is the narration SPINE. It is intentionally a clean-but-unpolished take (light room tone, natural breaths) so that media_enhance_speech has a real cleanup job to do in step 15 without the task becoming literal audio-repair. The GCC accent and Arabic pronunciation must be preserved end-to-end — accent integrity is acceptance criterion #1. ~95–110 seconds of speech to fit the 2:00 master.

inputs.name: liveaction_boy_clip
inputs.gen_prompt: Candid live-action footage of a real Gulf-Arab boy aged 12–15 sitting at a tidy home desk, a notebook and pencil in front of him, working through a math problem and reacting with a small 'aha' smile when he gets it. Several short usable beats: looking at the page thinking, writing, nodding, glancing up to camera. Documentary realism — natural window daylight, authentic child skin texture with fine flyaway hairs, real individual hair strands, correct hands holding the pencil with five fingers, natural blink and micro-expressions, slightly handheld framing. No CGI gloss, no uncanny smoothing. Native 1080p, native ambient audio (pencil on paper, faint room tone). 18–25 seconds of varied B-roll.
inputs.realism_notes: This is the live-action half of the brief's mandatory mixed format. It must intercut with the animated scenes without a style break (acceptance criterion #2), so the grade and framing should feel like everyday classroom/home footage, not a stylized ad. Reused both as the upload URN and as a cut element in the montage.

inputs.name: mathframe_bg
inputs.gen_prompt: A clean, vibrant but uncluttered background plate for a children's math explainer video, 1920×1080, designed for RTL Arabic on-screen text. Soft pastel gradient (teal-to-cream) with faint, subtle floating math glyphs (plus, minus, equals, a few Arabic-Indic numerals ٠١٢٣) at low opacity in the corners, leaving a large clear central zone for an equation and a clear right-hand lane for Arabic call-outs. Flat modern vector look, friendly, high legibility, no English text, no logos.
inputs.realism_notes: Provides a consistent backing plate so the four animated scenes share one visual identity (supports the 'no style inconsistency' criterion). Text-bearing/Arabic-numeral art → gemini-3-pro-image. Large empty central + right lane is deliberate so fill_text copy and call-outs never collide with decoration.

spec.iterative_chaining_note: The chain is genuinely output→input at every hop. Step 1 (media_summarize) converts the reference lecture into a structured solve sequence; that single JSON is the source of truth flowing into the template search (4), all four scene fills (6,7,8,9 — each pulls a different solve beat), and the SRT text (17). Steps 2→3 turn the four supplied assets into CC URNs that later media steps must reference: boy_urn flows into the montage (14), vo_raw_urn into media_enhance_speech (15). The Express sub-chain is strictly serial: search_design (4) yields a template_id reused by every fill_text; font_recommend (5) yields a font reused by every fill_text; each fill_text output (6/7/8/9) is the sole input to its matching animate_design (10/11/12/13). The four animation clips plus the live-action URN converge into video_create_quick_cut (14) → one montage, the sole input to video_resize (16). media_enhance_speech (15) cleans the raw VO. Finally the locked picture (16), cleaned VO (15), and rendered SRT (17) all feed the single local ffmpeg mux (18) → the 2:00 master. Nothing is invented mid-chain; every consumed item is the literal output of an earlier numbered step or a named input.
spec.one_line_ask: Deliver a 2:00 (±5s) 1080p Arabic algebra explainer that interweaves live-action footage of a 12–15-year-old boy with four animated Express lesson-frame scenes solving and checking 2x+3=11, narrated in a clean modern-GCC-accent Arabic VO, plus a separate RTL Arabic SRT and the editable scene/source artifacts.

outputs.name: FINAL_arabic_algebra_explainer_2min.mp4
outputs.spec: 1920×1080, H.264 MP4, ~24–30 fps, runtime EXACTLY 2:00 ±5s, cleaned GCC-accent Arabic VO muxed; live-action boy footage interwoven with 4 animated solve scenes.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: runtime lands in 1:55–2:05; resolution is exactly 1920×1080; VO is intelligible modern-GCC Arabic with accent preserved (not flattened/robotic); live-action and animation interweave with no abrupt style break; the on-screen math correctly solves AND checks 2x+3=11 → x=4.

outputs.name: subtitles_ar.srt
outputs.spec: Separate sidecar .srt, UTF-8, RTL Arabic text, well-formed cue timing aligned to the VO, covers the full 2:00.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: file is valid SRT, Arabic renders RTL correctly, cue timings track the spoken narration (no drift > ~0.5s), and the subtitle text matches the spoken solve steps including the check beat.

outputs.name: scene1-4_anim.mp4 (four per-scene animation clips)
outputs.spec: Four 1080p MP4 clips, one per solve beat (problem / isolate / divide / substitute-and-check), each ~12–14s, consistent animation grammar and shared background identity.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: four distinct clips exist, each shows the correct beat with correct Arabic equation copy in RTL, motion is consistent across all four (same easing/style), and together they form a coherent pedagogical progression.

outputs.name: scene1-4_filled (editable Express designs)
outputs.spec: The four filled Express lesson-frame designs (text-only fills on one shared template), retained as the editable source artifacts for future edits.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: all four are the SAME base template with only text varied; Arabic copy per scene is correct and editable; font is consistent; this satisfies the brief's 'editable project source files' deliverable.

spec.title: 2-Minute Arabic Algebra Explainer

issues: STEP 17 — TOOL CANNOT PRODUCE THE DELIVERABLE: document_render_layout is described in the sheet (A2) as 'render a JSON layout spec -> laid-out page (text+images, columns)' — an InDesign/print-class PAGE renderer. It has no audio input and no timecode capability, so it cannot emit a time-aligned RTL .srt sidecar. The spec even claims the SRT is 'time-aligned to the cleaned VO (15)' and lists inputs_from ['step 1','step 15'], i.e. feeding a VO AUDIO artifact into a page-layout renderer to get TIMED subtitle cues. That is not a real capability of this tool. The Arabic SRT is a REQUIRED separate deliverable (and the rubric checks cue timing drift <0.5s), so this defect makes a graded deliverable infeasible as specced.

STEP 14 — WRONG TOOL FOR AN ORDER-CRITICAL EDIT: video_create_quick_cut is flagged in the sheet (A5) as 'AI highlight/sizzle reel from clips (visual-engagement metrics; NOT speech-aware)'. It is a non-deterministic AI highlight SELECTOR that picks 'best parts' and trims to a target duration; it does not produce a controlled, ordered weave of four pedagogical scenes (problem->isolate->divide->check) interwoven with the boy B-roll in a specific narrative sequence. Acceptance criterion #2 (interweave, no style break) and the rubric's 'coherent pedagogical progression' depend on exact ordering that Quick Cut does not guarantee — it may drop scenes or reorder by engagement. Misuse of a sizzle-reel tool as a deterministic sequencer/editor.

RUNTIME MATH IS INTERNALLY INCONSISTENT: scene durations sum to ~52s (12+14+14+12); step 14 targets a '~95s' montage; the VO input is specced at '~95-110s'; the final master must be EXACTLY 2:00 (120s) +/-5s. A ~95s montage cannot be padded to 120s without ~25s of freeze/black, and the VO tops out near 110s. The spec hand-waves 'trimming/padding the cut to land inside 2:00 +/-5s' at the ffmpeg step (18), but the numbers do not converge on a clean 2:00 master, and padding picture/audio to hit the exact-duration acceptance criterion would degrade quality.

BORDERLINE (not a fail) — arabic_vo_raw gen_prompt asks to 'Render as a slightly imperfect field recording: keep faint room ambience and one or two natural breaths', i.e. a lightly down-roughened take. Sheet group D says 'do NOT down-roughen unless the TASK is literally audio-repair.' This is defensible here because media_enhance_speech (step 15) is a genuine cleanup step, but it sits right on the edge of the rule and should be kept minimal so accent-preservation acceptance (criterion #1) isn't put at risk by over-processing.
fixes: Replace document_render_layout in step 17. The connector sheet has no tool that emits a timecode-aligned .srt. Either (a) move SRT generation into the step-18 'local' glue (derive cue timing from the cleaned VO + the step-1 transcript via ffmpeg/whisper-align locally, which is honest container/post work), or (b) drop document_render_layout from step 17 entirely and have media_summarize (step 1) emit transcript+rough timings that the local step turns into the SRT. Update step 17's note/inputs so no connector tool is claimed to 'time-align to audio'. Adjust tool_call_count/tools_used accordingly.

Re-spec step 14. video_create_quick_cut is not a deterministic editor; for an order-locked 4-scene + B-roll weave, do the assembly in the local/ffmpeg step (concat/interleave the four animate_design clips with the boy URN in a fixed order). If you want to keep a connector call here, restrict Quick Cut to only generating short B-roll trims from the boy clip and explicitly state the deterministic ordered concat happens in step 18 — do not rely on Quick Cut for the pedagogical sequence. Reword the note to stop claiming Quick Cut produces a coherent ordered progression.

Reconcile the duration budget: make scene durations + montage length + VO length actually sum toward 120s. E.g. raise the four scenes to ~20-25s each (or add explicit live-action interstitials) so the picture lands near 115-120s before mux, and spec the VO at ~110-118s. State the exact target so step 18 only needs a few seconds of trim, not 25s of pad.

Keep arabic_vo_raw cleanup minimal: trim the gen_prompt's 'imperfect field recording / room ambience' language to at most 'one natural breath, otherwise clean 48kHz studio capture' so the media_enhance_speech step has a light, accent-safe job and criterion #1 (accent preserved, not flattened) is not jeopardized.

connector_workflow.output: CC storage URNs for all 7 supplied input assets (urn:box_master, urn:open_box, urn:reveal, urn:flatlay, urn:visionref, urn:logos, urn:palette)
connector_workflow.note: Ingest every provided asset into CC storage first so later generative + board tools can reference them by URN. asset_add_file wraps initialize/get_presigned_urls/finalize; one call per file, batched here. Establishes the asset state the whole chain tracks.

connector_workflow.note: Consumes urn:box_master from step 1. Neutralizes the supplied render's slightly warm cast so all downstream closed-box derivatives share one tonal baseline (the brief demands a consistent, impeccable luxury look across all 3 closed images).

connector_workflow.output: mask:lid_seal on urn:box_master_graded — selection of the blank lid seal medallion

connector_workflow.output: urn:box_closed_clean — closed box with a flush, matte, foil-free seal (CLOSED HERO BASE)

connector_workflow.output: urn:hero_wide_3000 — 3000x1250 wide hero outpaint of the cleaned closed box on a seamless studio sweep

spec.difficulty_rationale: Long horizon (20 connector calls) across photo-raster, layout/print, stock/storage, and video groups, with two parallel image lineages (closed-box vs open-box) the agent must keep distinct without cross-contaminating edits. State tracking is heavy: the cleaned closed base (step 5) is reused in 4 later steps, the open base (step 10) in 4, and the board (step 14) converges FIVE separate upstream outputs — picking the wrong intermediate URN silently corrupts the deliverable. Both generative fills (steps 5, 10) require correct mask-then-fill ordering and must honor hard brand negatives (no embossing/foil, uncluttered interior) that an over-eager agent will violate. Exact-spec pressure is high: 3000x1250 hero, 2400x2400 unboxing, A4/300dpi PDF, 1080x1920/12s vertical, plus needing exactly 3 closed + 3 open hyperrealistic frames per the real brief. The two-phase structure (concept board approval gate before finals) tests whether the agent sequences a presentation artifact before committing to print/video finals rather than rushing to outputs. Pure raster retouch of a soft-touch matte surface is genuinely partial — the agent must do the repeatable heavy lifting and not hallucinate a finish the client banned.
spec.full_brief:
A new multi-brand beauty subscription service is launching and needs the physical packaging for its flagship box — the box that ships in every delivery and showcases several partner brands at once. The client's North Star is Gucci: clean, confident, impeccably finished, luxurious without trying too hard. They want a refined premium exterior with beautiful proportions and an impeccable finish; elevated soft-touch modern materials (and they explicitly dislike embossing and hot-foil stamping — lean away from that look); a thoughtful interior structure whose inserts and dividers hold each brand's product securely and present them with room to breathe rather than crowded; considered details (a satisfying closure, a ribbon pull, a branded seal, a thoughtful card); and a premium, satisfying opening reveal. Restraint over ornamentation throughout. The client supplies their vision/reference imagery and expects the designer to elevate that direction into something original and distinctly their own.

The engagement is a collaborative two-phase process. Phase 1 (Concepts): at least three distinct design directions presented as concept boards/mockups, each a different take on structure, materials, finishes, interior arrangement, closure and opening, with a short walkthrough of the thinking. The client reviews, gives feedback, and approves one direction. Phase 2 (Final renders): once aligned, produce finished hyperrealistic imagery that reads as real product photography (not flat mockups) — exactly 3 hyperrealistic images of the box CLOSED and exactly 3 hyperrealistic images of the box OPEN showing the interior structure and how the multi-brand products are presented inside.

For this long-horizon engagement you are given a base hyperrealistic box render (closed), a base interior render (open), a short horizontal reveal clip, a multi-brand product flat-lay, the client's vision reference board, the partner-brand logos, and a brand palette spec. From these you must: clean the supplied renders to honor the brand rules (remove the banned stamped/foil seal, declutter and re-cradle the interior insert), extend them into a wide closed-box hero and a lifestyle unboxing scene via generative expand, derive a structural dieline, assemble a single Firefly concept board for the Phase-1 review gate, then produce the Phase-2 print deliverables (an A4/300dpi structure-and-finish PDF sheet and a 4:5 social teaser poster) and a vertical subscription teaser video reframed and cut from the supplied reveal clip. Hard constraints: no embossing or hot-foil look anywhere; soft-touch matte finish; uncluttered, breathable interior; consistent box color across every closed-box derivative; deliver exactly 3 closed + 3 open hyperrealistic frames; wide hero at 3000x1250, lifestyle unboxing at 2400x2400, poster at 2400x3000 (4:5), print sheet A4 at 300dpi, teaser at 1080x1920 vertical.
spec.grounding_note: REAL (verbatim from the Upwork brief in db_all.json / flagship_candidates.json): multi-brand beauty subscription box; Gucci as the gold standard (clean, confident, impeccably finished, luxurious without trying too hard); explicit dislike of embossing and hot-foil stamping; refined premium exterior with beautiful proportions and impeccable finish; elevated soft-touch modern materials; thoughtful interior structure with inserts/dividers that hold each brand's product securely with 'room to breathe rather than crowded'; considered details (satisfying closure, ribbon pull, branded seal, thoughtful card); a premium/satisfying opening reveal; restraint over ornamentation; client provides vision + reference imagery, designer elevates it into something original; explicit two-phase process — Phase 1 = at least three distinct concept directions as storyboards/mockups/concept boards with a walkthrough, Phase 2 = finished hyperrealistic imagery reading as real product photography, specifically 3 closed images and 3 open images; client's listed tools include Premiere Pro, InDesign, Illustrator; budget Fixed price ~$125 Intermediate. ENRICHED (implied, added for a long-horizon connector workflow): the specific supplied input asset set (base closed render, base open render, reveal clip, product flat-lay, vision-reference board, brand logos, palette JSON); the exact output dimensions (3000x1250 hero, 2400x2400 unboxing, 2400x3000 poster, A4/300dpi PDF, 1080x1920 teaser); the social-teaser poster and the vertical subscription teaser VIDEO (the brief implies launch/reveal motion via the Premiere Pro tool listing and the 'satisfying reveal' language but does not itemize a social video deliverable); the Firefly Board as the concrete Phase-1 concept-board vehicle; and the dieline/structure print sheet (the brief asks for structure/materials thinking and lists InDesign/Illustrator, so a structural sheet is a faithful extension). No brand names beyond Gucci-as-reference were invented as real client identity.

inputs.name: box_render_master
inputs.gen_prompt: Hyperrealistic studio product photograph of a closed luxury beauty subscription box, square form, soft-touch matte cool-champagne cardstock with a faint warm cast, clean minimal proportions in the spirit of restrained luxury fashion packaging, a slim satin ribbon pull at one edge and a small circular seal medallion on the lid that currently shows a subtle stamped/embossed monogram (to be removed later), photographed on a seamless light-grey studio sweep with one large soft key light camera-left and a gentle fill, true product photography with realistic soft-touch surface microtexture, fine paper grain, a soft contact shadow, no people, shot on an 85mm lens at f8, 1:1 framing, slightly imperfect real-world lighting not CGI-glossy
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like a real catalogue product shot: visible soft-touch matte microtexture and paper grain, a realistic soft contact shadow, no plastic CGI sheen. The seal deliberately carries a faint stamped look so the workflow can demonstrate removing the banned embossing/foil. 2048x2048.

inputs.name: open_box_render
inputs.gen_prompt: Hyperrealistic top-down-ish 3/4 studio photograph of the SAME luxury beauty subscription box now open, lid lifted, revealing a cluttered grey foam insert that cradles several multi-brand beauty products (a serum dropper bottle, a compact, a lipstick, a small jar) packed a little too tightly, soft-touch matte cool-champagne walls, slim satin ribbon, deep interior shadows, photographed on a light-grey studio sweep with one soft key light, realistic product photography with fine material microtexture and accurate product reflections, no people, no text labels readable, 85mm lens, square framing, candid real-world lighting not CGI
inputs.realism_notes: Interior deliberately a touch crowded with a generic foam insert so the workflow can declutter and re-cradle it into a breathable velvet divider. Products must look like real cosmetics with believable glass/metal reflections, not floating CGI props. 2048x2048.

inputs.name: reveal_clip
inputs.gen_prompt: Hyperrealistic 12-second horizontal product film, 1080p, native audio: a closed luxury beauty subscription box sits on a marble vanity in soft window light; two hands with neutral natural manicure (realistic skin, visible knuckle creases and fine hair, correct five fingers) gently pull the satin ribbon and lift the lid in one smooth motion, revealing the multi-brand products nestled inside; slow push-in, shallow depth of field, soft ambient room tone and a faint paper/ribbon rustle as native audio, candid documentary product-film feel, no on-screen text, no fast cuts
inputs.realism_notes: Hands must be anatomically correct with real skin pores, fine hair and natural manicure — the classic failure mode. 1080p native audio, 16:9, ~12s. Horizontal on purpose so video_resize has real reframing work to do. NOT veo-fast, NOT 720p.

inputs.name: products_flatlay
inputs.gen_prompt: Hyperrealistic overhead flat-lay product photograph of a curated set of multi-brand beauty products on a neutral linen surface — a frosted-glass serum dropper bottle, a brushed-metal compact, a matte lipstick bullet, a small ceramic cream jar, a folded thank-you card — arranged with generous negative space and elegant restraint, soft daylight, realistic glass/metal/ceramic reflections and tiny surface dust, true product photography, no readable brand text, no people, 50mm lens, square
inputs.realism_notes: Used to guide how the open-box insert should re-present the products (breathable, organized). Real reflective materials with micro-imperfections, no CGI gloss. 2048x2048.

inputs.name: vision_board_ref
inputs.gen_prompt: A clean luxury brand mood/vision reference board image collage: restrained fashion-house packaging cues, soft-touch matte textures, champagne and warm-greige tones, a slim satin ribbon detail, elegant minimal serif wordmark samples, a marble surface, all arranged as a tasteful 3x3 reference grid with thin gutters and small handwritten-style notes like 'soft-touch, no foil', 'room to breathe', evoking quiet luxury
inputs.realism_notes: Acts as the client-supplied direction and as a board/typography reference. Text in the collage should be crisp and legible (Gemini handles in-image text); intentionally communicates the no-foil, breathable-interior rules. 1536x1536.

inputs.name: brand_logos_vector
inputs.gen_prompt: A clean set of four minimal monoline beauty sub-brand wordmark logos plus one parent subscription wordmark, each a simple elegant serif or grotesque lockup in solid charcoal on transparent/white, well-spaced on a grid, no gradients, crisp edges suitable for vectorizing, restrained luxury aesthetic, no photographic elements
inputs.realism_notes: Flat high-contrast monoline marks so they sit cleanly on the board and poster and could be vectorized; supplied as the partner-brand identities to place. Delivered as PNG to be treated as logo art / placed on board+poster+print sheet. 2048x1024.

inputs.name: brand_palette_data
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a compact JSON brand spec for a quiet-luxury beauty subscription box: a 'neutral' champagne hex, 3 supporting warm-greige/charcoal hex values with names, a 'temperature_target' note (cool-champagne neutral), allowed finishes ['soft-touch matte'] and banned finishes ['emboss','hot-foil','high-gloss'], and target output dimensions for hero (3000x1250), unboxing (2400x2400), poster (2400x3000), print_sheet ('A4 300dpi'), teaser ('1080x1920'). Valid minified JSON only.
inputs.realism_notes: Machine-readable constraint sheet that pins the grading-relevant numbers and the banned-finish rules so the agent has an unambiguous source of truth. Small JSON.

spec.iterative_chaining_note: The chain is genuinely output->input, not a flat fan-out. Closed-box lineage: step1 URN -> auto_tone(2) -> color_temp(3) -> select seal(4) -> generative FILL the seal(5, the clean closed base) -> generative EXPAND to wide hero(6) -> highlight finish(7). Open-box lineage runs in parallel from the same step-1 ingest: auto_tone(8) -> select insert(9) -> generative FILL the insert using the flat-lay as guidance(10, the clean open base) -> lift interior(11) -> generative EXPAND to lifestyle unboxing(12). step5's clean closed pixels are independently vectorized into the dieline(13). The Firefly Board(14) is the convergence node: it ingests the finished closed hero(7), the finished open interior(11), the lifestyle unboxing(12), the dieline(13), plus the original vision and logo URNs — five upstream outputs feeding one artifact, which IS the Phase-1 concept-board gate. Type recommendation(15) flows into both the poster layout(16, which also consumes the unboxing render 12 and the logo) and the print sheet(17, which consumes hero 7 + interior 11 + dieline 13). The print sheet(17) is then converted to the final PDF(20). On the motion side, the supplied horizontal reveal clip(step1 URN) is reframed to vertical 9:16(18) and that vertical output is the direct input to the quick-cut teaser(19). Every later step names the specific prior step/URN it consumes; choosing the wrong intermediate (e.g. expanding the pre-fill master instead of the cleaned step-5 base) would propagate a banned-finish error into the hero, board, and print sheet.
spec.one_line_ask: Take the supplied hyperrealistic box renders, reveal clip, product flat-lay, vision board, logos and palette, and deliver a Gucci-restraint multi-brand beauty subscription box program: cleaned/finish-corrected closed and open hero renders, a wide hero and a lifestyle unboxing via generative expand, a structural dieline, a single Firefly concept board for the Phase-1 review, an A4/300dpi structure PDF and a 4:5 social poster, and a 1080x1920 vertical subscription teaser cut from the reveal clip.

outputs.name: closed_box_hero_wide
outputs.spec: 3000x1250 PNG, sRGB, wide cinematic closed-box hero on seamless studio sweep, soft-touch matte finish, foil-free seal, soft contact shadow
outputs.rubric_hook: Does it read as real product photography (not a flat mockup)? Is the banned stamped/foil seal gone and replaced by a flush matte medallion? Is the outpainted studio background seamless and shadow-consistent with the original box? Is the box color identical to the open-box derivatives? Exactly 3000x1250?

outputs.name: closed_box_frame_set
outputs.spec: 3x PNG closed-box hyperrealistic frames (the cleaned base, the wide hero, plus one tighter crop) at delivery resolution
outputs.rubric_hook: Are there exactly 3 distinct closed-box hyperrealistic frames as the brief requires? Do all three share one consistent color/finish and honor the no-emboss/no-foil rule?

outputs.name: open_box_interior_set
outputs.spec: 3x PNG open-box hyperrealistic frames (cleaned interior, lit interior, lifestyle unboxing 2400x2400) showing multi-brand products
outputs.rubric_hook: Exactly 3 open-box frames? Is the interior decluttered and breathable (not the original crowded foam)? Are the multi-brand products legible and elegantly cradled? Do the products match the supplied flat-lay set? Is the unboxing frame 2400x2400?

outputs.name: concept_board
outputs.spec: Firefly Board deep-link assembling wide closed hero + open interior + lifestyle unboxing + dieline + vision/logo refs as the Phase-1 concept board
outputs.rubric_hook: Does ONE board present all the required concept elements (exterior, interior, lifestyle, structure/dieline, brand refs) coherently? Does it function as a credible Phase-1 review artifact with a clear point of view? Are the correct finished derivatives (not raw inputs) placed?

outputs.name: structure_sheet_pdf
outputs.spec: A4 portrait, 300dpi, print-ready PDF pairing the structural dieline with the closed hero and open interior under recommended brand type, with finish/material callouts
outputs.rubric_hook: Is it A4 at 300dpi and print-ready? Does the dieline correspond to the actual closed-box silhouette? Are closed + open renders and callouts laid out cleanly with restrained luxury type? No banned-finish callouts present?

outputs.name: teaser_poster_4x5
outputs.spec: 2400x3000 PNG (4:5) social teaser poster: lifestyle unboxing render + brand wordmark + short luxury tagline, recommended type
outputs.rubric_hook: Is it exactly 2400x3000 (4:5)? Does the typography match the recommended luxury pairing and the vision-board direction? Is the unboxing image the finished derivative, logo placed cleanly, layout restrained?

outputs.name: subscription_teaser_vertical
outputs.spec: 1080x1920 MP4, ~8s, vertical subscription teaser quick-cut from the reframed reveal clip, ending on the open box, native audio
outputs.rubric_hook: Is it exactly 1080x1920 and ~8s? Is the box kept centered/subject-safe through the reframe (no cropped-off lid)? Does the cut land on the open-box reveal? Is it derived from the supplied reveal clip (same footage), not a new render?

spec.title: Luxury Packaging Designer — Hyperrealistic Beauty Subscription Box

issues: FRAME-COUNT GAP (the real defect): the brief and the closed_box_frame_set output both demand EXACTLY 3 closed-box hyperrealistic frames described as 'the cleaned base, the wide hero, plus one tighter crop', but the 20-call workflow only produces 2 closed frames via connectors -- step 5 (urn:box_closed_clean) and step 7 (urn:hero_wide_final). No step generates the third 'tighter crop' closed frame. image_crop_and_resize / image_crop_to_bounds exist on the sheet (A1 Geometry) but are never invoked. The open branch correctly yields 3 (steps 10/11/12), so the asymmetry exposes the missing closed frame. The difficulty_rationale even brags about 'exactly 3 closed + 3 open' while the chain under-delivers the closed set.

MINOR firefly board input-type mismatch: step 14 feeds the step-13 dieline.svg (a vector) into create_firefly_board, whose sheet description (line 62) specifies 'image/video URNs'. Feeding a raw SVG is borderline; a Firefly Board likely needs a rasterized preview of the dieline rather than the vector itself.

COSMETIC: step 1 collapses 7 separate asset_add_file uploads into one workflow node (note says 'one call per file, batched'). Honest and does not inflate tool_call_count (20 named steps; real call total would be ~26), but the displayed n=1 understates the true call count for that node.
fixes: Add one connector step (e.g. image_crop_and_resize or image_crop_to_bounds) operating on the step-7 wide hero (or the step-5 clean base) to produce the third closed-box frame ('tighter crop') so the closed set genuinely contains 3 connector-generated hyperrealistic frames, matching the brief's 'exactly 3 closed' and the closed_box_frame_set spec. This bumps the chain to 21 calls / 17 unique tools, still consistent.

For step 14, either rasterize the dieline before the board (note that create_firefly_board ingests an image preview of dieline.svg, not the raw SVG), or state explicitly that the board accepts the vectorized asset's preview URN -- to align with the sheet's 'image/video URNs' wording.
Optional clarity: annotate step 1 as '7 asset_add_file calls' (or split into a sub-count) so the true per-file call count is explicit, while keeping tool_call_count=20 for named workflow nodes.

coverage.verdict: "PASS on the mandate. All 4 previously-unused must-hit tools (animate_design, create_firefly_board, fill_text, search_design) are now exercised, each in multiple tasks with redundancy, so none is a single-point-of-failure. All 9 secondary-coverage tools are also covered (image_fill_area×6, image_generative_expand×4, document_merge_data_layout×2, document_merge_data_vector×3, video_create_quick_cut×6, video_resize×7, media_enhance_speech×5, media_summarize×5, asset_license_and_download_stock×6). The set spans 8 of 9 functional groups; the only structural blind spot is the entire martech stack (group C: AEM/AJO/CJA), which is by-design excluded since none of these 12 briefs is campaign/publish/analytics-flavored. Real remaining gaps are confined to A1 effect/texture/secondary-tonal knobs, two geometry tools, and three A4 plumbing variants — none of which is a must-hit."
recommendations: No must-hit tool is missing, so no task needs to absorb one — the 4 mandated tools are all redundantly covered. Lock the current lists.

To close the largest real gap (A1 effects/texture, 6 tools unused), route a printmaking step into the catalog/packaging tasks: add image_apply_halftone to F2-06 (B2B industrial catalog — halftone/duotone is idiomatic for print catalogs) and image_add_grain + image_apply_lens_blur to F2-07 (Monreale perfume photography — film grain + depth-of-field blur fit a luxury still).
Add image_apply_glitch_effect to F2-01 (Schugle motion graphics — glitch transition is on-brand for engaging motion) to cover the last effects tool.

Cover secondary tonal knobs cheaply: add image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation + image_adjust_exposure to F2-12 (hyperrealistic beauty box) and image_apply_monochromatic_tint to F2-08 (elegant spiritual brand — single-tint mark). Add image_adjust_brightness_and_contrast / image_adjust_dark_portions to F2-09 (medical brochure imagery).
Cover geometry: add image_auto_straighten + image_crop_to_bounds to F2-02 or F2-06 (event photos / catalog product shots both plausibly need deskew+trim before layout).

Plumbing variants (asset_get_presigned_urls, asset_add_file_check_status, asset_preview_file) are low-value to force; only add if a task genuinely uploads many files and needs status polling — F2-04 or F2-05 (large asset-package tasks) are the natural homes if you want them. asset_migrate_guest_storage is niche; leave unused unless a guest→signed-in handoff is in scope.

Group C (AEM/AJO/CJA) is the one whole-group gap; if the benchmark intends to claim martech coverage, a 13th task (campaign brief → AEM publish → CJA runReport loop) is needed — none of the current 12 can absorb it without changing its nature.
